My injuries began within hours on the same day as my second dose of the Moderna vaccine in April 2021 and continue to this day, nearly 5 years later. I have no doubt the vaccine caused my injuries, not only because of that timing but also because I've been tested for COVID every time it seemed even remotely possible I'd contracted it, and I've never tested positive. Moreover, since the virus emerged in the U.S. and public health officials began recommending protective measures, I've taken such measures (including face masks, handwashing, and avoiding risky gatherings) to a greater degree than anyone else I know. As of 4.5 years after vaccination, I had no detectable antibodies to the SARS-CoV-2 nucleocapsid but high antibodies to the spike protein.

My vaccine injuries have been serious to the point of being life-altering. Before my second dose of the Moderna COVID-19 vaccine, I was in excellent health. My only chronic health condition was infrequent migraines. I was very physically active, I had a successful and promising professional career, I did lots of volunteer work, and I generally had a full, happy life. Almost all of that was taken away from me, not only to my detriment but also to the detriment of the people I served or could have served through my career and volunteerism. The most debilitating of my more than a dozen new health problems have been severe chronic fatigue, chronic pain, and cognitive and neurological issues affecting, among other things, my ability to speak, write, remember, and concentrate. Approximately 1 year after my COVID vaccination, I was newly diagnosed with an autoimmune condition (Sjogren's).

I promptly reported my vaccine injuries to health care professionals, the federal government (including through V-safe and VAERS), and Moderna. I pleaded for help, as I took an extended leave from work, struggled with the most basic tasks of daily living, and suffered tens of thousands of dollars in lost wages and medical bills. No meaningful help was provided. To the contrary, I experienced gaslighting from many medical professionals, who denied that these new vaccines could cause any injuries (though others said they were seeing many patients like me but were pressured not to acknowledge COVID vaccine injuries publicly), and I witnessed the same shameful denials from government officials and other prominent voices. I felt abandoned by the very government whose advice I followed in getting the vaccine. Authorities and others I ordinarily would trust unjustly behaved as though people like me who were injured by the COVID vaccines were liars or mentally ill. I felt silenced by these reactions, fearful that speaking out about what had happened to me beyond a narrow set of health care providers and trusted friends and family could damage my reputation and any future career prospects I might have. I also wanted to avoid having the conspiracy-theorizing anti-vaccine community coopt my story for its own purposes. I continue to be very careful in sharing what has happened to me. These are the reasons I'm submitting this comment anonymously.

It shouldn't have been this way. It didn't have to be this way. Because so many people with power and resources have been unwilling to acknowledge or investigate the truth--that new vaccines can harm some people even as they help many others--we have lost precious years of opportunities to study the injuries caused by mRNA-based COVID vaccines (such as the Yale LISTEN Study is doing) and to develop treatments for those who have been injured. Title: Comment from jenkins, robert
Comment:
Public Comment to the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP)
Date: March 5, 2026
Docket No.: CDC‑2026‑0199

Subject: Urgent Call for Immediate Suspension and Recall of COVID‑19 mRNA Products Pending Comprehensive Independent Review

Members of the Committee,
The continued authorization and distribution of the COVID‑19 mRNA products represent a profound breach of biomedical ethics, scientific integrity, and regulatory oversight. Mounting molecular and clinical evidence now demonstrates that these products pose risks incompatible with accepted standards of vaccine safety and public health protection.

I. Molecular and Biological Safety Concerns
1. DNA Contamination and Genomic Integration Risk
Independent analyses have documented the presence of residual DNA within the lipid nanoparticle formulations, including sequences containing the SV40 enhancer and promoter elements. The presence of these sequences introduces the potential for genomic incorporation and unintended, long‑term expression. This constitutes a serious manufacturing contamination event in violation of core quality‑control principles and existing FDA biologics standards.

2. Immune Tolerance and Class Switching
Observation of an IgG3 to IgG4 antibody class switch after injection indicates an atypical immune tolerance response. Rather than improving viral clearance, this pattern may dampen critical immune defenses and predispose recipients to reinfection or chronic viral persistence. The committee must formally assess whether continued boosting amplifies immune suppression or tolerance dysfunction.

3. Oncogenic Pathway Suppression
Experimental evidence demonstrates suppression of the p53 tumor‑suppressor pathway and disruption of DNA repair signaling following lipid nanoparticle exposure. Given the central role of p53 in preventing malignancy, any degree of sustained down‑regulation presents a plausible oncogenic risk that demands urgent investigation.

II. Regulatory and Ethical Misconduct

The development and Emergency Use Authorization of these products bypassed standard regulatory safeguards and long‑term toxicological testing routinely required for genetic biologics. The EUA framework was improperly leveraged to accelerate deployment without adequate biodistribution, mutagenesis, or reproductive safety assessments.

Simultaneously, the suppression of alternative therapeutics, censorship of clinicians, and retribution against dissenting physicians created conditions incompatible with the ethical principles enshrined in the Belmont Report—beneficence, respect for persons, and justice.

Mandates and coercion further violated the principle of informed consent, as defined in the Nuremberg Code and Declaration of Helsinki, by compelling individuals to submit under threat of economic or social exclusion.

III. Required Immediate Actions
Immediate Recall and Independent Lot Examination:
Suspend all distribution until a third‑party forensic analysis can examine manufacturing lots for DNA contamination, lipid nanoparticle degradation, and evidence of potential genomic integration.

Public Release of All Preclinical and Clinical Data:
Require full disclosure of genomic sequences, biodistribution findings, and internal safety evaluations currently withheld under proprietary exemptions.

Moratorium on Coercive Policies:

Title: Comment from DIRENNA, Rachael
Comment:
I am submitting this comment to document a serious gap in transparency, notification, and accountability when serious adverse events occur following vaccination.

When my son was three months old, he received the rotavirus vaccine Rotashield. Shortly afterward he developed intussusception, a life-threatening condition in which the intestine telescopes into itself. He required emergency surgery to save his life. Two months later he required a second emergency surgery due to severe adhesions from the first operation.

At the time, our family was never informed that intussusception had been associated with the vaccine. We were left believing this was an unexplained medical emergency.

Years later I learned that Rotashield had been withdrawn from the market after it was linked to an increased risk of intussusception. By the time I discovered this connection, my son was eighteen years old and the statute of limitations for filing under the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program had long expired. Our family was denied even the opportunity to have the case reviewed.

This experience exposes a serious structural problem. When automobile manufacturers discover that a defect can harm consumers, they are required to issue recalls and notify owners. Families deserve the same level of transparency when a medical product administered to infants is later linked to serious harm.

The current system also raises a deeper ethical concern. Medical interventions must be grounded in informed consent. Policies that effectively pressure or require people to accept medical products while shielding manufacturers from direct liability undermine public trust. Forced medicine is an abomination to the principles of informed consent and bodily autonomy that should guide ethical medical policy.

I urge the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to consider the following reforms:

• Establish clear mechanisms to notify families when significant vaccine safety signals emerge after a product is licensed or withdrawn.
• Reevaluate statute-of-limitations rules within the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program when adverse events are discovered years later.
• Strengthen transparency and communication with families when serious adverse events are identified.
• Ensure policies governing liability and compensation treat affected families fairly.

No parent should discover decades later that a life-threatening medical event affecting their infant may have been connected to a product they were never warned about, only to learn that the system offers no path for acknowledgment or review.

Sincerely,
Rachael DiRenna

Title: Comment from Miller, Amanda
Comment:
I am Amanda Miller, RN BSN CPN writing in regards to Docket CDC-2026-1099.
As a Certified Pediatric Nurse, my primary responsibility is the safety and well-being of children. Vaccination remains one of the most effective tools we have to protect pediatric patients from serious illness, hospitalization, disability, and death. In my clinical experience, routine immunizations are a critical component of preventive care and a cornerstone of modern public health.

Because children rely on adults, clinicians, and policymakers to safeguard their health, it is essential that access to recommended vaccines remains broad, equitable, and guided by strong scientific evidence. I strongly oppose efforts that would unnecessarily limit access to vaccines or weaken existing vaccine recommendations for the general public. Reducing access or narrowing recommendations risks increasing preventable disease, particularly among vulnerable children who depend on community immunity and timely immunization to stay healthy.

At the same time, maintaining public trust in vaccines requires transparency and robust safety monitoring. While vaccine injuries are rare, they can occur. Programs such as the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS) and the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program (NVICP) are vital components of the vaccine safety infrastructure. These systems allow clinicians and patients to report adverse events, support ongoing safety surveillance, and provide a pathway for individuals who believe they have experienced vaccine-related harm to seek review and compensation.

Protecting public health means holding two truths at once: vaccines save lives and are overwhelmingly safe, and the small number of individuals who experience adverse events deserve to be heard, studied, and supported. Strengthening reporting systems, improving clinician education on adverse event reporting, and ensuring fair access to compensation programs are essential to maintaining confidence in vaccination programs.

I encourage the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) to continue using transparent, evidence-based methodologies when evaluating vaccine data and making recommendations. Pediatric patients benefit most when vaccine guidance is clear, consistent, and grounded in rigorous scientific review.

Children deserve the strongest protection we can provide. Preserving broad access to recommended vaccines, maintaining rigorous safety monitoring, and supporting those who experience rare adverse outcomes together form the foundation of a trustworthy and effective immunization program.

Title: Comment from Davidson, Marty
Comment:
I am writing to make the committee aware of a recent survey:
Health Freedom Defense Fund and Brownstone Institute initiated such a poll to find out. This Feb. 26-27 poll, conducted by Zogby Strategies, has documented remarkable supermajorities in favor of medical and health freedom, with numbers on objective questions exceeding 80%.

Polled were 1,000 registered voters with 93.6% definitely or very likely to vote. The party breakdown is 37% Republican, 36% Democrat, 27% Independent. Party breakdown shows broad support. The margin of error for overall results is +/- 3.2 percentage points.
Strongest areas of agreement (broad majority support):

Right to refuse medical treatment generally: 87.9% agree (58.8% strongly).
Right to make one’s own medical choices as a basic human right protected by law: 87.2% agree (59.5% strongly).
Doctors should discuss vaccine concerns openly without fear of medical board backlash: 88.1% agree (64.5% strongly — one of the highest “strongly agree” levels in the survey).
Health insurance should cover chosen treatments, including holistic/alternative options: 76.1% agree (43.6% strongly).
Right to refuse vaccines for adults: 80.4% agree (50.5% strongly).
Personal medical/vaccine decisions should never lead to employment denial: 70.6% agree (47.3% strongly).
Parents’ right to refuse vaccines for children/dependents: 65.7% agree — still a clear majority, but softer than adult refusal (37.4% strongly agree vs. 50.5% for adults).
On matters of school vaccine mandates, results show majorities:

Parents should be able to opt children out of school vaccine mandates: 54.5% agree (31.0% strongly). Among parents with children under 17, the agreement was 66.7%, with 42.8% strongly agreeing. To put this staggering result in context, other polls in recent years have concluded that more than 70% of the public supports school vaccine mandates.
College students should not have been expelled for refusing the COVID-19 vaccine: 65.4% agree (44.4% strongly).
On matters related to the COVID-19 era, the poll documents a strong majority opposed measures in retrospect:

COVID-19 lockdowns/restrictions caused excessive damage to American society: 61.9% agree (35.0% strongly) vs. 32.0% disagree.
On other matters related to medical freedom:

Childhood vaccine schedule expansion likely contributed to the rise in chronic diseases (among other factors): 48.3% agree vs. 38.2% disagree + 13.6% undecided — essentially split, but less than a decade ago, a strong majority said the vaccine schedule is safe, as noted in a Pew Research poll.
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services’ decision to conduct additional vaccine safety research is justified: 68.6% agree vs. ~21% disagree + 10% undecided.
Investigating the effects of thimerosal (a mercury-based compound), aluminum, polysorbate-80, polyethylene glycol and formaldehyde used in everyday medical products; 77.8% support, 47.8% strongly. (This question concerns the ingredients in vaccines without mentioning the word vaccines, yielding even stronger support.)
Overall, the poll shows very strong support (80–88%) for adult medical autonomy, the right to refuse treatment/vaccines as adults, freedom of medical speech for doctors and protection from employment discrimination based on medical choices.

Majority support remains when the question involves children (school mandates 54-66%, parental refusal for dependents 66%). More noteworthy, however, these results illustrate erosion in public support for school vaccine mandates since 2019, as also seen in other surveys.

Retrospective judgment on COVID-19 policies leans toward viewing them as excessively damaging. Trust in figures like Dr. Anthony Fauci and associated public health directives remains low (around 28-35%), with majorities believing guidance prioritized other interests or enabled excessive restrictions.

There is also broad approval for more vaccine safety research. The results reflect an electorate (especially among likely 2028 voters) that is protective of individual medical decision-making rights.

Finally, the poll results demonstrate that Americans will vote for candidates who protect their rights and freedoms, insist on transparency, and hold experts, pharmaceutical, and chemical companies accountable for their actions.

The lessons of this poll are palpable. If people are asked straightforward questions that impact their own health and medical wishes, and those of their families, they clearly come down on the side of freedom, transparency, honesty, and choice. This should not be surprising because these are core American values on which people are more united than divided.

Title: Comment from Entzminger, Kim
Comment:
My name is Kimberly E. I am writing in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199.

I am a person living with vaccine injury. I urge the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to consider patient perspectives and real-world experiences in its deliberations. Our presence here reflects lived suffering, unmet needs, and the importance of inclusive dialogue. We do exist. Please consider how policies on vaccine recommendation guidelines, post-adverse event care, and research priorities affect real people every day. I am submitting this comment in support of the petition to add COVID-19 vaccines to the Vaccine Injury Table under the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program.

I received a COVID-19 vaccine in 2021 and experienced a severe and prolonged adverse reaction. Shortly after vaccination, I developed a widespread rash that spread across my body along with significant breathing difficulties. My condition required multiple hospitalizations. Over the course of several months, I was hospitalized three times. My allergist stated that, "God's not finished with" me as the only explanation he has as to why I'm still alive.

During my final hospitalization, I spent a week receiving intensive treatment that included nearly 400 mg of intravenous steroids, blood thinners, allergy medications, and breathing treatments every four hours. I was unable to work from April 2021 through August 2021 due to the severity of my symptoms. I came home on a medication regimen so complicated my husband had to create a spreadsheet to check off the medications.

Even after the acute phase, the long-term effects have continued to affect my health. I have required repeated courses of steroids, at higher doses and for longer than typically prescribed. These medications have resulted in additional complications, including the development of cataracts that now require surgical removal. I was also put on two different biologics to try and suppress my immune response, but both caused significant side effects so they were discontinued.

Since the vaccine, I have experienced neurological and physical symptoms including weakness in my legs, numbness in my hands and feet, brain fog, and difficulty remembering basic information, severe, random reactions that result in hives and breathing issues. Though these symptoms have improved some, they still negatively impact my life. I also developed numerous new allergies, and existing allergies became significantly more severe. I continue to experience breathing problems and must keep a nebulizer available both at home and at work, and epi-pens with me at all times.

These health challenges have had a profound impact on my life and career. I had planned to complete a 30-year career in education and then transition into higher education as a second career. Because of the ongoing health issues caused by this reaction, I now worry about whether I will even be able to reach 25 years of service next year.

I filed a claim with the Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program (CICP) and have been waiting for years for my case to be reviewed. My case only moved into medical review after I contacted the office of Senator Tim Scott for assistance. The current system is extremely slow and lacks transparency for individuals who have experienced serious adverse reactions.

Adding COVID-19 vaccines to the Vaccine Injury Table under the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program would provide a more transparent, accessible, and fair process for individuals who have experienced rare but serious vaccine injuries.

Supporting those who have been harmed by rare adverse reactions is an important part of maintaining public trust in vaccination programs. I strongly support the petition to add COVID-19 vaccines to the Vaccine Injury Table.

Thank you for considering my comment.

A close friend of mine experienced a situation where their child was born healthy and was already walking at around 12 months of age. After receiving a vaccination following a recent flu illness, the child reportedly experienced significant developmental regression, becoming non-verbal and losing the ability to walk. According to the doctors involved, administering the vaccine shortly after a flu illness may have contributed to the outcome.

Experiences like this have raised concerns for me about how informed consent is handled in medical offices. In my experience, many doctors spend significant time explaining the risks of not vaccinating but do not spend equal time explaining potential side effects or screening for recent illnesses and allergies before administering vaccines. Parents should receive balanced information about both the benefits and the possible risks so they can make informed decisions for their children.

Doctors should consistently ask detailed questions about recent illnesses, immune reactions, and allergies before administering vaccines. Clearer screening procedures and transparent communication could help reduce potential complications and increase trust between parents and healthcare providers.

Another concern relates to access to pediatric care in Texas. In my experience, almost all pediatric offices make it extremely difficult for families who delay or decline vaccines to access routine care for their children. Finding a pediatrician willing to see children who are not fully vaccinated can feel like searching for a needle in a haystack. In some cases, offices refuse to schedule well-child visits for children who are not sick if they are not fully vaccinated.

If medical offices are concerned about exposure risks, a reasonable solution could be designated appointment hours or separate scheduling blocks rather than denying preventive care altogether. Children should still have access to routine pediatric care, regardless of vaccination status.

Parents should be able to openly discuss vaccination decisions with their pediatricians and still maintain access to routine healthcare for their children. Policies should encourage transparent communication, stronger informed consent practices, and continued access to care for all children.

Thank you for considering these concerns when evaluating vaccination policies and healthcare access regulations.

Title: Comment from Anonymous
Comment:
Vaccines, like any medical intervention, are not entirely without risk. While serious adverse reactions appear to be rare, they do occur, and it is important that these events are studied carefully and transparently. One of the ongoing challenges in medicine is understanding why some individuals may experience significant reactions while many others do not. It is important that we hear the stories of these people who have been vaccine-injured and find a way to help them.

As we continue to collect long-term data, it will be important for researchers and healthcare professionals to monitor potential patterns related to cardiovascular, neurological, and immune health over time. Ongoing research and open scientific dialogue are essential to maintaining both safety and public trust.

I also know someone personally who lost their mother shortly after receiving her second Moderna dose. Within a few days, she became critically ill and passed away from a blood clot in the brain. Whether or not the vaccine directly caused the outcome, it was a tragic situation that left many questions for the family. Experiences like this remind us how important compassionate listening, transparency, and continued research are in medicine.

Rather than dismissing concerns outright, we should be willing to ask thoughtful questions and continue learning.

How can the medical community better identify why certain individuals may experience severe reactions to medical treatments while others do not?

What systems are currently in place to track long-term outcomes after new medical interventions, and how can those systems be improved?

How do we balance public health recommendations with individualized medical risk assessment?

What role does informed consent play when recommending treatments that are still being studied long-term?

How can healthcare providers maintain public trust while still acknowledging uncertainty in emerging science?

Questions to consider moving forward.

Title: Comment from OBrien, Christina
Comment:
As an immune-compromised individual, I am so thankful that vaccines are available, and that so many of my fellow citizens are willing to be vaccinated. I have been vaccinated regularly with Covid mRNA vaccines (Pfizer and Moderna), and have had no side effects, nor have I caught Covid.

Title: Comment from Haacke, Chris
Comment: Our adult son was diagnosed with Asperger's Syndrome at age 29. He was a normal toddler until about age 3. He received his childhood vaccines necessary for school. He sang before he talked. As he grew, he had difficulty with relationships and had difficulty in social environments. He was gifted in many areas but talked to himself and had problems with focus. After completing college, he struggled teaching music in the classroom because he couldn't relate to his students, and had no administrative support. He eventually returned home to work in a factory. He had legal troubles, and ended up behind bars. It was during this time that he had several psychological tests, when the Asperger's diagnosis was made. He also suffered from depression and social anxiety disorder. We fully believe that his Asperger's was caused by the childhood vaccines. Without proof, we have no leg to stand on. We hope there is some justice for our son at some point. We would like to believe that the vaccine schedule had much more to do with his situation, and he was not solely responsible for his actions.

Title: Comment from Sh, Simin
Comment:
My brother was diagnosed with T‑cell lymphoma just a few months after receiving the Moderna COVID shots in March 2021. He died only five months later, following a nightmarish ordeal of treatment at an overwhelmed hospital, leaving our family in deep grief.
(His case has been submitted to VAERS, CICP, and Moderna, and supporting documentation is available upon request.)

In the years following the COVID vaccination rollout—especially throughout 2022 and 2023—I witnessed an alarming surge in serious health crises among people I know who received these shots. These included sudden and aggressive cancers—lymphoma, throat cancer, and even liver cancer in a young person—along with heart attacks and aneurysms.

Alongside these tragedies came a marked increase in autoimmune and inflammatory disorders: POTS, eczema, celiac disease, visual disturbances, endocrine dysfunction, dysautonomia, severe allergic reactions, and exacerbations of inflammatory bowel diseases. Many of these conditions appeared suddenly in young, previously healthy individuals following vaccination—often after misleading information, coercion, or pressure from workplace and university mandates.

As a chemist, I independently reviewed the official CDC and FDA data in 2021. It quickly became apparent that, particularly for younger demographics, the risks of vaccination far outweighed any potential benefits. With the court‑ordered release of manufacturer trial data and the emergence of new variants, the picture became undeniable: no meaningful benefit—only harm. The vaccines disrupted immune function, increased susceptibility to infection, and failed to prevent transmission—an admission confirmed under oath by manufacturer executives in the European Parliament.

We now face a profound medical and ethical crisis—one defined not by a virus, but by widespread institutional denial. Those harmed continue to suffer while silence and deflection replace accountability.

The question before you is simple yet profound:
Will you continue hiding behind reassuring language like “shared decision‑making” and “protecting the vulnerable” when the immunocompromised are precisely those most at risk from these products?
Will institutions continue to impose these experimental injections on healthy individuals?
Will you allow these shots upon innocent children whose only misfortune is having parents misled by relentless propaganda?

Or will you finally confront the truth—acknowledge the injured and the bereaved, ban these harmful injections outright, remove them from the market, and launch a nationwide effort to treat the injured and educate those deceived by the false promise of “safe and effective”?

Only through such decisive action can this tragedy be addressed, healing begin, and integrity be restored to medicine and public health.

Thank you.

Title: Comment from Sherman, Elizabeth
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from MORIN, KIM
Comment:
In March of 2021 I was 59, healthy and fit. I took a J&J COVID shot. I had no initial bad reaction; I have never had a severe reaction to any vaccine at that point.
In Oct of 2021 I got a flu-shot from CVS; first non-military sourced shot I have received. 10 days later, I experienced SUDDEN ONSET, woke up one morning with, swollen inflamed hands. They progressively got worse and my feet also were swollen and inflamed. I was initially misdiagnosed with Gout, then Lyme Disease. I took the 30 day course of antibiotics for Lyme, which caused other health problems for me after my gut biome was destroyed. An infectious disease specialist later confirmed it was a misdiagnosis. I was then misdiagnosed with Arthritis.
In May of 2022 I got my first case of COVID. It lasted severely for about 3 weeks. About half-way through that battle, I noticed my hands and feet were greatly improved. By the end. my hands and feet were 98% better, with just some lingering pain in 2 fingers.
I believe the COVID vaccine altered my immune system in some manner that caused me to have an allergic response to the flu vaccine, and then when my immune system activated to fight COVID, it cleared the allergic reaction that caused my symptoms. Those symptoms were severe, sudden onset, debilitating and did not respond to any standard treatments for inflammation. They have not returned. I have not received, nor can I ever take again, any kind of vaccine because of this episode; I have no idea how my immune system will respond.

Title: Comment from BUNTON, DARREN
Comment:
My name is Darren J. Bunton, and I respectfully submit this comment for inclusion in the public record regarding the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices’ upcoming discussions on COVID-19 vaccine injuries and Long-COVID.

First, I want to acknowledge the importance of vaccination as a public health tool and the tremendous responsibility carried by the ACIP in guiding national immunization policy. Vaccines have historically prevented countless illnesses and deaths, and maintaining public trust in immunization programs is essential.

However, public trust also depends on transparency and the willingness to recognize and study adverse outcomes experienced by some individuals following vaccination.

I am among those who believe they experienced a significant health event following COVID-19 vaccination. In my case, the health changes that followed vaccination had substantial impacts on my physical well-being and daily functioning. Navigating the healthcare system to seek answers has often been difficult, as many patients reporting post-vaccination complications have encountered uncertainty, inconsistent recognition of symptoms, and limited research focused specifically on vaccine-associated injury.

Regardless of one’s position on vaccines, it is important to acknowledge that adverse events do occur in a small portion of the population. For those individuals and families affected, the consequences can be life-altering. These experiences deserve careful scientific investigation, compassionate clinical care, and policy responses that recognize both the benefits and risks associated with vaccination.

As ACIP evaluates COVID-19 vaccine injuries and Long-COVID, I respectfully urge the committee to consider the following:

1. Strengthening research into vaccine-associated adverse events, including mechanisms that may explain why some individuals experience prolonged or severe reactions.

2. Improving clinical recognition and treatment pathways for individuals experiencing persistent post-vaccination symptoms.

3. Enhancing transparency in safety monitoring systems, including clearer communication about how adverse event data are collected, evaluated, and acted upon.

4. Ensuring that individuals who experience serious adverse outcomes have access to meaningful medical support and compensation pathways.

A balanced and transparent approach to vaccine safety ultimately strengthens public confidence. Acknowledging and studying vaccine injuries does not undermine vaccination programs; rather, it reinforces scientific integrity and public trust.

I appreciate the opportunity to submit this comment and hope that the committee will continue to evaluate these issues with rigor, compassion, and openness to the experiences of patients.

Respectfully submitted,
Darren J. Bunton
Indianapolis, Indiana

Title: Comment from McManus, Victoria
Comment: As someone who lives with a cancer patient undergoing chemotherapy, I strongly support COVID vaccinations twice at year at no cost to all - no requirements for secondary conditions or insurance should be required. Only vaccinating SOME people is not nearly as effective as vaccinating all people in order to protect those who cannot be vaccinated or for whom vaccination is ineffective. Protecting the immune-compromised also protects everyone else from virus mutations that can occur when an immune-compromised person has an extended infection. Given that masking and other mitigations have sharply declined, vaccination is one of our few protections for our people and our economy. Likewise, lessening the possibility of infection lessens the chance of Long COVID, which can be extremely disabling and will affect our citizens and economy for a generation to come. Don't be foolish and short-sighted; vaccinate and fund improved vaccinations and treatments.

Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. Thank you for your attention.

Trust in medicine cannot exist without transparency, accountability, and respect for individual autonomy. None of these things has occurred for the mRNA Covid 19 shots! Until these standards are upheld, public confidence will continue to erode.

Title: Comment from Shtaba-Clark, Olga
Comment:
My daughter Alice was 18 months old when she received her MMR shot. She had no health problems before the shot and was achieving her developmental milestones at appropriate times. She was walking before she turned one year old, she started to talk early and was social and curious baby. Six days after she was vaccinated with MMR combination plus HipB she suddenly developed projectile vomiting, fever of 104 and became weak and lethargic. She became severely ill and lost the ability to sit, stand, walk and she was not talking anymore. The fever and lethargy subsided and I was able to get her in the car and bring to pediatrician. He reassured me that my daughter is fine and it’s normal to get some fever after vaccine.
Alice slowly recovered, gaining back most of abilities she lost during two weeks of her illness and recovery. She only was not saying words as before. She became quiet and only occasionally was making sounds instead of naming objects and her needs, as before. Title: Comment from Vemuri, Ravi
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Reinthal, Alex
Comment:
To the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices,

I am writing to urge the committee to make COVID-19 vaccine recommendations that are specific to vendors and platforms, and in particular to support expanding the Nuvaxovid indication to include pediatric use.

mRNA vaccines should not be the only option available to children. Families deserve the ability to choose from multiple vaccine platforms, and protein-based options like Novavax offer a meaningful alternative. Children need robust protection from both post-vaccination syndrome and Long COVID, and broadening access to different vaccine technologies is essential to achieving that.

Novavax's protein-based platform offers several distinct clinical advantages: a broadened antigenic range, greater durability of immune response, mucosal immunity, a favorable IgG1/IgG3 antibody profile, fewer reported side effects, and longer-lasting protection compared to mRNA vaccines. These benefits are particularly relevant for pediatric populations, where parental confidence in safety and tolerability directly affects uptake.

I also encourage the committee to support next-generation saponin-based adjuvants such as Novavax's Matrix-M. These adjuvants have demonstrated strong safety profiles and enable vaccines that require less antigen per dose — an important consideration for pediatric formulations.

Finally, I ask the committee to preserve a shared decision-making framework that includes both Novavax and mRNA vaccines, and to ensure that all COVID-19 vaccines remain available to every American with no out-of-pocket costs. Cost barriers disproportionately affect children in underserved communities, and equitable access must remain a priority.

Thank you for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Amiri, Aicha
Comment:
Dear Members of the ACIP,

I am writing to urge the committee to support expanding the Nuvaxovid indication to include pediatric use. Children currently have no alternative to mRNA COVID-19 vaccines. Parents deserve the freedom to choose between vaccine platforms for their children, and the evidence supports giving them that choice.

Novavax's protein-based platform offers clear advantages for pediatric populations: durable immune responses, broad antigenic coverage, a favorable IgG1/IgG3 antibody subclass profile, and strong mucosal immunity. Reported side effects are fewer and milder than mRNA alternatives — a factor that directly affects both parental confidence and whether families return for subsequent doses.

The Matrix-M saponin adjuvant further strengthens the pediatric case. It enables effective immune responses at lower antigen doses, which is exactly the kind of optimization we should be pursuing in children's formulations.

Children remain at risk for Long COVID, and families should not be locked into a single-platform approach when a safe, proven alternative exists. The committee should preserve shared decision-making between parents and providers and ensure that individual families — not a one-size-fits-all recommendation — determine what is best for their children.

Please recommend Nuvaxovid for pediatric use.

Thank you.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

Title: Comment from BOREN, JANE
Comment: My son, who was 40 years old at the time, shared an office with another employee who had taken the "vaccine."  He did not take the shot.  He developed severe Bell's Palsy which was treated with Prednisone, and it resolved in a few weeks.  A few weeks subsequent to that, he was swimming in a lake for about and hour when he developed unmistakable Pulmonary Edema  (PE).  I am a retired RN. My family and my husband were in Idaho to celebrate our 50th wedding anniversary.  My son had gone swimming right after we arrived at the venue. About an hour later, I was called to the dock which was about 60 feet down from the house.  When I evaluated him, I could clearly see that he had the PE but I was baffled at what may have caused it.  He had no peripheral edema, and, though I had read about "foaming at the mouth" with PE, I had never in my long career witnessed it.  I had oxygen in my car and had it brought to the dock.  I called 911 and requested and emergency transport by boat as we were so far downhill from the road and my son was a big man.  We did finally get a fire boat rescue with 5 or 6 emergency medical staff to help transfer him to the hospital.  In the ER, he was diagnosed with Swimming-Induced Pulmonary Edema (SIPE), a condition of which I had never heard.  The ER doctor stated that she had seen 3 cases of this in the past week.  My son was hospitalized for three days (our entire vacation).  He had no insurance so continues to owe on that hospital stay.  He took it easy as he recovered from the incident.  The hospital apparently discharged him with a diagnosis of pneumonia which he clearly did not have. He was in good health, afebrile, and completely normal before swimming.  His case is important as it is illustrative of shedding injuries.  The fact that the ER physician had seen three cases of this quite unusual condition in one week is suggestive of an unusual cause which I have concluded is likely related to the "vaccine" from direct administration or shedding.  I have no supportive data or files as my son is an adult.  I am sure that if you would like supportive data/files I can assist in procurement of them.

Title: Comment from Wallace, Anne
Comment:
Dear ACIP Committee.

First, Praise the Lord for you-all and for Bobby Kennedy.

Second, I've never been sold on flu shots or other so-called vaccines, but I was not an antivaxxer because I got the DPT for tetanus every 10 years or so throughout my adult life because of my profession. I've never had a flu shot and I haven't had the flu since I was 12, which was 58 years ago (not hyperbole). In addition, I'd been watching one scary germ after another roll out every year or two for the last two decades when covid hit, being utterly unafraid of any of them. So, my immediate response to covid was, "Oh brother, here we go again."

I'm a scientist so "I-AM-The-Science-Mr. Fauci" (if he's a doctor, he's a mad doctor) was an obvious pharma shill to me. I didn't get the covid shot, I didn't stay home, I didn't sterilize my mail and packages, I didn't have my groceries delivered, I didn't wear a mask unless I absolutely had to, I continued to visit with friends, I didn't let the cleaning ladies go, I didn't stop working, I continued to run errands at every store that was open, AND I NEVER GOT COVID, nor did I ever take a covid test. I didn't trust them either. And now, I don't even have antibodies. There I was out in the world doing everything I was supposed to NOT do, and I don't have a single covid antibody. Hello? That's even true of my 90-year-old mother, who also didn't get the jab and didn't stay home, though she did take at least four covid tests, none of which was positive.

But my word, did I witness a LOT of harm and a lot of strained relationships! Just in my own circle, one friend, who got every shot and every booster, got covid at least three times and ultimately took his own life, to the horror of everyone. Something had clearly gotten disconnected in his brain. He was a lovely man who somehow went right off the rails for no apparent reason. Another lovely, healthy, lively, happy, active, outdoorsy friend in her 70s died in the night with no warning. Almost every single friend or relative who took the shot got covid at least once. A cousin became permanently disabled and now spends his days in a wheelchair, though he insists (but I don't believe) he has "long covid."

Beyond that, I witnessed the roll-out of a shamefully untested and experimental and toxic "remedy" that corporate media was claiming would save humanity, which was in serious peril from this "thing." I witnessed parents masking infants and toddlers, absurd mandates and lockdowns, politicians and so-called "health experts" encouraging everyone to get jabbed, accusing the unjabbed of being killers, forced jabs to save your job, jabbing infants and toddlers, jabbing pregnant women, government-coordinated censorship, black-listing and debanking people telling the TRUTH (or at least their God-given personal opinions), hospitals removing people from transplant lists for refusing the jab, doctors refusing to work with parents who won't go along with vaccines, literal concentration camps and police brutality in Australia, a stunning and highly suspicious internationally coordinated circling of the wagons (for real? the entire planet doing the exact same thing all at once with no debate or discussion for a "sudden" "pandemic"?), police arresting young people on the beach, implementation of hospital protocols literally designed to kill, and hundreds, if not thousands, of doctors losing their licenses for not going along with a NARRATIVE THAT DEFIED SCIENCE.

I'll stop there. I'm saying nothing you haven't seen yourselves. The blessing (there's always a blessing, even in this kind of massively destructive failure of public policy) is that it has shaken a lot of people into a kind of wakefulness and rebellion not previously present, that Bobby Kennedy can be Secretary of HHS, that the former ACIP committee was fired for egregious conflicts of interest and rubber stamping (the opposite of science), that lawsuits against mandates are being won, that exemptions are opening up, that medical freedom is being legislated. Slowly but surely.

Count me in as one voice against anything like this EVER happening again. One voice for medical freedom. One voice against medical mandates. One voice against censorship and most especially government-sanctioned censorship. One voice applauding most of you (some of you are still pretty entrenched in anti-science). One voice for restoring freedom to doctors and restoring their medical licenses. One voice for removing any FINANCIAL incentive to give vaccines, to refuse informed consent, or use hospital protocols that make no earthly moral, legal, ethical, spiritual, or scientific sense. One voice for doctors and hospitals and schools accepting everyone regardless of vaccination history.

There is NO one single source of Truth except God. There should never be a Fauci again.

Stand firm, please. For freedom, justice, equality, safety. For Americans.

Sincerely,
Anne Wallace

Title: Comment from Spearman, Paul
Comment:
I am an infectious diseases medical professional, and in this cpacity I urge ACIP to return to the use of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Hodinka, Richard
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Anonymous

I'm writing in support of COVID vaccines for everyone!

For those with compromised immune systems, the threat of COVID-19 didn't disappear when the pandemic "ended." While the general population has largely returned to a state of normalcy, immunocompromised individuals—including myself with autoimmune disorders—remain in a high-stakes battle where the virus is still a "lethal health threat".

The importance of COVID-19 vaccines for this community is not just a matter of personal safety; it is a critical component of a layered defense strategy.

The importance of the vaccine extends to the social circle of the immunocompromised. Since their own immune systems may not respond fully to the shot, they rely on the immunity of those around them. I need my friends and family to be able to receive the COVID vaccine in order to keep people like me safe and healthy.

Please make it so everyone is eligible to receive the COVID vaccine; to not only protect themselves but the people they love...people like me.

Thank you for your time.

Title: Comment from Quinnell , Rebecca
Comment:
I received one dose of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine in 2021. Not long after, my life changed in ways I never could have imagined.

I began experiencing heart palpitations, severe brain fog, and menstrual cycle irregularities. My skin erupted in painful rashes that spread across my face, arms, and legs, forming boil-like lesions that made even the simplest daily tasks unbearable. The pain was excruciating. For years I lived through flare-ups that left me physically and emotionally exhausted, and even today I still struggle when those symptoms return. But im lucky, more than most.

I am a nurse. Caring for people was not just my job — it was my calling. But when I spoke up about what was happening to me, when I stood my ground about my injuries and refused to pretend everything was fine, I lost my job. Losing my health was devastating. Losing the job I loved was another kind of heartbreak.

What has hurt almost as much as the illness itself is the silence. The dismissal. Doctors who would not even consider that my symptoms could be connected. Being made to feel like what I was experiencing wasn’t real, even while I was living it every single day. Right in front of their eyes!!!

This is not about politics. This is about people. It is about patients who deserve to be heard, believed, and cared for with honesty and compassion. When people raise concerns about their health, those concerns should never be ignored.

My story is only one voice, but there are many others who feel just as unheard. Human lives matter. Transparency matters. And the courage to face difficult truths in medicine matters!!!

Title: Comment from Rodriguez, Maria
Comment:
I have been an infectious diseases medical professional since 1994 when I finished my fellowship. Since my fellowship I have been interested in vaccines. Clinically I have been an advocate for vaccination with my patients (I work at the VA). We absolutely need evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage to the medical community and the population at large. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency makes as lose credibility in the process and the recommendations. Members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet or issue new or revised recommendations.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients (Veterans), their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not pre-existing beliefs.

I completely agree with the following: ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I am a committed advocate for COVID vaccines. I am a witness of the morbidity and mortality associated with severe COVID-19 illness, and I personally participated in studies evaluating its effectiveness among Veterans.
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Kristiansen, Martin
Comment:
Comment to ACIP – Individual Patient Experience (Denmark)

Submitted by: Martin Kristiansen
Former Senior IT Project Leader, Denmark

BACKGROUND
Healthy Senior IT project leader, 15+ years experience, worked 50-60 hours weekly, active lifestyle.

VACCINATION AND SYMPTOM ONSET
• Vaccine: Pfizer-BioNTech (2nd dose), August 10, 2021
• Symptom onset: August 13, 2021 (3 days after vaccination)
• Duration: 4.5+ years and ongoing (March 2026)

INITIAL SYMPTOMS (within 3 days)
Severe influenza-like symptoms, extreme dizziness (falling down stairs), tinnitus, pulsating headache, extreme light/sound sensitivity, nausea, fever, joint pain, elevated CRP (12-18 mg/L, persistently elevated 4+ years). Physician reported to Danish Health Authority.

CURRENT CONDITION (March 2026)
Medical assessment (April 2025) by my general practitioner:

Functional capacity:
- Maximum 2 hours activity/day in 30-minute intervals
- Cognitive function severely impaired after 30 minutes
- Unable to perform any occupational work
- Requires bed rest 22 hours/day
- Can only walk 20-25 meters before severe worsening
- Requires assistance for all household tasks

Persistent symptoms: Severe dizziness/balance problems (requires walking stick), post-exertional malaise, cognitive dysfunction, tinnitus with 30% hearing loss, chronic headache, extreme light/sound sensitivity, nausea, IBS with daily incontinence, 50 kg weight gain, chronic fatigue.

Medical diagnosis: Suspected ME/CFS - cannot be officially diagnosed in Denmark.

SYSTEM FAILURES
- Denied compensation TWICE by Danish Patient Compensation Authority
- Denied investigation TWICE by Infectious Disease Department (OUH) despite elevated CRP for 4+ years
- No treatment available in public healthcare for vaccine injuries
- All specialist care paid privately
- Medical conclusion: Condition chronic and permanent, no treatment options, any work attempt would worsen condition

EMPLOYMENT IMPACT
My employer provided maximum accommodation (100% home office, flexible hours, reduced workload). December 2021-2023: Multiple attempts to increase working hours without success. Even minimal work caused severe symptom worsening. April 2024: Full-time sick leave. December 31, 2024: Terminated due to illness.

Physician assessment: "Any form of employment in the ordinary labor market is impossible, and accommodations cannot make it possible."

FINANCIAL AND SOCIAL IMPACT
Lost career and income. All medical treatment paid privately. Denied disability compensation twice. Cannot perform personal care, shopping, cooking without assistance. Cannot participate in family events (2 hours requires 2-3 days bed rest). Marriage ended due to illness strain. Minimal interaction with son.

SYSTEMIC PROBLEMS IN DENMARK

EU Commission admission (August 2025): European Commission confirmed COVID-19 vaccines granted conditional authorization because "comprehensive data dossier is not yet available." Manufacturers obligated to "conduct further studies and provide more data to confirm efficacy or safety." Questions remain about complete safety data for long-term adverse events.
Reference: https://www.europarl.europa.eu/doceo/document/E-10-2025-002126-ASW_EN.html

Recognition barriers: ME/CFS cannot be diagnosed in Denmark. No diagnostic codes for vaccine injuries. Patients cannot be identified for research. No structured care pathways. Physicians fail to report despite mandatory obligations.

Research gaps: No systematic follow-up. Incomplete registry data. Vaccine-injured recruited for new trials due to lack of coding.

Compensation barriers: Applications denied despite clear temporal relationship and 4+ years documented illness. Burden of proof on severely disabled patients.

CONCLUSION
Became severely disabled within 3 days of COVID-19 vaccination. After 4.5 years: Remain severely ill. Denied recognition and compensation twice. Denied investigation twice. No treatment available. Lost career. Dependent on assistance. Physician confirms chronic and permanent with no treatment options.

Danish healthcare has no pathway for recognition, diagnosis, treatment, or compensation for vaccine-injured patients.

I urge ACIP to:
1. Establish diagnostic codes for post-vaccination chronic syndromes
2. Create dedicated clinical programs for vaccine-injured patients
3. Ensure long-term systematic follow-up and research
4. Improve adverse event reporting and pharmacovigilance
5. Include patient organizations in safety evaluations
6. Provide transparent communication about risks and support for those harmed

Respectfully submitted,
Martin Kristiansen
Denmark
March 2026

More details: https://martinjkristiansen.com/

Former Senior IT Project Leader
Now permanently disabled since COVID-19 vaccination (August 2021)

Title: Comment from Montague, Brian
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.
