Title: Comment from Meehan, Timothy
Comment:
Subject: Concerns Regarding mRNA COVID-19 Vaccine Recommendations – Personal Experience and Safety Questions
Dear ACIP Members,
I regret receiving three doses of mRNA COVID-19 vaccines (Pfizer) between early 2021 and 2022. Each dose produced progressively worse adverse events. After the third dose I was bedridden and ill for a full week. Five weeks later I developed persistent chest/heart pain that required medical evaluation. When Novavax (Nuvaxovid) became available, I experienced no adverse events at all.
These personal experiences have left me deeply concerned about the safety profile of mRNA vaccines, especially for repeated or future use.
We are now more than five years into the mRNA vaccine era, yet critical safety questions remain unanswered:
1. Contamination / plasmid DNA — Dr. Phillip Buckhaults (University of South Carolina) conducted independent testing in 2023 and presented his findings to the South Carolina Senate on September 12, 2023. He detected residual plasmid DNA in Pfizer and Moderna vials at levels far exceeding regulatory limits, including sequences capable of integration. The FDA has not publicly conducted or released its own independent replication or investigation of these findings. This lack of follow-up is unconscionable given the widespread use of these products.
2. Biodistribution studies — The FDA approved these vaccines without requiring comprehensive, long-term biodistribution studies in humans. Animal data showed lipid nanoparticles distributing widely (ovaries, testes, brain, bone marrow, etc.), yet no large-scale human biodistribution data has ever been required or published post-authorization.
3. Alternative exists — Novavax’s protein-based Nuvaxovid has a markedly better safety profile in my experience and in comparative data (lower rates of myocarditis/pericarditis, fewer systemic reactions). It is a safe and effective COVID-19 vaccine option that does not rely on mRNA or lipid nanoparticles.
Given these unresolved issues, I respectfully urge ACIP not to recommend mRNA COVID-19 vaccines for general or repeated use until the FDA has:
• Independently verified and addressed the plasmid DNA contamination findings.
• Required and published comprehensive human biodistribution and long-term integration studies.
• Conducted direct comparative safety trials of mRNA vs. protein-based platforms.
Public trust requires transparency and rigorous, independent science—not assumptions that “the old data are still good enough.”
Thank you for your time and consideration.
Sincerely,
Tim M
Aiken, SC

Title: Comment from Matison, Leora
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199

Public Comment for March 18 to 19 CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose.

I am a patient and person with a disability who's symptoms greatly resemble those caused by common post-viral illnesses. I also live with family who are immunocompromised and rely on herd immunity for protection their immune systems cannot give them for themselves. I am deeply worried about recent harmful and unwarranted changes on vaccine policy at the CDC and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition.

Today, I am writing to express grave concerns of the future of COVID vaccines and Long COVID.

Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines. Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection. It continues to be debilitating condition that affects people’s health.

RFK Jr., Marty Makary, and Vinay Prasad have previously imposed new policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will without doubt harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone. The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines. Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, and are especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions especially under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. Long COVID is a debilitating condition that affects millions of Americans. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop this serious condition. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year (due to their rapid waning), for all COVID vaccine formulations. I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID either by preventing it or developing effective treatments. I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using open and transparent processes, all of which are hallmarks of democracy.

Title: Comment from Thye, Lora

At the time, I was preparing to travel internationally to an area that was considered a red zone, and the messaging I saw consistently stated that the vaccine was safe and effective.

Shortly after receiving my first vaccine dose, I began experiencing transient ischemic attacks (TIAs). While working as a hairstylist, I had episodes where the right side of my body would stop functioning properly. These events typically lasted around five minutes before resolving.

My hope is that my experience will be acknowledged and included in the data as agencies continue evaluating vaccine safety and the long-term impacts on individuals.

Title: Comment from Holm, Larry
Comment:
Hello and thank you,

As a retired NASA research and development systems design test engineer, I applaud you for soliciting input from Americans with real world experience, which is vital in obtaining rock solid quality results.

There are 3 subject areas I’d like to address, as follows:

1 - Family, friends, loved ones, and myself injured after general vaccines
2 - Post-covid19 vaccine injuries among my family, friends, and loved ones
3 - The nature of ‘fit for vessel’ vaccine testing, science, and policy

So, number 1:
1a - Because my mother was not allowed to opt out for me from vaccines, which she wanted to do after my older brother’s death as a result of a vaccine, after 4 different vaccines, I suffered the most severe anaphylactic reactions as a child. Totally closed breathing passages, extreme interventions, hours on the edge, totally blacked out yet still hearing the doctors say things like “hurry up, we’re loosing this kid.”
1b - I know intimately of 3 deaths from regular vaccines; my older brother, a couple in my church, and my best friend who lost her first child the very night of receiving a vaccine (and the child was perfectly healthy beforehand)
1c - I’ve been connected to friends whose child developed autism since the ’80s. Since then and in my community now, I know approximately 400 families where both parents are certain that their child’s illness originated with a vaccine.

Concluding number one then, I’d like to make a statement regarding law, that is trial law evidence, because after my engineering career, I went into law, even teaching law research on a national basis. My observation is this:

The real experience of people is the number one safety and effectiveness signal. Why? In trial proceedings, the highest form of testamentary evidence is direct credible eye witness testament. Not industry policy/white papers, not controlled industry white papers, not peer reviewed group think, not boardroom agenda, no, none of those. At trial direct credible eye witness testimony, especially when there’s a mountain of direct credible eye witness experiences.

It’s a horrible state of affairs that policy push has all but excluded the massive records over the decades from eye witnesses, our friends, families, and loved ones!

Ok, number 2 - Covid19 related injuries
2a - my daughter is a highly skilled exotic animal surgeon serving our Army’s animal helpers. As a Captain she was phenomenally healthy, running 5 miles with an 80lb pack on her back. After the forced injection, which she tried to opt out of, she spent a very long time over 2 years in ICU with numerous severe life threatening after effects of being poisoned by that particular serum. Not days, not weeks, but months!

In my circle of family, friends, and loved ones, I know personally of 13 deaths. In several cases their death was a high-speed cancer, in others a slow HIV like wasting into death. My niece, an ER surgeon, suffers injury to this day as does my daughter.

Now 3, The nature of ‘fit for vessel’ vaccine testing, science, and policy
I have reviewed a few of the safety testing and approvals. I hate to say this, but they are solidly pathetic, for a quick and easy glance on that, please read attorney Aaron Siri’s new book.

In my engineering testing career, I apprenticed under Dr. Charles Wakefield, the head engineer responsible for testing and certifying the Boeing 747 aircraft. The depth, level, and breadth of tests performed is essential, otherwise the vehicle is not fit, and if any one component or system isn’t fit for the vessel, it doesn’t fly.

I surveyed the stated and undisclosed ingredients, and, considering that the body is complex, but not so complex that an ingredient couldn’t be fully vetted, I have to ask how were professional analysis and tests never done? How was that huge portion of the body that is a biofield, with bioelectric properties never looked at? A single isotope of normallly innocent substances through the mouth placed into a vein can greatly alter the body’s bioelectrical functions.

So there’s half the body properties never tested, please expand testing, and keep all products out of people’s veins until proven fit for the vessel.

Thank You,
Larry Holm

Title: Comment from Leeds, Rachel
Comment:
I am a disabled law student concerned about the future of vaccines. I urge the ACIP to protect access to vaccines, disseminate truthful information on the safety and efficacy of vaccines, and commit to research on long Covid.

As a person with an incurable chronic illness, getting sick with Covid or the flu can incapacitate me for far longer than the acute stage of the illness. When I contracted Covid in 2023, I needed to take a 2 month medical leave from my job and go on short term disability. Access to vaccines that are readily available and covered by insurance makes it possible for me to attend school, hold a job, and engage in civic life.

Vaccines are also important for herd immunity. Some disabled people cannot get vaccines due to allergies to vaccine components or do not benefit from vaccines because they are immunocompromised. When most people around them are vaccinated, they are protected and able to participate in public life. If people who can safely and effectively get vaccines decide against it because of incorrect or misleading information, it will become less and less safe for people with underlying health conditions to participate in public life. People in a discrete and already marginalized community will have to decide between a life of isolation and risking serious illness or death. This has been growing as a problem since Covid protections were lifted, and the ACIP has a responsibility to prevent it from getting worse. The best solution is a general recommendation for people of all ages including pregnant people to get vaccinated for Covid twice a year + a general recommendation for all other vaccines that prevent long term, serious, and potentially fatal illnesses on a schedule recommended by doctors.

Additionally, long Covid needs to be prioritized, researched, and considered when making decisions on vaccine recommendations. I’ve seen arguments that Covid vaccines should not be recommended because the effects have not been studied enough, but the same argument applies to Covid itself. which is a much greater threat. Covid impacts all systems in the body. Many who contract it develop chronic long-term complications. Make America Healthy Again leaders say they want to lower the rates of preventable chronic diseases in this country, which is an admirable goal. But the goal becomes hollow if the CDC fails to take scientifically proven steps to reduce a leading cause of long term chronic illness. I ask ACIP to take meaningful action to address long Covid.

Thank you so much for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Gleason , Sarah
Comment:
I received my 2nd shot of Moderna on 4/2/21. I was a healthy 41 yr old in the best shape of my life, smart, social, extremely independent and capable. I was thrilled to get vaccinated and rebuild my business and my finances after the shutdown. I love my work as a massage therapist, and it was killing me to not be able to do the work I love so much with my clients who I cared for deeply. When the shot went in, I immediately felt something in my system. I felt it coursing through my bloodstream, thought that was weird, based on the research. It was definitely not just staying in the muscle tissue. So afterwards, I waited in my car another 30 min to be sure I was ok to drive. By 12:30p, the flu reaction had begun. By 8p, I was experiencing fever, terrible chills and head to toe body aches. 3 days later, I felt extreme fatigue and brain fog, which seemed to be improving I even tried to work for a couple of weeks, but by mid-June I was incapacitated, and by July, the symptoms had exploded. I was in the ER, convinced I was having a seizure or stroke. Since then, 5 years later, no signs of stopping, it's been a rollercoaster of debilitating symptoms such as: extreme fatigue and cognitive disfunction, inability to regulate temp/intolerance to heat and cold, dizziness/vertigo, insomnia, tingling and numbness all over the body, random pain shooting, heart pain, palpitations/extreme sweating from any level of exertion, darting and double vision, floaters, inability to focus eyes and brain,, dehydration despite plenty of water and electrolytes, food sensitivities and allergies that didn't exist before, have to carry an epi pen, extreme muscle spasms, intense itching that can show up anywhere in the body, difficulty managing emotions, tantrums/meltdowns from overstimulation and takes very little to overstimulate, torso tension and pain like a girdle too tight, loss of appetite, ravenous hunger, esophagus spasms and closes around food causing choking. pain after eating anything at all, tachycardia, sensitivity to light and sound, feeling sick all of the time/constant daily malaise, difficulty reading, comprehending, texting, remembering, internal tremors that are also external at times, inability to sit or stand some days, no endurance for any activity. Hands and/or arms will just give out. Having to focus on walking/ wobbly and bumping into things, falling, inability to calm nerves, adrenaline dumps, muscles seizing, manic symptoms, extreme neck and jaw/face tension, unable to relax it, blood sugar issues/high A1c that diet management does nothing to bring down, inability to exercise since it induces even more malaise vertigo palpitations, can't have conversations without extreme reactions, can't even watch things for too long, some days can't tolerate it at all. Everything takes energy I either don't have or it triggers the nervous system to rev up, and then I have to do hours of breathing in a dark room to calm down. There's more too. The symptoms cycle. I have been diagnosed with POTS, if labels help, I have akathisia, paresthesia, full body neuropathy, migraines, occular migraines. Doctors have thought I have MS, chronic fatigue, autoimmune disease, Lyme disease, and more, but I pass all the tests, except for POTS. It's frustrating for them and for me, but I'm lucky to have doctors in my corner, but they are helpless too.

Despite this nightmare of debilitating symptoms I've been experiencing every single day for 5 years, I've been denied disability, even with doctor's notes and lawyers, I was denied. I applied to the CICP, didn't hear anything back until 2 years later they requested more information. I sent it immediately, and 3 years from that contact  I have heard nothing more from them. The CICP and VICP is a completely useless, smoke and mirrors situation. A complete joke, and a slap in the face for those of us who continue to suffer with no help and no end in sight.

I've reported this to VAERS from the beginning. That's a whole other broken, maddening system that does absolutely nothing to help us.

Symptoms are just as bad as they were the 1st year, unpredictable, unmanageable, ever-evolving. Most days, I can't drive, and if I do, I'm aware I shouldn't be, but I have no choice. I can't work, because I never know what symptoms the day will bring and when and to what intensity. Sometimes they last for hours, sometimes for months. Some symptoms never go away. On any given day, the more I do, the worse the symptoms get. I can't read much, I can't watch for long, I can't go for walks. Zoom is very overwhelming to the nervous system, so just phone calls when I can manage. It's extremely isolating. With no acknowledgement, people don't know what to think and some still don't believe it's real. Im left trying to explain things and advocate for myself, when I can barely do the basics each day. It's been a nightmare I wouldn't wish on anyone. We did what we were told was the safe, right thing. We deserve help.

Title: Comment from Petti, Brian
Comment:
I am submitting this comment to highlight a structural issue with the Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program (CICP).

The program currently requires claims to be filed within one year of the injury. For individuals who experience serious medical complications, this deadline can expire before they are even able to understand their condition or learn that the program exists. Severe illness, ongoing treatment, and cognitive impairment can make it impossible to navigate a complex federal claims process during that time.

As a result, some individuals are permanently barred from filing a claim simply because their diagnosis or recovery took longer than one year. That was me, a husband and father of 2 young children.

A reasonable improvement would be to extend the filing window or adopt a discovery based rule so the deadline begins when a patient reasonably becomes aware their condition may be related to a covered countermeasure.

In addition, adverse event reporting depends heavily on physician participation. Strengthening reporting compliance would help ensure serious events are properly documented and included in federal safety monitoring systems.

These changes would help ensure the program functions as a true safety net for people who experience serious adverse events.

Title: Comment from Homes, Luna
Comment:
Thank you for the opportunity to submit comments.

ACIP recommendations carry major legal and practical consequences. They shape the CDC schedule and drive awareness and policy nationwide. As such, ACIP must operate with strict transparency, scientific integrity, procedural fairness, and public accountability. The COVID era demonstrated the opposite too often: compressed process, one-sided presentations, conflicts of interest, censored public response, coercive implementation, and failure to confront uncertainty honestly. That record destroyed public trust, deepened polarization, and will require discipline, courage, and candor to confront and repair.

The point is simple. All material scientific disputes, safety signals, ethical objections, and policy tradeoffs from the COVID response must be publicly aired, publicly debated, and publicly answered. That includes vaccines, reporting, masking, testing, distancing, school and business closures, tracking, censorship, mandates, unethical mandates, travel restrictions, job loss and school exclusion for refusals, masking, medical license loss, deadly hospital protocols, inaccessibility of free-choice medications, refusal to let younger and college-age students into schools without shots, and the response to questioning and dissent. Agencies cannot restore trust by asking the public to forget. They must review the record, acknowledge failures, and formalize safeguards so this cannot happen again.

1. Process was not good enough.
The Administrative Procedure Act of 1946 and the Federal Advisory Committee Act reflect core duties of notice, openness, public participation, and reasoned decision-making. Advisory meetings are supposed to be open and timely noticed. Yet key COVID-era advisory actions proceeded on compressed timelines, with weak process, broken public access, and a later failure to account for it. (US Code)

2. Informed consent under EUA was ethically compromised.
Federal law requires that recipients of EUA products be informed that the product is authorized for emergency use, of the known and potential risks and benefits and the extent to which they are unknown, and of the option to accept or refuse, including consequences and alternatives. That standard cannot be squared with real-world conditions in which people were not informed and in which refusal cost people jobs, education, travel, and ordinary civic participation. A nominal right to refuse is not meaningful informed consent when refusal carries severe penalties. ACIP must say that plainly and adopt enforceable guardrails now.

3. Safety surveillance was not transparent enough to be credible.
CDC also states that VAERS is designed to detect safety signals that require follow-up in other systems. That means VAERS cannot be dismissed simply because it is imperfect. ACIP must require a public dashboard showing which safety signals are under review, what methods are being used, what remains unresolved, and what findings led to action, no action, or further study. It also must be improved dramatically.

4. Conflicts and one-sided proceedings damaged legitimacy.
The public saw agency and sponsor views dominate formal proceedings while contrary views were minimized. Technical compliance with disclosure rules is not enough. When recommendations affect products tied to major governmental and commercial interests, the process must be visibly independent. Equal time for qualified independent experts, advance posting of all waivers, and stricter conflict rules are mandatory if ACIP expects to be trusted again.

5. Payment incentives and outcome classification require review.
CMS created a 20 percent increase in certain Medicare inpatient payments for qualifying COVID-19 cases and later required a documented positive laboratory test for that add-on payment. This absolutely justifies retrospective review of coding, mortality classification, and incentive effects. Agencies must examine those facts openly instead of dismissing public concern.

ACIP is adopting some of these fixes, and most must continue and be formalized so this never happens again.

ACIP must adopt:
Publish a formal public-comment protocol; issue written responsiveness summaries; review past public comments and explain how they were handled; audit COVID-era notice, links, access, and recordkeeping; require balanced expert panels; post all conflicts and waivers in advance; and conduct a full after-action review focused on accountability and lessons learned.

HHS is now committed to transparency, gold-standard science, diverse expertise, and restoring scientific integrity through radical transparency. ACIP must convert those promises into binding process now, while a deeper review proceeds. The public trust lost during COVID will not be restored by slogans. It will be restored only by truth, accountability, and durable procedural reform.

Thank you for considering these comments.

Title: Comment from Barnett-Rose, Rita
Comment:
I am submitting this comment ahead of the upcoming ACIP meeting reviewing COVID-19 vaccine recommendations.

Across the United States, millions of Americans now report that they—or someone they know—experienced serious health problems following COVID vaccination. Families describe myocarditis, neurological injuries, autoimmune disorders, pregnancy complications, fast-acting cancers, and sudden deaths occurring after vaccination. Whether every reported case is causally linked to the vaccines is a question for scientific investigation. But the scale of these reports has created a profound credibility problem when official assurances continue to insist that serious adverse events are extremely rare.

Importantly, these concerns are not based only on anecdote. Federal safety surveillance systems themselves showed signals that warranted far greater scrutiny.

VAERS accumulated unprecedented numbers of reports during the COVID vaccination campaign, including thousands of deaths and serious injuries following vaccination. At the same time, data from the CDC’s v-safe monitoring program—released only after litigation—revealed that roughly one-quarter of participants reported being unable to perform normal daily activities after vaccination, with millions reporting missed work or medical care.

Meanwhile, the Vaccine Safety Datalink (VSD), one of the most powerful vaccine safety databases available, remains largely inaccessible to independent researchers and the public. Greater transparency and independent access to this data are essential if public confidence is to be restored.

The lived experience of many Americans does not align with the assurances that COVID vaccine harms are extremely rare. Until these signals are openly examined and fully investigated, public trust will remain deeply damaged.

This newly constituted ACIP committee has an opportunity to change course. I urge the committee to discuss the available safety data openly and honestly, to acknowledge the limitations of existing surveillance systems, and to support greater transparency and independent review of the data—including the VSD.

Given the unresolved safety signals and the current crisis of public confidence, ACIP should also reconsider its recommendation for COVID vaccination and suspend further recommendations until these concerns are thoroughly investigated.

Honest and transparent discussion of the data is essential to restoring public trust in vaccine policy and public health institutions.

Thank you for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Vaughan, Patrick
Comment:
Hi, my name is Patrick Vaughan, I am a scientist and engineer, and I possess no conflicts of interest.

The anti-vaxxer stance on the lack of safety of the covid-19 vaccines has focused on 3 points. The first being myocarditis, the second thromboembolisms (calling it the “clot shot”), and the third being the anti-vaxxer belief that the covid-19 vaccine is the cause of Long Covid. These points either target an incredibly small fraction of people, have been recently explained, or have no supporting evidence behind them.

For the claim the covid-19 vaccines cause Myocarditis, the signal was only seen in very young male children between 14-21 years of age. Of the small proportion of male children that develop this, the vast majority have the issue pass within a few days. Myocarditis was transient (passing within a short period of time) in ~90% of young male children, where it resolved on its own within 24-72 hours. Of the small percent of male children where it didn’t resolve within a few days, it could not definitively be associated as a direct response to receiving the covid vaccine. Additionally, from the covid safety data from the past 3 years, there has been no single for myocarditis above background levels observed.

For the claim that the covid-19 vaccine is a “clot shot”, aka it causes thromboembolism and thrombocytopenia, these signals have also never been associated with either the mRNA (Pfizer/Moderna) or protein-based (Novavax) platforms. It has however been associated with adenovirus-based covid-19 vaccines (Astrezeneca and J&J). These covid-19 vaccines were immediately noted for having these signals in 2021/2022, were pulled from the market, and are not available within the US. Furthermore, we now have direct scientific evidence for the cause of this outcome, and it is specifically due to a genetic predisposition and immune system interaction with the adenovirus component of those two vaccines. Notably, neither the mRNA nor the protein-based covid-19 vaccines possess this component. This is therefore not an outcome that is possible with the current available covid-19 vaccines.

There is also a misguided anti-vaxxer stance which has long persisted, the belief that the covid-19 vaccine is responsible for the development of Long Covid. There is no scientific evidence for this belief.

First, thousands of cases of long covid have been documented to have occurred before a covid-19 vaccine was even made available. Second, all studies have demonstrated that Long Covid rates are significantly reduced in vaccinated individuals as compared to unvaccinated. If the vaccine were the cause, we would not be seeing a signal for Long Covid in the unvaccinated, and we would instead see a higher rate of Long Covid in the unvaccinated, with multi-vaccinated individuals having the highest rate of occurrence. We see the opposite within all the data. In fact, the number of infections is strongly correlated with the Long Covid rate, where the greater the number of infections, the higher the chance any individual will have Long Covid. Furthermore, many studies have shown that covid-19 vaccination following a Long Covid diagnosis has contributed to increased recovery rates from the illness. Finally, due to a lack of precautions (mask usage) and a lack of testing (confirmation of disease), it is far more likely in every practical sense that a person with Long Covid developed the condition from either an asymptomatic or mild infection that they ignored. Then due to anti-vaccine hysteria, such as what is pushed by the members of this ACIP committee, they then falsely believe that the vaccine they received recently (or even years prior) is the cause of their new disability. We know it is not.

In terms of Long Covid recommendations, we should be using the NASEM definition for Long Covid – an infection-associated chronic condition that occurs after SARS-CoV-2 infection and is present for at least 3 months as a continuous, relapsing and remitting, or progressive disease state that affects one or more organ systems. Additionally, Long Covid can be broken down into subtype classifications (predominance via cardiovascular complications, neurologic complications, metabolic complications, respiratory issues, musculoskeletal issues, gastrointestinal issues, kidney issues, and etc.). We have direct scientific evidence that covid-19 impacts nearly every body system in negative ways, regardless of the severity of lack thereof of initial infection. This is the definition that should be utilized for Long Covid, and this committee should not try to alter what is considered Long Covid in an attempt to downplay its true burden on the US population at large.

The newly configured ACIP shows little to no knowledge of this framework and they have not used it to make any of their recommendations.

The agenda items for the March ACIP meeting are all outside that purvue of this committee in the limited context provided. Yes, all vaccines have some adverse effects but most are mild. Yes, the covid vaccine can rarely cause myocarditis, especially in adolescent and young adult males, but covid disease causes a higher rate of and and more severe myocarditis. That issue is very worthwhile to discuss along with what the covid disease burden is, including long covid and how effective the vaccine has been in preventing severe disease outcomes. It significantly protects against severe illness, hospitalization, and death from covid.

ACIP does not discuss coding for vaccine adverse events - that work is done by another federal advisory committee and it should not be on the agenda.

Please follow the ACIP charter and resume making recommendations based on strong scientific review. The covid vaccine prevented millions of deaths, ICU admissions, hospitalizations and severe illness during the pandemic. Covid still killed about 47,000 Americans in 2024.

What used to be the pre-eminent, gold standard committee for vaccine recommendations has become a national embarrassment. We need better.

Title: Comment from Garvey, Tim
Comment: Thank you for the opportunity to tell our family's story about vaccination. Our daughter was born in April 1985. At her 6 month Wellness Check she got a DPT shot, was cranky and sore and within a week having infantile seizures. We found out afterward the "P", pertussis vaccine, was known--for decades--to cause seizures. In 1986 the vaccine injury compensation law was passed that gave immunity to the drug companies and lip service to th vaccine injured. She is special needs with epilepsy, some CP and some autism. He got out, then sometime developed type 2 diabetes. His health was relatively steady, until 2022 when he was forced/coerced into getting 2 COVID Moderna shots. He died 6 weeks after his second shot of myocarditis on June 19th, 2022--Father's Day. He was 33 years old. Not the best Father's Day for me, was it? We were told we couldn't make a VAERS report because we weren't him and weren't his doctor but we found a way to do it anyways. The report was archived and suppressed but is retrievable anyways. In summary, we are just one family that has been affected by vaccine injury and death. Anyways, hope you all Listen and do the Right Thing.

Title: Comment from Scanlin, Kirsten
Comment:
I. Statement of Intent

I am filing this formal grievance to document the catastrophic physiological and financial ruin I have suffered following my sixth COVID-19 injection on October 7, 2023. For over two and a half years, I have been trapped in a body that feels like a torture chamber. I have formally been diagnosed with a Covid Vaccine Injury that caused Severe Small Fiber Neuropathy (SFN), Autonomic Nervous System Dysfunction, Autoimmune issues, a hysterectomy, and the list goes on.

II. Comprehensive Clinical Reality: Multi-Systemic Failure

Here is a list of debilitating symptoms I have endured or currently face. This is not a list of "side effects"; it is a total bodily collapse.

Neurological & Nerve Torture

Sensory Disturbances: Constant "red ant" biting sensations, stabbing nerve pains in the feet and other areas, and a "dripping" sensation in the buttocks, legs, hips, and ankles.

Nerve Pain & Burning: Severe burning and neuropathy in both feet and hands; nerve issues extending into my teeth; and intense pain when pressure is applied to fingers or arms.

Paresthesia: Tingling and numbness in the feet, toes, fingers, hands, face, nose, and lips; tingling on the scalp; and numbness radiating up both arms.

Tremors: Internal vibrating tremors that travel down the body; mild visible tremors.

Autonomic & Cardiovascular Collapse

Cardiac Distress: Constant chest pain since October 2023; heart racing (tachycardia) specifically upon waking in the morning.

Vascular Changes: New symptom of veins protruding visibly through the skin; blood pressure consistently lower than my baseline.

Regulation Issues: Acute heat intolerance; looking flushed for no reason; and a "wired" feeling.

Musculoskeletal & Systemic Debilitation

Profound Weakness: Extreme weakness in the neck, arms, and legs; a constant sensation of "heavy" legs and arms.

Chronic Pain: All-over body pain (combination of joint, muscle, and nerve pain); severe, stiff neck with weak muscles; and localized pain behind the left ribcage.

Exhaustion: Chronic fatigue and total exercise exhaustion; severe body stiffness upon rising.

Bulbar & Cognitive Impairment

Functional Loss: Dysphagia (fluids entering the nasal cavity); a persistent gagging sensation; and difficulty/strangeness speaking with my mouth or tongue.

Cognitive Decline: Severe brain fog; difficulty thinking and concentrating; and poor balance/vision issues.

Sensory Loss: Persistent tinnitus (ringing in the ears) and strange sensations in the left ear.

Pelvic, Urological & Gastrointestinal Distress

Acute Pelvic Pain: Severe pelvic floor pain (described as "knives" going up the vagina); severe lower abdomen cramping; and a constant "pulsation/vibration" in the hips and buttocks.

Urological/Reproductive: Persistent UTI-like sensations and urgency without infection; prior to hysterectomy, experienced uncontrollable menstrual bleeding.

Gastrointestinal: Upset stomach and chronic diarrhea.

Can you imagine the terror of these symptoms unfolding on your body and no doctor being able to figure out what is wrong with you for a very long time?

III. Financial and Moral Execution

I have good insurance, and we have been forced to pay over $200,000 out-of-pocket for diagnostics and pain management because the current system provides no support for the injured. This is a financial and moral execution. We followed the rules, and in return, we were abandoned to a life of misery and chronic pain.

IV. DEMANDS FOR IMMEDIATE ACTION

I am finished "reporting" symptoms for stagnant records. I demand the following:

Legislative Reform: Abolish the current, shielded legal framework and create a robust pathway for real compensation.

Statute of Limitations Extensions: It is an insult to suggest a two-year window is sufficient when victims spend years just seeking a diagnosis.

Accountability: The government must compensate every single person harmed, regardless of when they were finally able to file.

V. Conclusion

I deserve the life that was taken from me. My family deserves their life savings back. How does the CDC intend to rectify these systemic failures? I want answers, I want recognition, and I want the compensation that is owed to those of us who have paid the ultimate price for public policy.

Kirsten A. Scanlin

Kirsten@scanlin.com

Title: Comment from Sills, John
Comment:
Against my recommendation, but in favor of a brother’s recommendation, my healthy and active 95 year old mother, who never took her doctor’s yearly recommendations to get vaccinated for flu, pneumonia, or shingles, decided to get the “safe and effective “ Pfizer covid “vaccine”. She had her first injection in late February 2021. One week to the day later when leaving the drive through atm, she could not take her foot off of the gas pedal. She sped across Main Street between on-coming traffic, went into a vacant lot toward a convenience store, managed to miss the store and 2 large pecan trees before coming to a stop a few feet from a fruit stand when her 2 flattened tires (from hitting the curb so hard) plowed her to a stop. She had traveled over 100 yards. I remember telling one of my brothers that I hope it wasn’t from some bad reaction to the injection (his wife snickered at my ignorance). In mid March she took the second injection. I soon noticed her nearly losing her footing occasionally, not something I was accustomed to. Around the first of May she developed shingles in an ear and the back of her head and neck. At the end of the first week of May she had developed a severe case of facial palsy. Her doctor called it Bells Palsy while her ophthalmologist said it was Ramsey Hunt. She couldn’t blink or close her effected eye so he stitched it together to keep it from drying out. I wish I could say that was the worst of it, but it was all down hill from there for the next 2 years. She soon lost her ability to walk so my brother and I went into full time care for her alternating staying with her at her home. Her care became very demanding as she became wheelchair dependent, and as she went through times of leg spasms, hallucinations, hearing repetitive music in her head, having Parkinson’s-like symptoms and sometimes not knowing my brother or me. For the last 1+ year, we were aided by in-home hospice until she lapsed into a comatose sleep for the last 15 days of her life here on earth.
There is absolutely no doubt in my mind that the Pfizer injections were the cause of her demise. Prior to the injections I had retired and spent 7 years living near her and seeing her or doing things with her on a weekly or more frequent basis. She was completely independent until 1 week after her first injection when her 2+ year decline occurred. Fortunately I was leery of the genetic injections and never took one. And I never will!

Title: Comment from G, Jeanette
Comment:

This time in out life was the worst time in our life. Not only did we fight against illness, we fought to explain. We fought for understanding. And still 5 years later, we are still fighting. So yes, now that you have read this. And I'm sure, we all understand everything comes with risk.

For those that just want to continue to believe in vaccines, wouldn't it be in the best interst to investigate and learn more? Help the people that are sick. Provide care. How do you plan to help the families who lost their primary breadwinner. How about those who can no longer work. How do you plan to help them to keep your vaccine agenda afloat.

It is clear, the tide is turning. Information is at everyone's finger tips. It's time to get honest, provide solutions since the trust has been broken. Title: Comment from Keim, Stephanie

Members of CDC and ACIP,

I will keep my comment brief as brief as possible as I know there are mountains of data and evidence that demonstrate that vaccines, in general, and COVID vaccines specifically, are safe and vital to curbing the spread of infectious diseases and preventing the worst effects of diseases.

1. We need universal access to the updated COVID vaccines for people of all ages, and regardless of medical conditions, at least once a year. COVID vaccines provided twice a year would be better as research shows vaccine effectiveness starts to wane within a few months, and wastewater data demonstrates we have multiple COVID waves every year.

2. COVID vaccines reduce the risk of Long COVID and severe disease for people of all ages. Long COVID still affects 10-20% of people after they get infected, and has huge impacts on people’s quality of life, their cost of medical care, their ability to work, and their ability to go to school - because yes, long COVID affects children too. We can see through the statistics on disability, that rates have been on an upward trajectory since 2020 and are continuing to rise.

I hope you see that COVID vaccines are greatly important for individual health and overall public health, but if that doesn’t sway you, they are of great economic importance as well. We need updated COVID vaccines twice a year and the general public needs easy access to those vaccines.

Thank you,
Stephanie Keim

Title: Comment from Alexander, Joseph
Comment:
Dear ACIP Board Members,

I am writing regarding vaccine safety, side effects, and the growing public distrust surrounding how these issues are discussed.

For more than forty years, individuals who report developing chronic ailments after vaccination have frequently been dismissed or stigmatized. Many are treated as if they are irresponsible or conspiracy-minded simply for describing an adverse health event that occurred after receiving a vaccine. At the same time, the public messaging surrounding vaccines often minimizes or dismisses the possibility of side effects altogether, creating the perception that acknowledging them is somehow illegitimate.

To be clear, I am not claiming that vaccines provide no benefit. Vaccines may benefit some individuals. My point is that not every person needs or wants every vaccine, and not everyone is willing to accept the potential risks associated with them.

One reason trust continues to erode is that vaccine policy and messaging often appear to begin from a predetermined conclusion: that vaccines must be promoted at all costs. When policy is built around the assumption that the public must be persuaded or managed rather than fully informed, people inevitably question whether they are being given the full picture. In many cases, the underlying philosophy appears to be that a “noble lie” is preferable to open discussion because authorities believe people might otherwise refuse vaccination.

Public health messaging has also at times made sweeping claims that vaccines are the single greatest contributor to improvements in human health and longevity—sometimes even greater than basic sanitation or clean drinking water. Such statements strain credibility. Access to clean, potable water and improved sanitation are widely recognized as foundational drivers of increased life expectancy across the world.

I understand that many people strongly disagree with my perspective. However, my position is straightforward: vaccines should remain available to those who want them, but no one should be compelled to receive them. Personal medical decisions should ultimately remain with the individual.

For that reason, there should be clear federal protections for religious exemptions within programs such as Vaccines for Children (VFC). Any state that participates in VFC funding should allow both religious and philosophical exemptions. States can decide whether they wish to accept federal funding under those conditions.

Trust in public health institutions has also been damaged when influential figures appear to advocate for mandates while simultaneously shaping policy discussions. When individuals who publicly support mandates are perceived to have disproportionate influence over advisory processes, it reinforces the perception that policy outcomes are predetermined.

Rebuilding public confidence will require openness, transparency, and respect for individual decision-making. People are far more likely to trust institutions that acknowledge uncertainty, openly discuss risks, and respect personal autonomy.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerley,

Joseph Alexander

Title: Comment from Anonymous

I respectfully recommend that the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices continue to support and recommend availability of a non-mRNA COVID-19 vaccine option for the public.

While mRNA vaccines have played an important role in the pandemic response, maintaining multiple vaccine technologies is important for public health flexibility, vaccine confidence, and broader population coverage. A protein-based vaccine such as the one developed by Novavax provides an alternative platform that may be preferable for individuals who cannot or choose not to receive mRNA vaccines.

Protein-based vaccines use a well-established technology that has been used safely for decades in other vaccines. Offering this option can improve vaccination uptake among individuals who are hesitant toward mRNA technology but are willing to receive a more traditional vaccine platform.

Maintaining a diversified vaccine portfolio also strengthens preparedness against future variants and ensures that healthcare providers can offer options tailored to individual patient needs.

For these reasons, I encourage ACIP to:
•Maintain recommendation pathways for protein-based COVID-19 vaccines.
•Ensure continued access to non-mRNA options for adults and vulnerable populations.
•Consider the role of alternative vaccine platforms in improving vaccination coverage.

Providing multiple vaccine technology options supports informed choice and can help strengthen public trust in vaccination programs.

Thank you for your consideration.

⸻

Personal Submission – COVID-19 Vaccine Adverse Reaction

My life changed dramatically within two to three days of receiving the Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 Vaccine.

Soon after vaccination I began experiencing severe chest pain, heart palpitations, dizziness, and episodes where I could not open my eyes because of how unwell I felt. The symptoms were so frightening that I repeatedly had to call ambulances for emergency medical assistance.

My condition deteriorated into a severe, disabling illness affecting multiple systems of my body. A cardiologist diagnosed me as having an adverse reaction following the vaccination.

At one point my condition became so severe that my legs stopped functioning properly and for around six months I had to drag myself along the ground because I could not walk. I also lost bladder control, which was devastating and humiliating.

Despite the severity of these symptoms, I was often told my condition was psychological or “in my head.” The gaslighting has been ongoing, even while I continue to live with serious and disabling physical symptoms.

Before this happened, I was not anti-vaccine. My children are vaccinated and I chose to receive the COVID vaccine because I trusted the public health advice being given at the time.

Due to my illness I lost my job, which eventually led to losing my home. As a solo mother with two young children, we spent four months homeless while I was severely ill and struggling to survive.

My children have witnessed repeated medical emergencies and have had to call ambulances for me more times than I can count. No child should have to live with the fear that their parent may collapse or become critically unwell.

The impact of this illness extends far beyond my own health. When a parent becomes suddenly and severely disabled, the entire family is affected — emotionally, financially, and socially.

Through this experience I have connected with many others facing similar struggles. I run an online support group with over 250 members in just one state who report serious health issues following COVID vaccination. I have also met many people in person experiencing similar conditions.

Behind every number is a real person whose life has been profoundly affected — parents, workers, and families trying to cope with sudden illness and disability.

Most of us wish desperately that none of this had happened. Many of us even wish the vaccine had fully protected everyone, because people suffering from **Long COVID often experience many of the same debilitating symptoms that we do.

The financial burden alone is overwhelming. My medications cost around $385 per month, yet I am largely bedridden and cannot stand for long without blacking out.

I am sharing my story in the hope that it leads to recognition, research, and support for those who are suffering.

Regardless of how frequently these reactions may occur, those affected deserve medical investigation, access to specialist care, and financial support if they are unable to work.

I also believe institutions and pharmaceutical companies that have benefited financially should contribute to research, treatment access, and support programs for those whose lives have been profoundly affected.

Above all, I ask for compassion and empathy.

Please see us as human beings. We are parents, partners, children, and members of our communities. What has happened to us could easily have happened to someone you love.

For many of us, this is not a debate.

This is our reality, and we are asking to be heard and helped.

⸻

Title: Comment from Shepard, Jamie
Comment:
To whom it may concern, I received my first and only Moderna vaccine on 3/31/21 in Dearborn Michigan and within a half hour, my life has never been the same. When I look back, calling it a never ending nightmare, is the only way to describe how everything felt for so long. To suddenly have your body stop working correctly is an experience I wouldn’t wish on anyone, but to have that, while being gaslight, laughed at and ignored added an unnecessary level of cruelty that I’ll never understand. Being pushed into a place where it’s inconvenient for you to exist, simply because it’s not a politically popular topic and also because it can hurt profits, shows us how truly self serving our government and corporations really can be.

I immediately suffered with symptoms associated with MCAS and POTS. I lost over 20 pounds in less then 2 months, it was a constant fight to simply eat and breath. My oxygen levels kept dropping, my circulation felt stuck, my temperature dropped, I had constant adrenaline dumps. I couldn’t sleep for more then 20 mins at a time, only to be woken up over and over again with numbness and trying to breath. Later, through blood work, we found my Compliment system was way off and my histamine levels were high. I also have severely low ferritin levels that have not been figured out yet. At one point, a cardiologist at Mayo Clinic, said it could be endothelial dysfunction in the veins in my heart. I also developed many food intolerances and I have suffered many different neurological symptoms.

Right after receiving the shot, I spent two years of my live going to doctors. First the ER visits, then the specialist, then Mayo Clinic in MN, and then finally a functional medicine doctor. The function medicine doctor was where I found the most answers and understanding. I was diagnosed with MCAS, we also found out that a bacteria that can cause Lyme was highly active in my system, and there were lots of flags that popped up in my bloodwork. It’s interesting that insurance doesn’t seem to cover a lot of the care and treatments that can actually help us.

I finally had to give up searching for help all the time, due to the costs and mental health. With what I learned from the functional medicine doctor, and then through my own research, I’ve worked hard on holistically healing myself the best I can. Every year is a challenge, but I keep trying and making progress. I do still struggle with driving, can’t fully exert myself to do things like hiking and exercising, I am still on a very restricted diet and I still have neurological symptoms and flair ups. I will say that being away from our medical system feels a lot safer to me and I’ve gotten more relief with diet and lifestyle then seeing doctors, who for the most part, didn’t understand what was happening and how to help. The worst were the ones who insisted that non of this is even real. Thinking outside the box and continuing to learn and be open is an incredibly important skill that seems to be missing in a lot of doctors offices. I’m so thankful for the ones who are open and understanding.

I feel like this has been such a missed opportunity to figure things out, prevent others from getting hurt and creating trust in our medical system. Instead we were ignored and the pharmaceutical companies did nothing, they showed zero interest in how badly their products hurt people and the numbers are impossible to know for sure because no one wanted to keep track. The CDC told me they were sorry and to report it to VEARs and that’s all they could do. I feel this is unacceptable and these government agencies and companies need to do better. Especially considering they make billions off of these products and non of it goes to helping the people they injure.

We need lots of things, but the first step is acknowledgment. People injured were trying to get help and were immediately labeled all sorts of things and brushed aside. At a National level we need to be acknowledged and the truth that these shots can cause damage to some people who took them needs to be told. It’s been at least over half a decade now, so please show that people injured can be taken seriously and helped. If we are to ever have any trust again in any of this, it has to start with the truth of what happened and for it to stop being hidden from the general public.

I appreciate your time and I hope we can start seeing progress on acknowledging and helping all those who have suffered. This would be a big step in building trust and showing that those in charge can put aside money and politics to do what’s right for our citizens.

Title: Comment from Tomatis, Cristina
Comment:
As a pediatric infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Nicodemus, Olivia
Comment: I am submitting this comment as a daughter who watched her father die in less than 24 hours. My father died on July 3, 2025. He had just turned 71 years old the month before. He was not someone with chronic health problems. He did not have high blood pressure. He did not have high cholesterol. He had never been diagnosed with heart disease and he was not taking medications for any ongoing medical conditions. There is no family history of heart disease among his father or brothers. My father never smoked. My father rarely ate red meat. In the week before his hospitalization he began feeling sick. Doctors later described it as a viral illness. Within days, his heart began to fail. What followed was something no family ever expects to experience. My father went into cardiac arrest. Doctors inserted an Impella device to support his heart and placed him on ECMO to keep his blood circulating when his heart could no longer function. Machines were breathing for him and pumping his blood. Our family stood around his hospital bed listening to doctors explain that he had severe myocarditis, an inflammation of the heart muscle. They told us it was likely caused by a virus. They also told us they might never know which virus. Less than 24 hours after being admitted to the hospital, my father was dead. One day he was here. The next day he was gone. What has stayed with me since that day is not only the grief of losing my father, but the lack of answers. My family has always trusted the medical system and public health guidance. We have never viewed vaccination or medical care through a political lens. We believed these systems exist to protect people and improve public health. But when a previously healthy person dies suddenly from myocarditis and no clear cause can be identified, families are left with questions that seem to have no place to go. Myocarditis has been widely studied in relation to viral infections and immune responses. When unexplained fatal cases occur, it is essential that they are thoroughly investigated so patterns can be identified and future risks better understood. I am not claiming certainty about what caused my father’s death. But uncertainty should not be the final answer when someone dies suddenly from a condition that is known to have multiple potential triggers. Families deserve to know that cases like this are taken seriously and studied carefully. The importance of transparency public trust in healthcare and public health institutions depends on transparency. When people believe difficult questions are avoided rather than explored, trust erodes. Investigating rare but severe medical events is not anti-science. It is how science improves. Millions of Americans—including my family—made healthcare decisions based on guidance from public health authorities. Maintaining trust requires open investigation of serious adverse medical events and honest communication about what is known and what is still uncertain. I respectfully urge federal health agencies to: 1. Strengthen investigation protocols for severe or fatal myocarditis cases, particularly when they occur in individuals with no prior cardiac history. 2. Improve active surveillance systems that analyze patterns in cardiac events rather than relying only on passive reporting. 3. Expand research into immune-mediated myocarditis and sudden cardiac inflammation to better understand possible triggers. 4. Increase transparency in how myocarditis cases are tracked, analyzed, and communicated to the public. 5. Closing My father trusted the healthcare system. So did our family. Watching him die so suddenly from myocarditis, with no clear explanation, has left us grieving and searching for answers that may never come. If sharing his story encourages stronger investigation, greater transparency, or even one new piece of understanding about this condition, then perhaps something meaningful can come from the worst day of our lives. No daughter should have to spend the rest of her life wondering what truly happened to her father guessing if it was related to COVID vaccinations. Personally, I received the first initial COVID vaccinations administered in two different shots a couple weeks apart. Historically, I have also gotten vaccinated for Influenza because I have two young sons and I volunteer for hospice but since losing my father in the summer of 2025, I will always hesitate putting another vaccine into my body until I have a better understanding of what happened. Respectfully, Olivia Nicodemus

Title: Comment from Paul , Lindsay
Comment:
I am submitting this comment because my life was permanently altered after receiving a COVID-19 vaccine, and I believe the committee responsible for monitoring vaccine safety must hear directly from those living with the consequences.

Before vaccination, I was healthy, active, and had no history of systemic autoimmune disease, neurological degeneration, or immune deficiency. I trusted the public health recommendations given to Americans and believed I was doing the right thing for my health and for my community.

What followed changed my life.

After vaccination, my health began to deteriorate in ways that doctors initially struggled to explain. Over time, as more testing was performed and more specialists became involved, a disturbing pattern emerged. My body was no longer functioning the way it once had.

Since then, I have been diagnosed with a growing list of serious medical conditions affecting multiple body systems.

These include autoimmune disease, episodes of paralysis, progressive muscle degeneration with visible atrophy, a brain microadenoma, and immune dysfunction including immunoglobulin deficiency. I have also developed cardiac inflammation including Pericarditis with valve degeneration.

Additional diagnoses now include Hashimoto’s disease, Small Fiber Neuropathy, Crohn’s disease, Psoriasis, and Fibromyalgia.

The list continues to grow as physicians attempt to understand the cascade of immune and neurological dysfunction that has occurred.

These are not just diagnoses written in a medical chart.

This is watching my muscles waste away.
This is living with chronic pain and neurological symptoms that do not stop.
This is profound fatigue that makes even basic daily activities difficult.

My quality of life has been dramatically reduced.

The financial burden has been overwhelming. I have spent thousands of dollars pursuing answers through specialists, diagnostic testing, treatments, and medications—many of which are not covered by insurance. Like many individuals who believe they have experienced vaccine injury, I have been left to navigate this complex medical reality largely on my own.

But the most painful part has been the silence.

Too often, patients reporting serious post-vaccination complications are dismissed, ignored, or told their experiences are unrelated without meaningful investigation. When patients who trusted public health guidance are not acknowledged, trust in the system begins to erode.

Transparency does not weaken public health.
Ignoring injured patients does.

I am not sharing my experience to discourage vaccination. I am sharing it because individuals who experience serious adverse events deserve to be acknowledged, studied, and supported.

Public trust depends on honesty, transparency, and compassion for those who are harmed.

Behind every adverse event report is a real human being whose life has changed.

My life is one of them.

I urge this committee to expand research into post-vaccination immune and neurological disorders, strengthen surveillance for adverse events, and ensure that patients whose health has been permanently altered are not left without answers, support, or recognition.

The success of public health should never be measured only by the number of vaccines administered.

It should also be measured by how we care for the people who were harmed after trusting the system.

Dear Members of the ACIP,

Virtually all public health and medical professional societies have found the HHS Secretary’s steps gutting the ACIP reprehensible, and many other actions he has initiated to consistently be in the wrong direction. At the same time, he has betrayed efforts to protect Americans from known toxins (e.g., pesticide linked with carcinogenic effect). In the vaccine arena, actions forced on CDC have not been either transparent, nor do they approach anything near a “gold standard”. The actions have greatly diminished the American people’s trust in the CDC, just as it has in the FDA, NIH, and CMS. Medical and public health experts in a number of non-US countries have expressed incredulity at what the HHS Secretary has done.
While it may take generations for HHS and its agencies to recover their capabilities on behalf of this great Nation’s population, there are basic steps which can, and must be taken by the ACIP to ensure we move in the right direction.

These include, but are not limited to the below:

Issue 1: For agenda items, the input of CDC subject matter experts (SMEs), their concerns, and their analysis, must be solicited in a timely manner by the HHS and ACIP, with the findings of the SMEs shared with the ACIP and the HHS Secretary. The ACIP, to maintain credibility, must address concerns of the SMEs, as well as those of any public health and medical professional societies (e.g., CSTE, AAP, ACOG, etc.). Failure to address the concerns makes it appear that the ACIP operates not on formal, evidence & science-based analysis, but rather that it relies on “gut-level” ideas that have been foisted on the ACIP and CDC. Such foisting results in unsound, anecdotal ideas being used to guide taxpayer expenditures in a wasteful manner.
Issue: Failure to use the ACIP’s previously adopted Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation “GRADE” framework to make certain that discussions of vaccine recommendation evidence is of high quality, providing data-based evaluations of both the benefits, and any harms of vaccine(s).

Issue 2: Failure to use the ACIP’s Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework that provides an transparent process to analyze particular vaccine benefits, and potential harms, cost, assurances that it will provide fair/just benefit to populations with higher risk of disease due to multiple factors (e.g., socio-economic status, racial and ethnic makeup, etc.). Not having information on such multiple factors increases likelihood of wasteful, inefficient resource allocation and vaccine targeting.

Issue 3: ACIP, to assure unethical studies that hark back to the Tuskegee syphilis experimentation on blacks, must go on record to state any studies conducted by CDC, must incorporate CDC Investigational Review Board (IRB) approval, with the IRB study posted on the CDC web page in a searchable format. The recent proposal for a study to be conducted outside the United States on Hepatitis B vaccine, with one arm of the study population not receiving a birth dose of Hepatitis B vaccine was in violation of ethical guidelines, and severely set back CDC’s reputation, even though the decision to conduct the study was forced, in a non-consensual manner, upon CDC. The ACIP must go on record to state that, when a vaccine study is proposed in a non-US setting, the host nation partner(s) must also be provided a timely opportunity to review, and comment on the study proposal. This will improve transparency, and assure a return to the ethics expected of CDC, and will protect CDC from unsound, unethical studies, being forced, at significant reputational, as well as financial, cost to the United States.

Issue 4: Conflicts of Interest among membership of the ACIP needs to be better addressed for transparency to occur. It is important that any financial affiliations be fully disclosed by each member, along with the potential financial impact of their decision, in any direction, on any item that they vote on. This needs to include any benefit from existing legal suits, holdings in supplement / “alternative” / homeopathic / and allopathic entities, and any “speaker fees” and travel for organizations and commercial entities in the prior three years. This should be evident, and readily searchable, upon their appointment to the ACIP, and in advance of any meeting.

I very much look forward to seeing the ACIP make sincere efforts at its upcoming meeting to return to having a top-notch reputation. Your taking vigorous action can enable your recovery.

Sincerely,

Konrad E. Hayashi, MD, MPH & TM
Captain (R), Medical Corps, United States Navy
Decatur, Georgia 30033-4001

Title: Comment from CANrise19 Advocacy Society - Nonprofit Organization
Comment:
Submission to ACIP – Experiences of Vaccine-Injured Patients and Systemic Barriers to Recognition and Care (Canada)
Submitted by: Michelle Worton, Chair
Canadian Nonprofit Organization for COVID-19 Vaccine-Injured

This comment is submitted to inform ACIP about the real-world situation faced by individuals who developed serious and persistent health problems after COVID-19 vaccination, with a specific focus on the experience in Canada.

Our nonprofit organization represents individuals across a larger age demographic, although many of whom were previously healthy, often younger with a healthy lifestyle and strong immune system - mean age 47.61 years. They developed severe and often chronic conditions after vaccination. Their experiences highlight systemic gaps in safety monitoring, recognition, diagnosis, treatment and compensation for covid vaccine-related injuries. These issues are not unique to Canada and are increasingly reported by patient organizations under our organization Covid Vaccine Injury Alliance https://www.covidvaccineinjuryalliance.org and researchers worldwide.

Vaccination remains an important public health tool. However, ethical vaccination policy must also recognize and support the individuals who experience serious adverse outcomes. Ignoring these patients does not strengthen trust in vaccines — it undermines it. Transparent recognition, research and care for vaccine-injured individuals are essential both for justice and for maintaining confidence in public health programs. Vaccine programs rely on public trust.
Trust grows when safety monitoring is thorough and inclusive. When ACIP examines COVID vaccine injury data and patient experience, it reinforces an essential principle: safety systems must work for everyone. Reviewing difficult outcomes doesn’t weaken public health — it strengthens accountability. Safety surveillance must include those who experienced harm. Robust public health systems examine benefits, risks, and rare adverse events transparently. Patient engagement in these conversations is not oppositional — it is foundational to long-term trust in immunization programs. Inclusion strengthens credibility.
