Title: Comment from Hallowell, Kelly
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical physician, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Petti, Brian
Comment:
My name is Brian Petti from Mentor, Ohio. I have been married to my wife for 20 years and we are raising two young children. I was an exceptional technology entrepreneur in Northeast Ohio, developing custom software platforms that served thousands of K–12 schools, leading universities, and countless American businesses.

After receiving the COVID-19 vaccine, I developed severe neurological and systemic complications that left me physically and cognitively disabled and unable to continue the work I had spent my life building.

What I want this committee to understand is not just the injury, but what happened after.

Under the PREP Act, people like me are forced into the Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program, which requires claims to be filed within just one year of the injury. The reality is that my injury was so severe that for years I was not capable of navigating a complex federal claims process. For nearly four years I was dealing with debilitating health problems, cognitive impairment, and constant medical crises. During that time I could barely manage basic daily life, let alone somehow discover an obscure federal compensation program, decipher its complex requirements, and fight through the paperwork while my health and my life were falling apart.

By the time I was well enough to even learn the program existed and begin understanding my condition, the one year deadline had already passed.

So after losing my health, my career, and spending my life savings on healthcare, I was told I was too late.

There is another problem that must be addressed. Vaccine injury reporting relies heavily on physicians, yet many injured patients are never properly reported to federal safety systems. When doctors fail to document or report serious adverse events, those injuries effectively disappear from the data.

If public trust in vaccine policy is going to continue, we need stronger enforcement of adverse event reporting requirements and real accountability when those requirements are ignored.

A system that gives vaccine injured people one year to file while they are still trying to survive is not a safety net. It is a barrier.

I trusted my my government. I live with the consequences.

Secretary Kennedy, the public was asked to trust the system. Now the system must show it is worthy of that trust by standing with the people who were harmed.

She repeated “Nana Dada Nana Dada Nana Dada” without any meaning. She had diarrhea and constipation (almost potty trained prior), and would cry without reason. The pediatrician said she was fine and that she would catch up with her peers. But it made no sense. Why would she be progressing so well, then all of sudden loose skills? I repeatedly went to the pediatrician’s office, begging for help. I was finally told, “Mrs. Gutierrez, I cannot help you anymore.” I never went back.

I finally took Sarah to her original pediatrician and for her vaccines. The MD refused to vaccinate, and said once we got settled in Sacramento, CA, to get a new pediatrician, and a referral to a speech therapist. I didn’t question why he didn’t give either of my children their vaccines. Why? I don’t know. But I had a feeling he knew then what was driving Sarah’s behaviors - vaccine injury.

Once in Sacramento, the MD observed Sarah and refused giving vaccines. She referred Sarah to an Occupational Therapist. I have a feeling the MD knew vaccines injured Sarah, and didn't want to cause more injury to my daughter, as well as my younger son.

Sarah was diagnosed late 2003 with Autism. After a year, we started biomedical treatment. The MD said it was vaccine injury. We had lab work done for Sarah. After several blood, urine and fecal labs, we found our daughter was rot with fungus, elevated titers to Bordatella Pertussis (‘P’ in DTaP vaccine) and Rubella (‘R’ in MMR vaccine) Viruses, Mercury (500x more than should be), Aluminum, and Lead Toxicity. We saw immense die off of fungus after giving her anti-fungal treatment. We saw increased eye contact and some single words with anti-viral medication. We have been battling with her vaccine injury, ever since then.

Sarah is now 26 years old. She has splintered skills in reading and comprehension, her grade level is somewhere between 1st and 2nd grade. She does not socialize with her peers. Her language is not spontaneous. She cannot hold a conversation. She will never drive a car. She does not recognize a joke. She will never go to college. She will never have a boyfriend, get married, or have children. She can’t work. She doesn’t understand the concept of money. She has seizures, takes 3 different medications, and has a VNS (Vagus Nerve Stimulator) device implant.

We completely rearranged our lives for our disabled daughter. I could not work. Sarah was put in 14 different placements within 4 years of public school because no one setting worked. I finally faced facts and home schooled her. We struggled to pay bills. We were $40,000.00+ in debt supporting our immune compromised child. We routinely buy supplements to keep her healthy. Sarah sees a neurologist now, only because she has seizures. He does not believe in vaccine injury like most doctors I’ve seen, but I need him to help her with meds and her VNS device, so I deal with it. I do not trust the medical system.

Sarah needs around the clock care so she does not harm herself. She has no concept of strangers, or impending danger. She is fearless. She has walked up to strangers and hugged them. We have cameras all over the inside and outside of our house, and I have a monitor with visual of Sarah at all times. That monitor goes to bed with me on full volume. I am terrified she will eventually harm herself, because I cannot watch her every second of the day and night.

We CANNOT sit by and watch the further destruction of our future. The CDC has FRAUDULENTLY continued this facade for years. CDC and Merck Whistleblowers have come forward. VACCINES CAUSE AUTISM. We have no doubt in our minds. Sarah is our science. I regret EVERY DAY that I did not research vaccines prior to my children’s birth. I have DEEP DISGUST for the ACTIONS of the CDC! THE HOLOCAUST ON OUR FUTURE GENERATION MUST END AND HEALING MUST BEGIN!

Sincerely,
Michelle Gutierrez

Title: Comment from Moore, Christina
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199

March 12, 2026

Public Comment for the March 18-19, 2026, CDC ACIP Meeting

To the CDC Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices:

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose.

As a two-time cancer survivor with an autoimmune illness which evidence indicates can be caused by a virus, I value widespread vaccine access and clear messaging which encourages vaccine uptake. Making America healthy again depends not only on individual efforts but also on community care in order to protect each other. Even though my immune system may not be able to respond sufficiently to protect myself fully via vaccines, having family and friends around me stay up-to-date with their vaccines helps me stay healthy.

Today as I write this, I have never to my knowledge had COVID-19. Vaccines are not the only layer of protection we have used in this pandemic, but they have been an important one.We have discovered, through our deliberate efforts to avoid infections these last 6 years, how much healthier I could become with no diet or lifestyle changes except not getting sick with communicable illnesses several times a year.

My mother passed away in the fifth year of the pandemic, and she also never had COVID. Due to Alzheimer’s Disease, she could not make protective choices for herself, but because we knew her risk factors we, her caregivers, made sure that she and we stayed current on all our vaccines, including COVID vaccines.
Vaccinations decrease suffering not only from the acute illnesses they prevent, but from other long-term, very serious illnesses also caused by pathogens including COVID , Epstein-Barr virus , enteroviruses, Human papilloma virus (HBV), herpes zoster, and measles. Vaccines are increasingly demonstrated to decrease risk of Alzheimer’s disease , various cancers, heart disease , autoimmune illnesses , immunocompromise, and degenerative diseases like post-polio syndrome and long covid .
Long COVID has surpassed asthma as the most common chronic illness in children , and we have yet to see what health, economic, and national security effects that will have a decade or two or more farther on.
In light of all this evidence (and I’ve only scratched the surface), I respectfully ask the committee for the following:
1.We need population-wide access to updated COVID vaccines for people of all ages, regardless of medical conditions, at least once a year.

2.Americans need clear communication, based on the evidence, that COVID vaccines reduce the risk of Long COVID and severe disease for people of all ages.

3.Vaccine-recommendation frameworks must include strong ethical foundations and must make the best available disease prevention available to all Americans.

Vaccine availability and uptake is a matter of America’s national, economic, and health security. Please study and act in accordance with the preponderance of evidence and remove as many obstacles as possible to widespread vaccine access.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Title: Comment from Bell, Kendall
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Spaeth, Kristina
Comment: My name is Kristina Spaeth.  I, too, am an individual living with an ongoing condition brought on when I received a COVID-19 vaccination. I also live with the emotional consequences of having experienced a miscarriage one day after taking the vaccination.  I am sure the comments are full of many such individuals, who have been attempting to persevere in our lives and seek the truth about our condition, despite a complete lack of support and research from our health care institutions, providers, and government which was instrumental in mandating the vaccine and pushing for it broad acceptance in society.  Despite all of this I am not anti-vaccine; I believe that many of our traditional vaccines have been proven to save lives through collective immunity.  However where the government supports and encourages universal vaccination, it also has a role to play in the protection of its citizens via the following means: 1. Funding, publishing, and promoting research into every approved vaccine to understand potential harms to individuals; 2. Enhancing informed consent by making adverse reaction information easily accessible and requiring providers to inform individuals prior to offering the vaccine; 3. Supporting individuals who have been harmed by vaccines both by researching medical interventions to improve their injuries and supporting them with living expenses.  In particular, the COVID vaccines need to be added to the VICP program, and the timeline for filing must be extended.  Those of us who have battled even our immediate healthcare providers for recognition that our health condition is the result of vaccination ("but for" vaccination we would not have the health conditions we now suffer), deserve to have more time, now that research is continuing to unfold into these vaccines, to prove our case.

Title: Comment from Pledger, ChristinaP
Comment:
My name is ChristinaP Pledger. I am 49 years old. I am a mother of five children ages from 29 to 14 and I’m a grandmother of three beautiful little souls. I graduated from the licensed practical nursing program in Manitoba in 2000. I worked full-time as a nurse in acute care and long-term care home care and palliative care until 2016. In 2019 I returned to healthcare as a healthcare aid and in 2020. I began the reinstatement process and receive my student license again in March 2021. I had just entered into the bachelor psychiatric nursing program in March 2021. I have always been an advocate for vaccines. All of my children and myself have received all of our vaccinations including yearly flu shots. I was working on the front lines of Covid in 2020 and 2021. I was redeployed to the city an hour from my home to help on the front lines of code red. I actively listened to the daily Covid updates and when the vaccines were rolled out in March 2021, we were told we were mandated to receive these vaccines to protect ourselves and others from the deadly virus. I received my first vaccine on March 3 of 202. Almost immediately after my injection, I began to experience flu like symptoms. This has happened to me before after receiving the flu shot so I just rested waiting for the symptoms to pass. However, instead of passing they were sent and on March 8 my doctor gave me a two week sick note because I was to return to work and I was not better. On March 12, I was taking two emergency in the city by my supervisor because I was in such excruciating pain in my neck, head and body. My eyes were blurred and my vision was doubled and I was not able to drive myself. I asked the ER doctor if this could be a result of the vaccine because I’ve never experienced this before and she said she didn’t think that it was likely and only treated me for a migraine and sent me home. However, all of my symptoms continued to linger and get worse. I spoke with my family doctor on the phone asking if it was safe for me to take my booster and she said she thought there would be no problem. She just thought I was having a severe reaction to the first dose, but that it would pass. I began to develop severe neurological issues after my second dose on March 23, 2021. I began to experience facial drooping on my left side. My tongue felt like it was swollen and burning my swallowing became difficult. I was experiencing an extraordinary amount of pain from my head to the bottom of my feet. It felt as if someone was pouring hot oil over my entire body. I began to experience weakness from my soul of my feet to mid thighs around my torso, my shoulders, my upper back and my arms. I began to have lots of function in my legs and arms, and it was very difficult for me to grasp anything with my hands due to the severe pain. I was experiencing loss of bowel and bladder control, and I had to begin to wear a neck collar because the pain in my neck was so excruciating. It felt like I was being decapitated with a chainsaw. I was started on steroids in April as my PCP tried to figure out what was going on I began to use neck traction because we wondered if I had had some nerve compression. However, this did not explain the severity of my symptoms, and she began to wonder if this was actually a result of a Vaccine injury. I received an elective neck surgery on May 21, 2021, hoping that this may potentially help alleviate symptoms of nerve compression. The surgery was successful however I woke up with even more significant pain and disability. After multiple MRIs and neurological assessments, my surgeon, the radiologist and neurologist queried everything from transverse myelitis, MS, Sjorgan syndrome, autoimmune diseases, and they finally agreed that I had suffers a “very rare”spinal cord stroke. I was transferred to Saskatchewan to receive a second opinion. It was determined that I had suffered a severe and significant injury that resulted in my cord swelling causing a spinal cord injury which lead to cervical myopathy, Myelomalacia Transvere Myelitis an entire host of neurological issues. I was unable to walk or care for myself for the first three years, and My Husband had to quit his job and stay home to look after me. I ended up suffering a significant mental health issues and was started on electric shock therapy ketamine therapy, and hospitalization in a psychiatric ward.
I have never regained full function, and I am. I am permanently disabled. I am now dealing with early diagnosis of early dementia I have severe depression and PTSD. My husband and my children have also experienced depression PTSD & anxiety. My elderly parents had to return to work to help my family pay our bills. I was unable to care for my children, and they had to relocate to Saskatchewan to live with their father. Our family has lost every financial asset we have ever worked for familles like mine deserve justice. Canadian providers are too afraid to diagnose our injuries.

Title: Comment from Anonymous

Hello world, thank you for the opportunity to say what I would like to say.

I was a healthy middle aged man, exercised regularly, my life was good, simple and mostly happy. I never caught the coronavirus illness before getting the shots I received April through May 2021. I was told I may have irritation, dizziness and may need to rest following the vaccination and that was all the warning I received.
By October I lost the use of my leg, I could not walk for over a week, I crawled. When I could walk again it was very difficult, taking weeks to rehabilitate. Title: Comment from Johnson, Daniel
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Henderson County Department of Public Health
Comment: I have been a nurse for 24 years, 14 of those as a public health nurse. I have personally given tens of thousands of vaccines in my career. Although I realize that allergic reactions and severe adverse reactions do rarely occur, I have never had a patient have a severe anaphylactic reaction or one call us to report having a serious adverse reaction from a vaccine we administered. The idea that the vaccines we give children is too much for their immune systems is missing the point of it being a perfect example of medical advances we should be celebrating. In 1980, I was vaccinated for 7 diseases with vaccines that contained over 9,000 antigenic components. Now, or until this committee made their changes to the childhood schedule, kids were being vaccinated for 14 diseases (not including COVID and RSV) and received a grand total, all doses counted by their 1st birthday, of about 300 antigenic components. That means that not only are we able to protect kids from double the diseases, but now we are able to do this with less of a challenge to the immune system. This should be celebrated, but this committee has chosen to use their less than adequate training to determine that the safety studies relied upon by multiple developed countries for decades are not good enough. As these diseases continue to reemerge, everyone of the members of this committee will be responsible for every death caused by a vaccine-preventable disease.

Title: Comment from Alice, Margaret Anna
Comment:
Thank you for the opportunity to comment. I have been documenting COVID vaccine harms (https://vaxxharms.com) since 2021 and know many individuals who have suffered debilitating injuries, bereavement, and death. I am grateful to hear ACIP is taking a step toward acknowledging the suffering of Americans by adopting a new ICD medical code (T50.B25) specific to COVID vaccine injury. I urge the adoption of this code as well as a moratorium on COVID vaccines due to the astronomical number of injuries and deaths (1,674,311 reports to VAERS through January 30, 2026, including 39,025 deaths) and threat to the human genome (https://jpands.org/vol31no1/hulscher.pdf). Thank you for seeing, hearing, and helping the vaxx-injured.

"Lament of the Vaxx-Injured"
by Margaret Anna Alice

They told us it was safe.
They told us it was effective.
They told us everyone was doing it.
They told us not getting it was selfish.
They told us it was the only way
we could study, work, shop, live.

We listened to them.
Why would they lie to us?
We trusted them.
Why would they hurt us?

So we got it.

And every day since
that irrevocable,
life-shattering decision
has been unrelenting
agony and exhaustion,
apprehension and regret,
uncertainty and dread.

Our lives are now a continuum
of questioning,
of searching,
of testing,
of fretting.

They tell us we’re faking it.
They tell us we’re lying.
They tell us we’re imagining it.
They tell us we’re anxious.
They tell us we’re exaggerating.
They tell us we’re grifting.

And once it’s undeniable—
when the diagnostics show
heart damage,
blood clots,
myocarditis,
pericarditis,
cardiac arrest,
hematologic abnormalities,
impaired immune functionality,
shingles,
Bell’s palsy,
Guillain–Barré syndrome,
autoimmune disease,
multiple sclerosis,
transverse myelitis,
neuropathy,
paralysis,
multi-organ inflammation,
leprosy,
strokes,
dementia,
prion disease,
cerebrovascular disease,
brain damage,
tinnitus,
turbo cancer,
DNA integration,
deep vein thrombosis,
post-vaccination syndrome,
multisystem failure—
then they tell us
they’re baffled.
Then they tell us they don’t
know how to fix it.

Every day, we research
as if our lives depend on it,
because they do.
Because only we
and our loved ones
care enough to save us.

For some, we’re a statistic,
a number on a spreadsheet.

For others, we don’t exist.
They don’t want us to exist.

They refuse to see us.
They refuse to hear us.
They refuse to feel us.
They refuse to help us.

Every minute, we scrape together the strength
to endure the anguish,
to resist the gaslighting,
to seek out supplements,
procedures, practices—
anything that will offer relief,
anything that will soften the suffering,
anything that will delay our terminal diagnosis.

They tell us to shut up.
They tell us not to scare others.
They tell us it is rare.
They tell us it was worth it.

And no one takes responsibility—
not the NGOs that hocus-pocused us,
not the corporations that poisoned us,
not the governments that coerced us,
not the agencies that betrayed us,
not the institutions that mandated us,
not the philanthropaths who targeted us,
not the ideologues who ridiculed us,
not the tyrants who scapegoated us,
not the politicians who maneuvered us,
not the enforcers who penned us,
not the psychologists who manipulated us,
not the propagandists who petrified us,
not the experts who hoodwinked us,
not the scientists who head-faked us,
not the censors who erased us,
not the doctors who pushed us,
not the nurses who needled us,
not the pharmacists who stabbed us.

They call us anti-vaxxers.
But we got jabbed?
They call us conspiracy theorists.
But we believed them?
They call us science-deniers.
But we trusted it?

And now we’re on our own—
without support,
without compensation,
without legal recourse.

Some of us have lost
our jobs,
our insurance,
our babies,
our lives.

They don’t need us anymore.
They don’t want us anymore.

They got what they ordered
… and moved on.

Each twinge of pain
is silver in their pockets.
Each desperate scream
is Muzak in their waiting room.
Each vaxxicide
is another piece of evidence
buried.

So that’s why we have to live.
That’s why we have to survive.
That’s why we have to fight.
That’s why we have to wage justice.

We are living proof
of their fraud,
of their cruelty,
of their tyranny.

Chisel our truth into your hearts.
Tattoo our stories onto your arms.
Photograph our faces with your minds.

Testify to our torment.
Testify to their lies.
Testify to their crimes.

See us.
Hear us.
Feel us.
Help us.

• Original poem with links to supporting evidence: https://lamentofthevaxxinjured.com
• Reading by Cody Hudson: https://lamentvaxxinjured.com

Comment: In March of 2021, my life was destroyed forever by the mandated vaccine. I wrote love letters to my kids knowing I was dying. I lived, but suffer permanent nerve damage with a condition called trigeminal neuralgia, nicknamed the suicide disease. My children lost their father as i lost my ability to do many activities with them, my wife lost a husband who was able to provide for her & our kids in the way I dreamed of. Now I have no answers, no apologies, no research on how to heal neuralgia & nerve damage to my face. The physical pain I feel every moment is beyond heartbreaking to me & my family. I simply want us to be seen, researched, and provided some sort of help.

Title: Comment from Stiltner, Joyce
Comment: I took two of the Pfizer Covid shots in March 2021.  After taking the shots, my legs started cramping severely.  I stay in pain with the cramps every day.  I made numerous trips to the ER after the shots.  Local ER did not believe there was anything wrong with me.  I was not able to get out and go to the grocery store or any place without the cramping becoming more severe.  January 2022 I did get Covid and was hospitalized and treated like I had the plague.  The hospital treated me really bad.  By March 2022, My right leg and foot not only cramped badly but my foot started losing feeling in it and I couldn't walk.  I was take to Pikeville Medical Center ER and was immediately taken to ICU with blood clots in my right leg.  The doctor tried to dissolve the clots with medication which did not help.  Then doctor Adauci took me to surgery to surgically remove the clots.  I was in surgery for hours and he told me when he would try to remove the clots they would go right back into my leg.  That surgery did not get rid of the clots.  The doctor informed me that my leg would have to be removed from the knee in order to prevent the clots moving to my heart.  I feel like my life was ruined from the poison in the Pfizer Covid shots.  My life has not been the same since I took them.  This week makes four years without one leg and living in a wheelchair.  I feel that the shots were given to the public without proper testing.  In my case, I want Pfizer and the United States government to be charged for ruining my life.  I have talked to countless other people who took the vaccines and they have lost hearing, had heart issues, cancer, etc.  All of us were living a healthy life before being made to take the vaccines.  Dr Facci and all the drug companies made milllions of dollar with these vaccines.  So many deaths and health issues.  I WANT someone to pay for this.

Title: Comment from Franklin, Diana
Comment:
Maurice Franklin Died From the Moderna Covid Shot -

My husband, Maurice, had a pretty severe gut reaction after his 2nd shot. He seemed fine for about 6 months, then the gut issue returned. He developed several severe symptoms after that, which I learned after his death was Multi-system Inflammatory Response. He developed debilitating pain in his lower back, which spread up his back and down his legs. He slowly lost much of his hearing. He developed Chronic Fatigue, so it was difficult for him to leave the home. He had tinnitus, which got worse and had a several day episode of severe vertigo. His muscles began wasting away. I may have left something out, but the last day of his life, he suffered a seizure. In the hospital, they could see where he had suffered multiple mini-strokes, which probably explains why more and more in the last 2 months he just sat around doing less and less. They were taking him in for another MRI to check a suspicious spot in his brain, when he had a brain aneurysm and his head filled with blood. The surgeon said we could try to save him with surgery, but he would have to be in a nursing home the rest of his life barely able to function. He was 57 years old. Date of death August 8, 2022.
He got the shot to protect others and about 2 months before he died said he wished he had never gotten the shot.

Title: Comment from O'Mara, Mike
Comment:
I received the Moderna mRNA ‘vaccines’ the summer of 2021 - I got the first two ‘loading dose’ shots, no boosters. Within a few weeks after the second shot, a cascade of symptoms began which would go on to affect nearly every bodily system there is and lead me to what can only be described as a slowly progressive postvax wasting disease. I have since learned from many postvax victims that the second of the initial ‘loading doses’ was the beginning of their conditions as well.

I went from completely healthy, able to work 40-60 hours weekly plus do a daily jog and workout to utterly physically destroyed, all the muscle in my body dissolving off of me asymmetrically, with my entire left side of my body even worse than my right, as seen in ALS, MMN, inclusion body myositis and other neuromuscular diseases.

I lost my business, all work ability, all ability to continue hobbies / socializing, everything. I have not been outside my home save for my many hospital and private clinic visits or to let the dogs right outside or remove the trash to the curb for 4.5 years now. I still force myself to play my piano a few times a month, because it is all I have left of my former life as a professional composer / writer / teacher / performer, and I continue desperately and to this point in vain to ‘workout’ as much as my body still allows to try and maintain whatever little strength I have left. Other than that, I am trapped lying around in agony 24/7.

I fought desperately both in person and online to receive any kind of recognition and actual serious medical testing and potential treatment everywhere I could, and have been across the country to five different states attempting to receive adequate care. I have been through 10 neurology specialists and been seen at every major hospital that would take me. Mayo Clinic and Cleveland Clinic both rejected my case when one of my doctors made the mistake of noting my disease was ‘likely due to an autoimmune process post vaccination’ in a referral letter.

Thus far, the medical community has bestowed several diagnoses on me, including: ‘severe’ (their words) CIDP, major axonal polyneuropathy, small fiber neuropathy, and POTS. But they all agree that my atypical presentation does not ‘fit’ any of those diseases as they’d like it to - and so they just don’t know what I really have. There is no ‘official’ diagnosis for it. And thus, they won’t treat. And I continue to waste away with more and more muscular atrophy, pain and weakness.

Sadly, online I have found yet more isolation, mocking, slandering and hatred towards me. The large so-called ‘support groups’ reject me for speaking the unvarnished truth of my experience and having my own opinions. The online health gurus say ignorant things like I’m ‘not trying enough’ or keep preaching the same supplements, protocols and diets I have desperately tried for more than 3 years now to no avail. And certain members of the ‘vax injured family’ see it fit to slander and lie about me when they say they want to ‘lift everyone up’ and ‘share everyone’s story’.

I had an X account before with over 21K followers. They had it mass false reported and I lost the account with no appeal given. They shadow banned my original Facebook account; that platform won’t let me log back in though they have also never told me what ‘violation’ I might have done, if any. The trolls found everywhere I have ever been online and got me banned / shunned / mocked.

I have had cops sent to my door under false pretenses in an attempt to get me killed. They have sent suspicious packages to my home, including a book on how to kill myself. My partner’s private social media photos were taken and shared around. They found every single person they could - people who haven’t seen nor shared as much as a word with me in over 5, 7 even 10 years to give them supposed ‘dirt’ on me so they could slander me online. And on and on.

Now, I get next to zero traction in spreading my story and attempting to get help. No one reposts or engages save those who wish to further tear me down, mock and slander me.

I want to keep fighting, but without support leading towards any serious medical assistance I see less and less of a reason to do so with every passing day as I descend further and further into disability and towards an early end.

I have shared medical evidence of all I have stated above time and time again. I am selling nothing; I gain nothing from ‘lying’ and have no reason to lie as telling the truth has cost me my family and all of my former friends for political reasons based around something that should never be political - a human life and one’s health.

I only want a chance to be seen and heard.

- Mike O’Mara

Title: Comment from Bidwell, Joe
Comment:
Dear Committee Members & CDC Staff,

In March of 2021, I received two doses of Pfizer’s Covid-19 vaccine. Following both doses, I experienced pressure-induced pain radiating down my inoculated arm; with the pain being more severe and longer lasting after the second dose. Over the next few months, I lost range of motion in both shoulders, and was eventually diagnosed with bilateral frozen shoulder. Fortunately, several months of physical therapy seemed to resolve this affliction.

Unfortunately, in July of 2021, I coughed up a blood clot, and a CT scan revealed multiple nodules in both lungs. Over the next year or so, I had two bronchoscopies, a needle biopsy, thorascopic lung surgery, several additional CT scans, and a plethora of blood tests. Despite ambiguous findings, and the absence of customary symptoms, I was ultimately diagnosed with invasive pulmonary fungal disease. I underwent two courses of oral anti-fungal medication; six months on one drug, a few months off, then a year on another. Follow-up imaging seems to indicate that the latter treatment successfully eradicated the infection.

The bad news, however, is that an explanation for why I got this disease in the first place, along with a prediction about the likelihood of future recurrences, remain doggedly elusive. Invasive fungal infections are rarely seen in those who have a healthy immune system. And in spite of their best efforts, including whole genome sequencing via an NIH research study, my doctors have failed to find in me any significant immunodeficiencies. They do not deny the suspicious timing between my Covid vaccination and the discovery of pulmonary nodules, but they do tend to dismiss that reality as mere coincidence. Needless to say, I am unconvinced.

In May of 2020, I wrote about the unintended consequences of our response to the pandemic: “Every human intervention comes with a cost. And too often, we leap into action without considering the nature of that price or whether it is worth paying. This is partly due to our overly exuberant faith in the power of smart people and smart ideas. It is also, however, the product of our tendency to value some siblings more than others.”

I do not doubt that the Covid vaccines helped many. But I am equally certain that they harmed others. Under normal circumstances, such outcomes would be unsurprising. Clearly, however, we have been living through abnormal times. Some have demonized the vaccines; and some have divinized them. Unfortunately, the smart people who wield the majority of power in our nation fell into the latter camp, and so the vaccine injured have been left out in the cold to fend for themselves, like lepers.

One of the few doctors courageous enough to challenge the prevailing wisdom noted that she was not alone in seeing the problem, but that some of her colleagues were “willing to accept these vaccine injuries as unavoidable collateral damage in a mass vaccination program.” [1] Yes, any new technology, particularly a medical intervention, will inevitably produce collateral damage. But common decency demands that such harm be acknowledged, mourned, and redressed. To our great shame, we stubbornly refuse to love our wounded siblings, adding insult to their ignored injury.

Please understand, I appreciate my doctors. And maybe they are right; maybe my situation was just a tragically absurd coincidence. Then again, maybe the vaccine did disrupt my immune system in some unusual manner. The uncomfortable truth is that neither of us really know which version is correct. But it will never cease to frustrate me that so many of them, along with the system to which they belong, would prefer to conjure up a magical fungus fairy in order to avoid even contemplating an investigation into the inconvenient possibility of the vaccine’s involvement. That is faith, not science, and certainly not good medicine.

Thank you for considering my story.

[1] Letter from Dr. Patricia Lee to Dr. Peter Marks and Dr. Tom Shimabukuro, 9/28/2021; published online by Sen. Ron Johnson.

Title: Comment from Nayak, Jennifer
Comment:
As a pediatric infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Pfenning, Leonore
Comment:
Good Morning, my name is Leonore Pfenning and I appreciate the opportunity given me to talk about my son, Alex.
When I found out I was pregnant I did everything I could to give him the best start in life. I ate well, didn't drink alcohol, exercised, did Tai Chi, meditated and maintained good thoughts and attitude.
When he was born in September of 1988 he was perfect. He was breast fed until 6 months of age and hit all his wellness check milestones. He was a happy healthy child.
I'm not quite sure when the ear infections started but he started having them one after another. Alex was on several different antibiotics in his first few years to combat these constant ear infections. At some point during this time he also started crying or screaming this high pitched scream at times which was unusual for him, definitely out of the norm and it was very difficult to calm him down.
It wasn't until I naively and trustingly handed my son over to the pediatrician for another set of vaccinations at about the age of 3 which included his DTap that he stopped talking. I don't remember him having a fever or other obvious neurological issues but at the time he had a wide vocabulary for a 3 year old. Within a couple of days after his immunizations he just started grunting and pointing, not using his words. Since the only thing that had happened to him was getting vaccinated I went back to the Pediatricians office to let them know what was going on and question whether this could be because of the vaccine. I remember to this day the Pediatrician telling me it wasn't possible the vaccines could have taken my son's language away. He didn't say they were safe and effective but it wasn't possible for any ill effects from being vaccinated. It must be something else. And I believed him.
We took Alex to a Speech specialist but it wasn't until he was near 4 years of age that he started getting his words back. Also during this time as much as we worked with him, he was still wearing diapers until he was close to 4 years of age.
I just want to summarize his life after this:
He was diagnosed with ADD in kindergarten and put on Ritalin
He was diagnosed with Aspergers as an adolescent.
Neurologically he had no filters, and had difficulty with relationships and change to his routine.
He ended up taking his own life at 36 years of age.

Title: Comment from Holland, Nikki
Comment:
See attached file: My Video Story and a Thank You to the ACIP Committee!

My name is Nikki Holland. For the past five years I have been living with serious medical complications that began after my COVID-19 vaccination. First, I want to sincerely thank the ACIP Committee for bringing the topic of COVID-19 vaccine injury to the table at your upcoming meeting. For those of us living with these injuries, simply acknowledging and discussing this issue gives us hope that it may lead to greater understanding, research, and eventually better treatment and support.
I have attached a short two-minute video (had to be embedded in a ppt file to submit) that shares parts of my personal story through photos and videos from what happened after my COVID vaccination and a Thank you to the ACIP Committee for bringing the topic of Vaccine Injury to the table! You can also watch at the video directly on this link where I uploaded it to make it easier to view:

https://youtube.com/shorts/S_XaYA_UZIE?feature=share

I hope you will take a moment to watch it and remember my story and the stories of others like me as you begin these discussions. Here is my written story with more detail to accompany:

In 2021, about 36 hours after my second Moderna mRNA COVID-19 vaccine, I developed severe nausea, vomiting, and worsening shortness of breath. Five days after my COVID Vaccine I was hospitalized with a respiratory failure and severe multisystem inflammatory response that caused my body to begin attacking itself. Exactly one week after the vaccine, I had to be placed on a ventilator to breathe. That was the first of five times I required a ventilator over a two-month period. During that time I was in and out of the hospital, transported by life flight four times, and treated at six hospitals across two states. I spent a month in inpatient rehabilitation to regain basic strength, followed by months of home health therapy and outpatient pulmonary rehabilitation.

My life has changed dramatically. I required a tracheostomy, a feeding tube for several months, and multiple implanted medical devices and procedures just to help my body perform basic functions. I went from taking three medications to more than twenty. Before the COVID Vaccine I was an active single mom of 3, someone who ran 5Ks and exercised regularly, and was working full-time as a Physical Therapist. After my COVID Vaccine I struggled with significant physical limitations requiring assistive devices or wheelchair for mobility, supplemental oxygen at times, use of a ventilator at night, and continue to live with a tracheostomy.

Since the COVID Vaccine I have gained multiple medical diagnoses from the medical impairments that have arose, including: respiratory failure, gastroparesis, chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP), POTS, deep vein thrombosis, neurogenic bladder requiring surgical interventions, systemic/multisystem inflammatory response syndrome, laryngospasms, and other significant neurological and muscular impairments. Almost overnight, I went from a healthy, active professional and mother to fighting simply to stay alive. I lost my career and much of my independence, and I have faced overwhelming medical and financial challenges while still trying to care for my children. I have improved in some ways over time, but I have also had to grieve the loss of the life I once had.

That is why I want to thank you again for being willing to discuss vaccine injury. For those of us living this reality, acknowledgement and thoughtful investigation are essential steps toward opening doors to more research, understanding, better care, and preventing others from falling through the cracks and ongoing safety of future vaccines. Please discuss more research, more treatment, more resources for help, and more acknowledgment with informative recommendations to the public and medical community on vaccine injury. Thank you for taking the time to listen and bring the topic of Vaccine Injury to the table at your ACIP Meeting!

-Nikki

Title: Comment from Tobin, Nicole
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Colan, Cheryl
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199
Public Comment for March 18–19 CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To the Members of the CDC ACIP Committee,

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose.

I am writing as a caregiver for an older family member and as someone who relies on clear, evidence-based public health guidance. When you are responsible for helping another person stay healthy and safe, vaccine policy is not an abstract issue. It directly affects the decisions families and caregivers make about risk, prevention, and medical care.

Vaccines remain one of the most effective tools we have to prevent serious illness. At the same time, vaccination rates for several diseases are declining, and outbreaks of vaccine-preventable illnesses such as measles, pertussis, and COVID-19 continue to occur across the United States. In a country with the scientific capacity and resources of ours, people, especially children, older adults, and medically vulnerable individuals, should not be suffering or dying from diseases we know how to prevent.

I am concerned about policies that limit access to COVID vaccines or create additional barriers to vaccination. Restrictive recommendations can unintentionally create confusion among patients, caregivers, and healthcare providers about whether vaccines remain necessary or effective. Clear and consistent guidance from ACIP is essential for maintaining public trust and helping clinicians communicate effectively with patients.

The scientific evidence continues to show that updated COVID vaccines reduce the risk of severe illness, hospitalization, disability, and death. Broad and equitable access remains particularly important for those at higher risk, including older adults, pregnant people, children, and individuals with underlying health conditions.

Long COVID also remains a serious public health concern affecting millions of Americans. Preventing infection through vaccination remains one of the most important available tools for reducing the risk of long-term health consequences.

I respectfully urge the ACIP committee to continue following the scientific evidence and to maintain clear, strong recommendations that support broad access to updated COVID vaccines. Caregivers, patients, and clinicians depend on ACIP’s guidance to make informed decisions that protect health and save lives.

Thank you for your consideration.

Respectfully,
Cheryl Colan

Title: Comment from Woods, Julie
Comment: My name is Julie Woods, and I am writing in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199. I am covid vaccine injured and co-founder of the CANrise19 Advocacy Society (CANrise19) living in Gananoque, Ontario, Canada. I began experiencing numbness, tingling, and pain. My heart started racing, and my brain didn’t work the same. I got extremely fatigued and had no energy. I could no longer walk up and down the side walk, let alone play sports. And as my symptoms progressed, I was unable to keep up at work or with my studies. I had to give up my job, transitioning to 1 day a week working community outreach at a local History Museum after I’d previously worked there as a summer student. I also had to drop down to a reduced course load for students with disabilities. My health declines have had me take multiple leaves of absence, but I am slowly working my way through it with the goal of supporting others like me in placement and beyond. Like many in our country, my condition continues to deteriorate due to lack of access to appropriate medical care. In 2023, I was lucky enough to find React19, a US advocacy group, and was blessed to be a member of their pilot emotional support group. Following the group, I became their volunteer support group coordinator. In October of 2024, React connected me with Dr. Doug Jones in Utah, who diagnosed me with small fiber neuropathy, postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, micro clotting, mitochondrial dysfunction, and insulin resistance. When I came home and shared my story for the first time publicly on social media, I became connected with Michelle Worton, and together, we co-founded CANrise19. We are a member of the COVID Vaccine Injury Alliance and a not for profit assisting those across Canada impacted by COVID Vaccine injury. We aim to do so on physical, emotional, and financial levels by maintaining a private provider list, facilitating emotional support groups, and developing a medical care fund. As of March 10, 2026, CANrise19 represents 161 patients and 32 loved ones, across 9 of 10 provinces. Of the 32 loved ones, five have reported their loved one died because of their injury. We have 1287 followers on Facebook and 191 followers on our Instagram account. A total of 44 members have shared their injury stories on the CANrise19 website, with an additional 3 individuals sharing the testimony of a loved one who passed. Between September 1, 2025 and March 10, 2026, 74 members have registered their injuries with CANrise19. The respondents provided statistics such as their location, age of injury (N = 72 Respondents, Mean = 47.61 Years Old, Range = 16 to 73 Years Old), and the number of vaccinations received (N = 74 Respondents, Mean = 2.03 Vaccines, Range = 1 to 6 Vaccines). Additional statistics pertaining to the vaccine manufacturers, LOT numbers, and the respondents' status within the Vaccine Injury Support Program/Vaccine Injury Compensation program can be seen within CANrise19's organizational submission. We hope that ACIP's consideration of patient perspectives and the experiences of those whose lives were forever changed by vaccine injuries worldwide, will encourage collaborative and impactful dialogue. We aspire to provide a platform and a voice for critical discussions surrounding how recommendation guidelines, research priorities, and post-adverse event care affect real people, each and every day, because together, we CAN and we will RISE above all vaccine injuries.

Title: Comment from Tobin, Nicole
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Harder-Chancellor, Hannah
Comment: Hi My name is Hannah Harder. In March of 2021 I went to be an early adopter and received one dose of Pfizer Covid 19 vaccine. Within 45 minutes I would begin having a very severe multisystem reaction that went on for days, but I believe the neurological response still continues. I have had a very hard time getting physicians to fully engage or diagnose everything that happened to me. One allergist thinks I may have had a pseudoallergic reaction and another immunology specialist thinks I may have had CARPA Compliment Activated Reactive Pseudoallergy. Initially I was also placed on a steroid but couldn’t handle it. My face was trying to swell through five days of around the clock multiple types of above label antihistamines. I have never felt more like I was going to die. I had chest pain, migrating joint pain. And then for six weeks a headache so severe I couldn’t turn my neck and was worse then malaria or a concussion. My balance was severely compromised, my eyes lost the ability to team or focus, I thought it would affect my ability to walk. I was severely affected by noise, developed a dysautonomia, lost strength and ability in some cervical and the radial nerve. I was severely affected by light and sound and spent most of the first six weeks in bed. I have since learned I have a complement C5 deficiency and am suspected to have idiopathic angioedema. After this reaction my chemical allergies became severe (esp to the ingredient PEG which is in the vaccine and closely related chemicals like polypropylene glycol) and debilitating. Since that time I also feel that it caused a chronic reoccurring occipital neuralgia severe headaches that I never had before. I have been to at least nine specialists and have had a very difficult time getting this vaccine injury fully treated. What happened to me was severe and while I am provaccine and understand their importance, I think reactions like this should prove that no medication, including vaccines should be mandated because sideeffects can be life altering and sometimes long term. I would encourage more investigation into neurological responses, immune or genetic conditions that might predispose someone to more severe response, treatments and treatment pathways for those affected, and additional investigations into immune responses to both polyethylene glycols and polysorbates. The FDA also needs to not allow medications to be exempt from damages or injury.

There were times I was directly refused care, with physicians outrightly saying that since the mechanisms for many adverse reactions were unknown, they could not provide treatment. Since the introduction of the covid vaccines, few scientific studies have been conducted to determine the mechanisms and factors that may contribute to many covid vaccine injuries. Scientific investigations on covid vaccine injuries have not been a priority among decision makers, and few federal resources have been allocated to date. Yet the merits of investing in rigorous research are considerable. Findings have the potential to aid the recoveries of those injured as well as increase the public’s trust in covid vaccines. Title: Comment from Weid , N
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199

Public Comment for March 18 to 19 CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose.

I am a patient deeply worried about recent harmful and unwarranted changes on vaccine policy at the CDC and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition. This is a vital issue to me because of the following below.

Today, I am writing to express grave concerns of the future of COVID vaccines and Long COVID.

Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines. Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection. It continues to be debilitating condition that affects people’s health.

RFK Jr., Marty Makary, and Vinay Prasad have previously imposed new policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will without doubt harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone. The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines. Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, and are especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions especially under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. Long COVID is a debilitating condition that affects millions of Americans. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop this serious condition. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year (due to their rapid waning), for all COVID vaccine formulations. I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID either by preventing it or developing effective treatments. I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using open and transparent processes, all of which are hallmarks of democracy.

Thank you for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Pelczarski, Sheila

I did not take any COVID-19 shots because I was injured by a Tetanus shot at age 9 and a few other shots in subsequent years. All of these people were otherwise healthy and active before receiving the COVID shots. They are listed in chronological order of their deaths below.
1) A 31-year-old healthy construction worker who died the next day after being forced to get the injection to keep his job in the Fall of 2021.
2) A coworker at my former place of employment, a 50-something year-old male with a physically active job.
3) My ski club friend, a female athlete in her early 60's, had gotten at least the first two shots and a booster. She collapsed while packing for a ski trip in January 2022. According to the coroner, her heart was in fine shape, yet she somehow died of a "cardiac event."
