Title: Comment from Association of Women's Health, Obstetric and Neonatal Nurses (AWHONN)
Organization: Association of Women's Health, Obstetric and Neonatal Nurses (AWHONN)
Comment:
Maternal immunization continues to be the most effective way to reduce maternal, fetal, and infant
complications from vaccine-preventable diseases, including COVID-19, flu, pertussis, RSV, and Hepatitis B.
Protecting and expanding evidence-based vaccine recommendations during pregnancy and the postpartum
period helps protect pregnant patients, their infants, their families, and their communities. The Advisory
Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) has a history of and has been instrumental in providing
evidence-based recommendations and guidance on vaccination during pregnancy, which the Maternal
Immunization Task Force that AWHONN is part of, and other health care organizations use to inform clinical guidance for their
members and patients.
As a nursing professional organization representing nurses who care for pregnant patients, the Maternal
Immunization Task Force strongly reaffirms the importance of recommending and advocating for all routinely
recommended maternal vaccines and the hepatitis B vaccine at birth for all newborns. The proven safety and
efficacy of these vaccines have led to their recommendation during pregnancy and after birth. Yet, the
continued decline in vaccination rates among this population highlights a critical need: a renewed
commitment to educating the public and patients on the benefits of vaccination and removing barriers to
access.
The health and safety of our patients rely on our ability to provide the most up-to-date and clinically accurate
information on disease prevention strategies. The Maternal Immunization Task Force strongly urges the ACIP
to continue to review clinical data and disseminate information regarding maternal immunization and
immunization for infants, which is grounded in scientific rigor and supported by medical evidence. This
allows for shared decision making guided by evidence-based information between the clinician and the
patients and their families. It is important to note that routine vaccine recommendations do not negate the need for shared decision
making. This process is critical for pregnant patients considering health decisions that may affect them and
their infants. Shared decision making in maternal immunization can improve vaccine confidence, address
vaccine risk and benefit perceptions, and build trust in evidence-based medicine.
Vaccines are the most efficacious public health tool available in preventing infectious diseases and their
related adverse maternal, fetal, and infant health outcomes. AWHONN will continue to protect and uphold
our recommendations for routine maternal and infant immunization that are grounded in rigorous review of scientific evidence.

Title: Comment from Simonton, Angela

Title: Comment from Ruffalo, Michelle

I was running in 5ks half marathons prior to the Covid vaccine and now I walk slowly and painfully. My quality of life has greatly diminished from this biggest crime against humanity ever committed.

Michelle Ruffalo Kearns
2nd Grade Teacher

Title: Comment from Physicians for Informed Consent
Comment:
Request for Review of Assumptions in the Mitkus Aluminum Pharmacokinetic Model

Comment on Docket No. CDC-2026-0199

The Federal Register notice for this meeting indicates that ACIP will discuss vaccine injuries and the methodology used for vaccine recommendations. As aluminum adjuvants are widely used in routine childhood vaccines, the pharmacokinetic assumptions underlying aluminum safety assessments are directly relevant to these discussions.

A key analysis frequently cited in discussions of aluminum safety is the FDA-authored modeling paper by Mitkus et al., "Updated aluminum pharmacokinetics following infant exposures through diet and vaccination" (Vaccine, 2011). This model compares aluminum exposure from vaccines in infants with the minimal risk level (MRL) for aluminum established by the Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry (ATSDR).

However, the Mitkus model appears to apply an oral aluminum absorption assumption of 0.78%, whereas the ATSDR’s 2008 derivation of the aluminum MRL used an absorption assumption of approximately 0.1%. Using the larger absorption value when constructing the comparison curve effectively increases the modeled safety threshold by approximately 7.8-fold, potentially altering the relationship between modeled infant aluminum body burden and the regulatory benchmark.

Because the Mitkus model is widely cited in policy discussions concerning aluminum adjuvants, it may be valuable for ACIP or CDC to request an independent review of the modeling assumptions used in that analysis. Clarifying whether the assumptions used in the model are consistent with those used to derive the ATSDR minimal risk level would strengthen the scientific foundation for ongoing vaccine safety discussions.

Educational summaries of aluminum exposure in vaccines have been compiled by Physicians for Informed Consent, including the document “Aluminum in Vaccines: What Parents Need to Know” available at https://physiciansforinformedconsent.org/aluminum-in-vaccines, and the book "Vaccines and the Diseases They Target: An Analysis of Vaccine Safety and Epidemiology."

Supporting materials attached:

1. Mitkus et al. (2011) – the FDA-authored pharmacokinetic modeling paper referenced above.

2. March 9, 2020 letter to Dr. Gregory Poland (Editor-in-Chief of the journal Vaccine) describing the apparent discrepancy between the aluminum absorption assumption used in the Mitkus modeling and the absorption assumption used to derive the ATSDR aluminum minimal risk level, and requesting review of the issue as a potential erratum.

Thank you for considering this request.

Respectfully,
Shira Miller, M.D.
Founder and President
Physicians for Informed Consent
Newport Beach, CA

Title: Comment from McKinnell, Maxine
Comment:
To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose.

I am a concerned American, and family member of immunocompromised individuals, and am deeply worried about recent harmful and unwarranted changes on vaccine policy at the CDC and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition. This is a vital issue to me because of the following below.

Today, I am writing to express grave concerns of the future of COVID vaccines and Long COVID.

Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines. Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection. It continues to be debilitating condition that affects people’s health.

RFK Jr., Marty Makary, and Vinay Prasad have previously imposed new policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will without doubt harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone. The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines. Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, and are especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions especially under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. Long COVID is a debilitating condition that affects millions of Americans, including many of my friends and family. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop this serious condition. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year (due to their rapid waning), for all COVID vaccine formulations. I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID either by preventing it or developing effective treatments. I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using open and transparent processes, all of which are hallmarks of democracy.

Thank you for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Stephenson, Joan
Comment:
To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I am a longtime medical writer and editor, with a PhD in biology and decades of experience in communicating the findings of scientific research to the medical community and the general public. I have no conflicts to disclose. I am deeply concerned about recent changes to vaccine policy at the CDC, as well as the future direction of efforts to prevent and treat post-acute sequelae of COVID-19, commonly known as “Long COVID.”
Among my concerns are the low vaccination rates for several infectious diseases, and that many communities in the United States are experiencing outbreaks of vaccine-preventable illnesses such as measles, pertussis, and COVID-19. These infections can cause severe and sometimes lifelong health consequences and disrupt lives. Notably, in 2026, children should not die or suffer permanent disability from diseases that can be prevented with safe and affordable vaccines, which are one of the most significant advances in the history of public health and medical science. At the same time, Long COVID continues to affect millions of Americans and remains a debilitating condition that significantly affects people’s health and quality of life. In addition, these concerning developments pose a risk to the security of Americans.
Recent policies associated with Robert F. Kennedy Jr., Dr, Marty Makary, and Dr. Vinay Prasad that limit access to COVID vaccines are deeply troubling. As many medical experts have noted, restricting access to updated vaccines is likely to harm the health of Americans. COVID vaccines should remain widely available and free to all. Scientific evidence clearly shows that vaccination within the past year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe illness, disability, and death across all age groups.
I urge the ACIP to follow the scientific evidence, restore universal COVID vaccination recommendations, and ensure that vaccines remain accessible to everyone. I also urge continued scientific oversight and investment in preventing and treating Long COVID. Decisions about vaccine policy should be made through open, transparent, and evidence-based processes.
Thank you for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Anonymous
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199

Public Comment for March 18 to 19 CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose.

I am a disabled American who is deeply worried about recent harmful and unwarranted changes on vaccine policy at the CDC and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition. This is a vital issue to me because I am high-risk for complications from COVID, and I care about the well-being of everyone--this virus harms everyone. Public health is necessary to guard and protect for all!

Today, I am writing to express grave concerns of the future of COVID vaccines and Long COVID.

Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines. Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection. It continues to be debilitating condition that affects people’s health.

RFK Jr., Marty Makary, and Vinay Prasad have previously imposed new policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will without doubt harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone. The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines. Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, and are especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions especially under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. Long COVID is a debilitating condition that affects millions of Americans. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop this serious condition. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year (due to their rapid waning), for all COVID vaccine formulations. I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID either by preventing it or developing effective treatments. I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using open and transparent processes, all of which are hallmarks of democracy.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,
Concerned American

Title: Comment from Pierone, Gerald
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Gonzales Jr., Frank
Comment:
COVID Vaxxed 200 Times More Likely to Die from Brain Clots
Hunter Fielding June 28, 2024
A disturbing new study has revealed that those injected with COVID mRNA shots are 200 times more likely to die from a blood clot in the brain, official government data confirms.
According to a study by Dr. Peter McCullough and his team, COVID injections had an over 1,000-fold increased risk of blood clots in the brain compared to the flu vaccine.
The shots also have more than a 200-fold increased risk compared to all other vaccines.
The COVID jabs carry a much higher risk of blood clots in the brain compared with other vaccines, according to a new study by Dr. Peter McCullough and colleagues.
The researchers looked at reports in the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention’s (CDC) Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS) from January 1990 through December 2023.
They counted the number of cerebral thromboembolism events — as in, blood clots of the brain’s veins or arteries — reported in people who received a COVID-19 shot compared with those who received a flu shot or other vaccines.
Blood clots that block blood flow to the brain account for roughly 87% of all strokes, according to the American Stroke Association, a division of the American Heart Association.
McCullough — a cardiologist with over 1,000 publications and over 685 citations in the National Library of Medicine — warns that the study showed “an unacceptable risk of catastrophic thrombotic injuries to the brain” in individuals who took one or more COVID-19 shots.
McCullough summarized the study’s results on Substack:
“Compared to influenza vaccines given over 34 years, COVID-19 vaccines in 36 months of use had over 1000-fold increased risk of most blood clot events, and compared to all vaccines combined administered over 34 years, this risk remained at over 200-times greater with COVID-19 vaccination.”
Prior research has suggested that the spike protein in both the SARS-CoV-2 virus and the COVID-19 vaccine can cause serious blood clotting, McCullough and his co-authors noted in their report.
McCullough warns that blood clots in the brain are like a ticking time bomb that is difficult to detect or treat and “oftentimes leave patients with devastating disabilities.”
“This and other sources of data,” he added, “support calls to remove all COVID-19 vaccines from public use.”
The study — co-authored with Claire Rogers, Dr. James Thorp and Kirstin Cosgrove — is under peer review and available online as a preprint.
Women especially at risk
For the study, McCullough and his co-authors used 12 search terms to find VAERS reports of adverse events related to blood clots in the brain: cavernous sinus thrombosis, cerebral artery thrombosis, cerebral infarction, cerebral thrombosis, cerebral venous sinus thrombosis, cerebral venous thrombosis, embolic cerebral infarction, ischaemic cerebral infarction, sigmoid sinus thrombosis, superior sagittal sinus thrombosis, thrombotic cerebral infarction and transverse sinus thrombosis.
In the three years since the COVID-19 vaccine rollout, they found 5,137 reports of a brain blood clot event in those who received one or more COVID-19 shots.
Meanwhile, VAERS data showed only 52 reports since 1990 of a brain blood clot event following a flu vaccine and 282 since 1990 following all other vaccinations, they said.
The actual number of events may be higher due to underreporting in VAERS, they said, for several reasons.
First, CDC staff may have “tremendous difficulty” in processing the reports due to an overwhelming increase in VAERS reports since the COVID-19 vaccine rollout.
Also, clinicians may not have the necessary awareness and knowledge to recognize injuries as being vaccine-related and thus may not report them in VAERS.
Moreover, they added, “The VAERS database is well known for its difficulty in entering events, making it even more challenging for healthcare workers to submit each event with limited time in a hospital or clinic setting.”
The authors said their findings are especially concerning for women of reproductive age — who are particularly at risk for certain blood clotting events in the brain, such as cerebral venous thrombosis.
The American Heart Association said in a Jan. 29 review article that roughly two-thirds of all cerebral venous thrombosis cases occurred in women of reproductive age.
Given this reality, the study’s authors called for “an immediate global moratorium on the use of COVID-19 vaccines … with an absolute contraindication in women of reproductive age.”

Title: Comment from Coderre, J.
Comment:
Thank you to the CDC and the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) for reviewing vaccine safety.

Many vaccine-injured patients describe symptom patterns consistent with Small fiber neuropathy and dysautonomia. These patients often report persistent complaints affecting multiple body systems. This broad pattern is consistent with disorders affecting small sensory and autonomic nerve fibers.

Because these conditions require specialized testing not readily available such as skin biopsy or autonomic evaluation, many remain undiagnosed. Greater focus on testing for small fiber neuropathy and autonomic dysfunction may help identify the underlying cause and guide appropriate treatment.

Case summary: Previously healthy 50-year-old individual with no prior neurological or autoimmune disease developed new-onset neuropathic and autonomic symptoms approximately 10 days after AstraZeneca COVID-19 vaccination, later confirmed by biopsy-proven Small fiber neuropathy and subsequent diagnosis of Sjögren’s syndrome.

Prior to vaccination I was healthy and had no history of neurological disease, autoimmune disease, chronic neuropathic pain, or autonomic dysfunction. I had never experienced symptoms such as burning skin pain, numbness, dysautonomia, tinnitus, or persistent fatigue.

I received the AstraZeneca COVID-19 vaccine on April 20, 2021. Approximately 10 days after vaccination, I developed neurological symptoms including burning skin pain, numbness, tingling, and autonomic disturbances such as abnormal sweating and postural tachycardia.

These symptoms persisted and led to extensive medical evaluation. In May 2022, a skin biopsy confirmed small fiber neuropathy, demonstrating reduced intraepidermal nerve fiber density (IENFD), which is the standard pathological measurement used to diagnose this condition. Small fiber neuropathy represents damage to small sensory and autonomic nerve fibers.

I tested positive for TS-HDS autoantibodies, which are associated with immune-mediated neuropathy.

Further evaluation identified an autoimmune process. I was subsequently diagnosed with Sjögren’s syndrome, confirmed by a positive minor salivary gland (lip) biopsy.

Since the onset of these conditions in 2021, I have experienced persistent neuropathic pain affecting the limbs, face, and upper back, chronic tinnitus, fatigue, muscle weakness, and autonomic dysfunction.

Small fiber neuropathy can be difficult to diagnose because routine neurological examinations and nerve conduction studies are often normal.

Specialized testing such as skin biopsy is required, meaning cases may be under-recognized if this evaluation is not performed. Access to this diagnostic test is extremely limited in Canada.

In my case, the diagnosis of small fiber neuropathy was confirmed by a skin biopsy analyzed at Dr. Todd Levine’s Corinthian Reference Laboratory in the U.S.

However, I have encountered significant barriers in Canada because neurologists have declined to review or accept biopsy results obtained from the U.S. This has created difficulties in recognition and management of the condition despite objective pathological evidence of nerve fiber loss.

Greater awareness and acceptance of validated diagnostic testing for small fiber neuropathy, including skin biopsy analysis performed in specialized laboratories, may help improve diagnosis and patient care.

Although I reside in Canada, vaccine safety monitoring is an international issue. Continued research, transparency, and recognition of potential immune-mediated neurological complications are important for proper diagnosis and care of affected patients.

Thank you for considering documented patient experiences as part of this safety review.

Title: Comment from Anonymous
Comment:
As a public and occupational health educator, I am writing to express deep concerns regarding the future access to COVID vaccines and the ongoing impact of Long COVID nationally.

Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines.

Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection. It continues to be debilitating condition that deeply affects people’s lives and health in multiple ways.

New policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone.

The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines.

Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, and are especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions especially under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. It affects millions of Americans. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop it. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year (due to their rapid waning), for all COVID vaccine formulations. And I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID by preventing it and developing effective treatments.

I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using open and transparent processes.

Title: Comment from Neighbor, A Concerned
Comment: While I myself have not been injured by a Covid vaccine (because I decided against use of these products prior to their introduction to the market), I know that many in my community and in the lives of loved ones did not.  To date, I have attended multiple funerals for individuals who received one or more Covid vaccines (one due to deference to the authority of military-base physicians who were recommending them for elders; one due to her job with a Washington, D.C.-based governmental department; one due to her medical provider's recommendation as a childhood cancer survivor; one a physician himself who took the recommended Covid vaccines; one due to the insistence of the administrators at the long-term care facility where she lived).  I personally knew these individuals, whose ages spanned from late 20's to mid-90's at the time of initial Covid vaccination, and I knew many of them to be otherwise healthy, vibrant individuals who suddenly developed either recurrence of cancer (in one individual's case) or who within 1-3 years of Covid vaccination, developed aggressive cancers that caused their deaths (in the case of 4 individuals).  Although at none of their funerals and in none of their obituaries was the Covid vaccine mentioned, among those who were unvaccinated by Covid vaccines, there remain wonderings, whispers, and weeping for what might not have been had these beloved individuals not received these Covid vaccines.  These were precious family members, cherished community members, and dear friends whose lives were cut short and whose final months (and sometimes years) of their lives were marked by misery, characterized by cancerous suffering, and ultimately robbed of relationship by vaccine products that never had any business being put into the bodies of human beings.  There are permanent social, relational, and emotional gaps in the fabric of my life and the untold number of lives that these individuals knew and loved, and those holes cannot remain covered up by falsehood any longer.  These individuals were brave, courageous, and community-minded individuals who were either coerced, compelled, or exhorted by then-trusted authorities to receive the Covid vaccines, and they complied.  Their compliance was more costly than I believe any of them could have imagined.  I implore the ACIP to do everything in its power to remedy the damages done, to bravely pave a future of truly informed consent with respect to these Covid products or any such future products, and to bring national-level recommendations into line with what these life-shattering losses due to Covid vaccination should be teaching us, if we are willing to listen.

Title: Comment from Anonymous
Comment: I am writing to share the story of a mother whose child was injured by vaccines at 12 months of age.  I met this mother and her adult son during the course of supporting them with disability-related services, and she shared her son's story with me.  Her son, who was then in his early 20's, is a quadriplegic who uses a reclined wheelchair to support his neck, arms, torso, and legs, and he needs total support with his activities of daily living.  She described her son to me as having been a healthy infant who was meeting milestones early with crawling, babbling, starting to walk, even speaking words prior to his first birthday.  As recommended, she took her healthy son to his 12-month well-child check, and when the nurse informed her that her son would be receiving multiple vaccines at the appointment, this mother told me that she "felt a check in her spirit" when she realized the number of vaccines that would be entering her child's body.  She asked the nurse if that was safe, and the nurse dismissed her concerns, stating, "Oh, he'll be fine."  The mother was still hesitant, but she decided to trust the nurse's experience and authority.  That decision, she told me, would turn out to be devastating.  Within 24 hours of returning from that fateful appointment, this mother started observing regression in her child.  She informed me that he quickly lost his ability to walk, resorting to crawling, and then eventually lost that as well.  She said that his previously acquired language disappeared, and his gross motor and fine motor movements became very limited.  This young man is now fully dependent on the caregiving of others for every task, from toileting to wiping his mouth; he has no language except via eye contact, and his future is limited by the will of state-level and federal-level legislation for what disability-related services and supports are available.  This is unthinkable to know that this mother's trust in a medical provider and a medical system has so permanently altered the course of her child's life for the worse.  And like every parent with a child in these shoes, she wonders and silently fears what will become of her son when she is no longer able to care for him.

Title: Comment from Johnson, Sara
Comment:
To CDC ACIP,

I am submitting this comment as a patient whose case directly rebuts the idea that post-vaccine illness is too vague or too politically inconvenient to study.

My first Moderna dose was on December 29, 2021. My second was on January 30, 2022. My illness began on February 27, 2022, about 30 days later. On June 24, 2022, I had a negative nucleocapsid antibody test, showing no evidence of prior SARS-CoV-2 infection before my illness began. My first confirmed acute infection was not until December 3, 2022. That chronology matters. It is exactly the kind of case that gets erased when agencies act as though all chronic post-COVID symptom clusters must be infection-first.

CDC and ACIP should formally recognize post-vaccine Long COVID, or post-vaccination syndrome with overlapping Long COVID symptomatology, as a legitimate area of scientific, clinical, and public health concern. My illness has included severe fatigue, post-exertional malaise, cognitive dysfunction, and major functional impairment consistent with Long COVID and ME/CFS. I cannot exercise at all, and even basic daily activities can worsen my condition. Patients like me should not be excluded from the Long COVID conversation simply because the initiating event appears to have been vaccination rather than infection.

The policy response must go beyond passive acknowledgment. CDC and ACIP should support formal recognition, surveillance criteria, research inclusion, clinician guidance, and diagnostic and billing pathways for patients whose illness began after vaccination, including those with no evidence of prior infection at onset. Existing research already reflects the need to study these groups seriously and side by side. Yale’s LISTEN study was created to understand Long COVID, post-vaccine syndrome, and the corresponding immune responses. NIH protocol NCT04573062 includes a cohort for COVID-19 vaccine related medical conditions alongside post-COVID cohorts. Public health institutions should build the infrastructure to study and care for this population, rather than leaving patients in limbo.

Acknowledging vaccine injury must not be used as a rationale to abandon COVID mitigation. Vaccine policy and infection control are not substitutes for one another. Safe healthcare still requires respirators, masking, ventilation, air cleaning, and aerosol-aware standards, especially for people already living with disability or immune dysfunction. People with Long COVID, ME/CFS, and post-vaccine illness should not have to choose between seeking care and risking reinfection or further harm.

The harm here is not only biological. It is also institutional. Patients like me have faced lack of recognition, lack of research pathways, lack of coding and reimbursement, lack of safe healthcare access, and a climate in which people harmed after vaccination are either exploited by anti-vaccine politics or erased by institutions unwilling to face the implications. ACIP and CDC should reject both erasure and exploitation, recognize this patient population, include it in Long COVID-related science and policy, and help build the clinical and research infrastructure patients have been denied for far too long.

Thank you,

Sara Johnson
Los Angeles, CA

Title: Comment from Casanova, Nicole
Comment:
My name is Nicole Casanova. I am submitting this comment in strong support of publicly convening a review of COVID-19 vaccine injuries. I urge the Committee to expand this work, act on the data already submitted by injured individuals, and develop concrete policy recommendations to address the ongoing medical, financial, and psychological harm experienced by vaccine-injured Americans.
I am one of those people. I received the COVID-19 vaccine — not entirely by choice, but under requirement. I delayed vaccination for over a year because I knew my body was vulnerable. When I could no longer travel or re-enter the United States without it, I complied. What followed changed my life permanently.

II. BACKGROUND: MY PERSONAL EXPERIENCE
Following vaccination, I developed eight separate autoimmune conditions, including:
• Lyme disease reactivation
• Mast Cell Activation Syndrome (MCAS)
• Epstein-Barr virus reactivation
• Lichen planus
• Behçet's disease
• Additional autoimmune conditions related to spike protein pathology
I have seen more than 30 doctors across multiple countries to stay alive. I have been gaslit, dismissed, and turned away. I submitted my adverse events to VAERS, participated in studies like Yale. Not once has anyone from the government or medical responded, or acknowledged my submissions.

III. ANALYSIS: THE REAL AND ONGOING IMPACT
The harm I have experienced is medical, financial, social, and existential. I want the Committee to understand what this injury has actually cost:

Financial Devastation
• I liquidated my IRA to pay for medical treatment. I now owe significant taxes on those withdrawals — for a vaccine I was required to take.
• I have spent well over $100,000 USD out of pocket to survive, for treatments not yet “recognized” by mainstream medicine.
• I recently completed EBOO (extracorporeal blood oxygenation and ozonation) treatments at $4,000 each in the United States. I now live in Thailand, where the same treatment costs $600
• I spent $900 on blood tests this week alone.

Displacement and Uprooting
The cost of staying alive in America, combined with the complete absence of financial support or medical acknowledgment for vaccine-injured, means I am mostly in other countries seeking cheaper care. Many injured individuals have lost their jobs, their homes, their savings, and in some cases their families and relationships.

Silencing and Institutional Gaslighting
I am shadow-banned and algorithmically suppressed on multiple social media platforms for speaking about my injury. The silencing of vaccine-injured voices has been systematic. We were ignored, censored, and dismissed.
This gaslighting — by the government, the media, and even our own doctors — has been more devastating than the physical illness itself. When you are sick, bankrupt, and everyone tells you it isn't happening, the psychological toll is immeasurable. Assisted death is the option for those out of money, options, hope.

IV. RECOMMENDATIONS
I respectfully urge ACIP and CDC to take the following actions:
• Formally acknowledge the existence and scale of COVID-19 vaccine injury as a real, documented, and ongoing public health matter.
• Create a dedicated federal support program for vaccine-injured individuals that includes financial assistance, subsidized medical care, and access to emerging treatments.
• Require meaningful follow-up with individuals who submitted adverse event reports to VAERS — we submitted our data in good faith and received nothing in return.
• Fund independent, long-term research into spike protein pathology, MCAS, autoimmune reactivation, and other documented injury patterns.
• Establish a no-fault compensation fund for documented vaccine-injured individuals, modeled on existing vaccine injury compensation frameworks.
• Host additional public hearings to ensure the ongoing experiences of the injured are part of the policymaking record.
• Actively work to end the algorithmic and institutional silencing of vaccine-injured voices, so that people can speak, connect, and find support without penalty.

V. CONCLUSION
I am grateful that this meeting is happening. I know it almost didn't. I want the Committee to understand that for millions of people, this hearing represents the first time we have felt seen in years. That is how low the bar has been set.
We are not asking for sympathy. We are asking for acknowledgment, research, treatment access, and the same basic protections afforded to anyone injured by a government-mandated or government-recommended medical intervention.
I am still sick. I am still paying. I am still here. Please do not let this window close.
Thank you for taking the time to read this comment and for the work you are doing to address vaccine injury. It matters more than I can fully express.

Respectfully submitted,
Nicole Casanova

Title: Comment from Anonymous
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199

Public Comment for March 18 to 19 CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I have no conflicts of interest to disclose.

In March 2020, before vaccines against COVID were developed, I was a healthy college athlete when I caught SARS-COV-2 from a teammate. Today, I am a federal employee with Long Covid. Long Covid has significantly altered my life - I use a wheelchair, and I can only work part-time.

It has been demonstrated that COVID vaccines reduce the chance of acquiring Long Covid ("Increasing number of vaccinations further decreases Long COVID 23–36%" - https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S221133552500227X). Every year, I get at least one COVID vaccine to prevent my health from worsening further and to prevent what happened to me from happening to others in my community.

As I approach my seventh year of becoming disabled, I am deeply worried about recent harmful and unwarranted changes on vaccine policy at the CDC and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition.

Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines. Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection and has an estimated economic cost of $2-6 billion annually (https://sph.cuny.edu/life-at-sph/news/2025/03/19/long-covid-cost/).

RFK Jr., Marty Makary, and Vinay Prasad have previously imposed new policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will without doubt harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone. The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines. Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, and are especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions especially under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. Long COVID is a debilitating condition that affects millions of Americans. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop this serious condition. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year (due to their rapid waning), for all COVID vaccine formulations. I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID via prevention and the development of effective treatments. I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using transparent and democratic processes.

Thank you for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Anonymous
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199

Public Comment for March 18 to 19 CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I am a senior citizen who is also immunocompromised, and I am deeply concerned about recent harmful and unwarranted changes to vaccine policy at the CDC, and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition.

A vaccinated population not only protects those who are vaccinated, they also protect those who are not, or who cannot be, vaccinated. And they protect those for whom vaccination may not be completely effective, such as myself. Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines. Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection. It continues to be debilitating condition that affects people’s health.

RFK Jr., Marty Makary, and Vinay Prasad have previously imposed new policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will without doubt harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone. The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines. Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, which is especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions, including those under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. Long COVID is a debilitating condition that affects millions of Americans. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop this serious condition. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year for all COVID vaccine formulations, due to their rapid waning. I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID both through prevention and by developing effective treatments. I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using open and transparent processes, all of which are hallmarks of democracy.

Thank you for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Lemos, Diana
Comment:
Public Comment to the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP)

I am a public health professional working in population health and health equity. My work focuses on translating evidence into practice to improve health outcomes. I have no financial conflicts of interest related to vaccines or vaccine manufacturers.

I urge ACIP to continue providing strong, science-based guidance that ensures equitable access to COVID-19 vaccines and other recommended immunizations. ACIP’s recommendations play a critical role in shaping national vaccine policy and ensuring that people across the United States can access lifesaving preventive care.

ACIP must clearly acknowledge that available COVID-19 vaccines are safe and effective and remain vital for children, adults, and pregnant people. Millions rely on ACIP recommendations to maintain access to vaccines through insurance coverage and public health programs. Weakening these recommendations risks creating barriers to care and widening health inequities.

ACIP must also continue recommending widespread and unhindered access to vaccines that protect people across the life course. Vaccination remains one of the most effective public health interventions ever developed.

In addition, ACIP should preserve a universal recommendation for COVID-19 vaccination for people of all ages, including children and pregnant people, with vaccination available at least twice annually. Consistent guidance helps clinicians, families, and health systems make informed decisions.

Long COVID remains a serious public health challenge and must continue to be addressed by science-based experts committed to developing effective treatments and prevention strategies.

On a personal level, this issue matters deeply to me. I contracted COVID-19 following a workplace exposure and experienced lasting health effects, including chronic fatigue, brain fog, and severe sciatica that affected my daily functioning. During the same period, my immune system was under such strain that I also developed shingles.

These experiences underscored how serious COVID-19 infection can be and why prevention remains critical. Long COVID is not abstract—it continues to affect many people long after infection.

For these reasons, I urge ACIP to maintain strong, universal COVID-19 vaccine recommendations and continue advancing science-based policies that protect public health.

Title: Comment from Nicole , Nurse
Comment:
My name is Nicole and I am a Central Florida, acute care Registered Nurse of 20yrs. I worked as a heart transplant nurse during the COVID 19 pandemic and saw firsthand what the COVID-19 vaccines did to those vulnerable heart transplant patients. Weakening their immune systems more, denying them a transplant if they didn’t comply, and watching them come back and forth into the hospital because their immune systems couldn’t handle anything that came their way. This amplified when the types of cardiac patients became younger and more fragile as we saw an increase of myocarditis, pericarditis, blood clots, and neurological issues admitted to our unit. Our once fairly stable unit, saw a rise in freshly covid vaccinated patients suffering cardiac, neurological, and sepsis events. The staff that rolled up their sleeves to be “team players” were also starting to show health issues. A rise in call outs due to pain, fatigue, N/V, and inability to seemingly fight off anything that came there way. Their ages ranged from early 20’s-50’s. Irregular and missed periods, and trouble getting pregnant were also becoming common conversations but with no clear indication of what was causing it. It was so subtle, most would have missed it. Fast forward to when I came forward to my local and state government to plead with Orange County Commissioners and Florida Legislators that what I was seeing as a nurse was unfolding rapidly in front of me. I was mocked, bullied, segregated, and eventually deaths threats ensued because not only did I refuse to get the vaccine, I was speaking out against what most thought was really happening because the news said so. I was the minority that stood up for not only my patients, but myself. Speaking out to anyone who would listen that in all my years as an RN, I’ve never seen such vile and lack of compassionate care to those who chose to not vaccinate and the amount of harm being seen come through the hospital doors. The stories I could tell of those not vaccinated being discriminated against and their care jeopardized because they chose to not vaccinate are too many to count. The level of trauma I endured watching it happen day in and day out with what seemed like zero end in sight- gut wrenching. In 2023, I went to Tallahassee to rally with medical freedom groups across the state of Florida where SB252 was passed. I had been a hospital nurse who refused the vaccine and had been openly discriminated and segregated for it at one of the largest hospitals in Orlando. Fighting to have kept my religious exemption back in 2020, I was now faced with discrimination for other vaccines as well. With SB 252 passing, Floridians could no longer be mandated to take an experimental COVID 19 vaccine. A campaign to #BanTheJabs and #BanTheJabsFlorida unfolded as more individuals were being harmed and losing their lives due to these less than safe and effective products. Floridians and beyond were calling for their immediate removal due to their harm. These initiatives, grass root groups, and pushes to end mandates in Florida blessed me with meeting more vaccine injured and bereaved human beings. For this, I continue to show up, speak out, and work on a local and state level to push for informed consent, bodily autonomy, and the right to choose. I also am a huge medical advocate for patients, families, and the vaccine injured. Heavily pushing Cody’s Law with the author Heather Hudson and others, demanding justice for those harmed and no longer with us due to the Covid19 vaccines. There are too many names to count of who are injured and no longer with us because of this mass vaccination mandate that has ruined millions of lives across the U.S. The stakes continue to be high as the damage of these products are showing a significant rise of heart attacks, strokes, neurological issues, and trouble fighting infections. As I continue to work in the hospital setting, the rise of sick, injured, and patients that continue to be a revolving door in the hospital can be seen with anywhere from 2-10 Covid 19 vaccines in their systems.
We need the injured and bereaved to be able to tell their stories without judgement, censorship, or way of being cherry picked to share with the masses. We must have accountability.
We must continue to fight for the vulnerable and give a voice to the voiceless. The rights of individuals should not be based on their vaccination status. “Where there is risk, there must be choice.”

Title: Comment from Hearn, Dorothy
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199

Public Comment for March 18 to 19 CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I am writing as an American who is concerned about the future of COVID-19 vaccines, and Long COVID research and treatment. I am writing because of my own health risks associated to COVID-19 infections, as well as for my friends, family, coworkers, and all of us. For myself, I have a genetic condition which puts me at high risk of negative outcomes from COVID infections. I am the primary wage earner in my household, as well as the sole driver. For me, a COVID-19 infection is very likely to result in hospitalization, which would result in loss of work -which means a loss of income- as well as high medical bills. My genetic condition is not curable, so this is not something which is going to go away. The condition also puts me at increased risk of Long COVID, and higher than average risk of death. Any of these outcomes -hospitalization, Long COVID, death- would be a hardship for my family. So, yes, my interest in the continued support of the already researched and approved vaccinations is for me, but it is also all of us. Removing the scientifically researched and approved vaccines, stopping the proven research methods and work already done toward the prevention and treatment of Long COVID, are vital. COVID-19 is not just a cold or the flu. It has been scientifically proven that COVID-19 is a vascular disease which affects the whole body, including heart and other organ health, it leads to blood clots, and more.

As others have already said and shared, I support the following:

I am deeply worried about recent harmful and unwarranted changes on vaccine policy at the CDC and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition. This is a vital issue to me because of the following below.

Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines. Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection. It continues to be debilitating condition that affects people’s health.

RFK Jr., Marty Makary, and Vinay Prasad have previously imposed new policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will without doubt harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone. The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines. Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, and are especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions especially under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. Long COVID is a debilitating condition that affects millions of Americans. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop this serious condition. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year (due to their rapid waning), for all COVID vaccine formulations. I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID either by preventing it or developing effective treatments. I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using open and transparent processes, all of which are hallmarks of democracy.

Thank you for your consideration.

Title: Comment from Association of Immunization Managers
Comment:
The Association of Immunization Managers (AIM), a non-partisan, tax-exempt 501(c)3 member
organization representing the 66 federally funded immunization programs, appreciates the
opportunity to provide public comment in advance of the March 18-19, 2026 meeting of the
Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP). Our members are responsible for the
frontline implementation of national, state, local, and territorial vaccine policy, and we remain
committed to ensuring all individuals have access to life-saving vaccines through a stable and
predictable public health infrastructure.
As the Committee deliberates potential recommendations that will impact national
immunization schedules, we urge a steadfast commitment to the following principles:
• Adherence to the ACIP Charter: The Committee must operate within its defined scope,
providing advice and guidance to the CDC Director regarding the use of vaccines for the
effective control of vaccine-preventable diseases in the U.S. civilian population.
• Reliance on Standardized Evaluation Frameworks: National policy should be informed
exclusively by a rigorous, transparent evaluation of scientific data using established
methodologies. We strongly advocate for the consistent use of the Grading of
Recommendations, Assessment, Development, and Evaluation (GRADE) approach to
assess the certainty of evidence. The Evidence to Recommendations (EtR) framework
should be fully utilized to synthesize evidence across key domains, including benefits,
harms, and health economic analyses.
• Prioritization of Peer-Reviewed Science: It is essential that recommendations be based
on peer-reviewed, high-quality scientific literature rather than personal anecdotes or
unsubstantiated claims. Personal narratives, while important for individual clinical
interactions, do not provide the necessary statistical power or methodological rigor
required to inform national public health policy.
• Adherence to the Gold Standard of Science: Parents and healthcare providers rely upon
credible science for vaccine confidence. This standard was defined in Executive Order
issued by the White House on May 23, 2025 and refers to “science conducted in a
manner that is reproducible, transparent, communicative of error and uncertainty,
collaborative and interdisciplinary, skeptical of its findings and assumptions, structured
for falsifiability of hypotheses, subject to unbiased peer review, accepting of negative
results as positive outcomes; and without conflicts of interest.”
• Consistency and Implementation: Abrupt or non-evidence-based changes to the
immunization schedule create significant administrative and clinical barriers.
Predictability in recommendations is vital for state and local programs to manage
vaccine supply, provider education, and public communication.
AIM urges the ACIP to resume its long-standing role as an expert-driven, science-based advisory
body. This commitment is the foundation of public trust and the primary safeguard against the
resurgence of preventable diseases.
Sincerely,
Michelle D. Fiscus, MD FAAP
Chief Medical Officer
Association of Immunization Mangers

I am a concerned citizen deeply worried about recent harmful and unwarranted changes on vaccine policy at the CDC and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition. This is a vital issue to me because of the following below.

Today, I am writing to express grave concerns of the future of COVID vaccines and Long COVID.

Title: Comment from Wa.ng, And.rew
Comment:
Docket: CDC-2026-0199

Public Comment for March 18 to 19 CDC ACIP Committee Meeting

To Members of the CDC ACIP committee:

I have no financial onflicts of interest to disclose.

I am a public health professional deeply worried about recent harmful and unwarranted changes on vaccine policy at the CDC and the future prevention and treatment of Long COVID as a health condition. This is a vital issue to me because of the following below.

Today, I am writing to express grave concerns of the future of COVID vaccines and Long COVID.

Current vaccination rates for many infectious diseases are very low, and many US communities are directly affected by outbreaks of vaccine-preventable diseases such as measles, pertussis, and COVID. Many of these infections can cause serious and potentially lifelong consequences to people’s health. Children in 2026 should not die or become disabled from infectious diseases because they lack access to affordable and effective vaccines. Long COVID also continues to affect millions of Americans caused by a COVID infection. It continues to be debilitating condition that affects people’s health.

RFK Jr., Marty Makary, and Vinay Prasad have previously imposed new policies that limit access to COVID vaccinations. Creating such barriers is reckless and will without doubt harm the public’s health. Updated COVID vaccines must remain universally accessible and widely available at no cost to everyone. The scientific evidence is irrefutable, so it not only endangers Americans’ health but also is a waste of precious resources and time to require additional efficacy studies, which would only serve to sow doubt in vaccines. Receiving a COVID vaccine within the last year reduces the risk of symptoms, severe disease, disability, and death for people of all ages, including previously healthy people. Restrictive policies create barriers to universal access to COVID vaccines, and are especially essential for children, pregnant people, older adults, and adults with underlying health conditions especially under the age of 65. These restrictions also create immense confusion among the public on the usefulness of COVID vaccines.

Long COVID is a severe health condition caused by a COVID infection. Long COVID is a debilitating condition that affects millions of Americans. The ongoing spread of COVID infections will continue to drive an increase in the number of people who develop this serious condition. We need science-based experts to provide oversight on the prevention and treatment of Long COVID. It is very important to develop effective treatments to address this condition.

I urge you to take action, follow the evidence, and reinstate universal COVID vaccination recommendations for people of all ages, authorizing them twice a year (due to their rapid waning), for all COVID vaccine formulations. I urge you to ensure science-based experts oversee the future direction of addressing Long COVID either by preventing it or developing effective treatments. I ask this ACIP committee to vote for policies advancing universal access to vaccines and the prevention of Long COVID as well as to ensure that decisions are considered, evaluated, and concluded using open and transparent processes, all of which are hallmarks of democracy.

Thank you for your consideration.

Andrew Wang, PhD, MPH, CPH

Title: Comment from Ramirez, Andres
Comment:
March 12, 2026

Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
Department of Health and Human Services
1600 Clifton Road NE, Mailstop H21-12
Atlanta, GA 30329-4027

Re: CDC-2026-0199

Dear Members of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices,

On behalf of the National Hispanic Health Foundation Action Fund (NHHFAF), I appreciate the opportunity to provide comments ahead of ACIP's March 18–19, 2026 meeting on vaccine safety, Long COVID, and ACIP methodology. NHHFAF's mission is to promote social welfare through health advocacy for Hispanic communities. We write regarding Hispanics who have witnessed adverse outcomes following vaccination or who, for documented medical or other reasons, are seeking alternatives to traditional vaccines for their children and loved ones.

Hispanics face persistent barriers to healthcare access, including language difficulties, lack of insurance, transportation challenges, and long wait times. These families deserve an advisory process that respects their concerns, accounts for lived experience, and remains flexible to new data.

The COVID pandemic placed Hispanics under great strain. In the US, Hispanics were three times as likely as whites to contract COVID-19 and two times as likely to die from it. Hispanics managed complex medical needs amid shifting guidance and limited information, often while facing language barriers and healthcare discrimination.

The pandemic also showed that some patients experienced serious adverse events following COVID vaccination. Myocarditis and pericarditis, particularly in adolescent males, have been documented by CDC and FDA, with an estimated excess of over 20 cases per million second doses among 18–29-year-olds. Among documented myocarditis cases following vaccination, 26% of patients were Hispanic. Such outcomes must be addressed within communities already facing healthcare mistrust.

Serious concerns have been raised about the short- and long-term effects of COVID infection. Long COVID disproportionately affects Hispanics. Hispanic adults report Long COVID at 8.3% compared to 7.1% for white non-Hispanic adults. This post-infection condition can create immune disruption, trigger immune responses against healthy tissues, and result in chronic health complications.

Given these elevated rates of COVID-19 infection and Long COVID in Hispanic communities, COVID-19 vaccine labels can be improved to more accurately and transparently guide healthcare professionals and patients when choosing among appropriate prevention options.

The COVID pandemic showed that a one-size-fits-all approach to prevention fails many Hispanics. While Hispanic children demonstrate strong vaccination coverage overall, Hispanics continue to experience barriers to vaccine access, including misinformation and healthcare discrimination.

Even where vaccines are available, hesitancy remains a concern. About 30% of Hispanic adults report COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy, higher than the overall US adult hesitancy rate of 26.3%. Surveys of Hispanics consistently find that hesitancy is driven by concerns about safety, side effects, rushed development, and mistrust of health institutions, rather than opposition to prevention itself.

Some Hispanic children have underlying conditions that complicate vaccination, and parents are concerned about safety based on their child's medical history or past reactions. Families are seeking alternatives that align with their values, their pediatrician's guidance, and their experiences navigating a healthcare system that has not always served them equitably.

Monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) are a scientifically validated alternative for families who cannot or choose not to pursue traditional vaccines. Unlike vaccines, mAbs provide passive immunity that does not depend on an individual's immune response, making them valuable for immunocompromised children, infants, and those with contraindications to available vaccines. Expanding ACIP's recommendation framework to include mAbs as a preventive option for children would give pediatricians and Hispanic parents an important, evidence-based tool they currently lack equal access to.

ACIP's recommendations carry enormous weight; they drive insurance coverage, state immunization schedules, and clinical guidance nationwide. When ACIP focuses solely on traditional vaccines and excludes alternatives such as mAbs, it limits parental choice, restricts pediatric access, and can drive hesitant Hispanics away from preventive care.

NHHFAF supports prevention through a broad toolkit and an ACIP framework that takes the immunological complexity of this virus seriously across the life course. Hispanics are counting on ACIP to lead with both scientific rigor and cultural competency. We look forward to your consideration of these critical issues affecting the health and well-being of America's Hispanic communities.

Sincerely,
Andres Ramirez
NHHFAF President

Title: Comment from Doromal, Nathaniel
Comment:
I am a medical journalist and a MPH candidate. I have long followed the issue of vaccination. The more I examine the issue, the more I am disturbed by gaps in the vaccine safety literature and the deliberate ignorance of the medical professionals to fix them. Here are the issues.

Problem #1: The lack of investigation into vaccine injury by public health and medical authorities
- Despite decades of knowledge, medicine still doesn’t know fully about the etiology of vaccine injury.
- It remains unclear how vaccines relate to autoimmunity despite IOM acknowledgement of autoimmune conditions like Guillain-Barre linked to vaccines.
- Prioritization of defense of vaccine policy has superseded investigation into vaccine injury.

Problem #2: Rampant use of “relative risk” assessments and faux safety comparison vs. “the disease itself”
- Medicine commonly uses obviously biased, apples-to-oranges comparisons between safety of the vaccine vs. adverse events of the disease itself.
- Receipt of the vaccine does not guarantee protection from the disease (i.e., problems with the COVID vaccine).
- Vaccine safety should “stand-alone” without regards to the disease itself. A truly safe vaccine should have accompanying absolute risk measurements.

Problem #3: The lack of inquiry into aluminum adjuvants despite a litany of troubling research
- There is no fundamental safety research that establishes minimum risk levels for aluminum for humans.
- There is evidence that aluminum adjuvant can accumulate inside the brain and organs. This is poorly understood and the resulting effects are unknown.
- There is no consideration given to cumulative vaccines and accumulating aluminum adjuvant in the body.

Problem #4: The reliance upon broken VAERS system
- Medicine has known about the non-causal, anecdotal nature of VAERS system yet is not required to investigate cases nor vet incoming reports.
- VAERS is the widest, public-facing reporting system for vaccine injury yet it has obvious flaws that prevent attribution of adverse effects specifically to a vaccine.
- Medical professionals commonly deride “VAERS as anecdotal” and “VAERS as observation,” and, when combined with previous facts, means we are “flying blind” to full extent of vaccine injury.
- There is evidence to suggest vaccine injury is under-diagnosed in the population.

Problem #5: Common beliefs like “vaccines don’t cause autism” are not backed by the evidence
- Medicine has repeated the claim for decades that “vaccines don’t cause autism,” but only one vaccine (MMR) and one ingredient (thimerosal) have been thoroughly studied.
- Medicine has ignored the cumulative effects of vaccination and the 90+ doses on the schedule.
- There is evidence that maternal immune activation and infant inflammation are associated with autism.
- There is no safety evidence that evaluates multiple vaccines given at the same time (i.e., Hannah Poling case).

Problem #6: A “one-size fits all” reductionist approach ignores genetics, existing toxic loads, and vulnerable subgroups
- Reductionism taken too far ignores obvious factors that exist in the populace.
- Certain genetic profiles (i.e., MTHFR gene mutation), existing toxic loads, and unknown factors can lead to vulnerable subgroups that are not taken into account by existing safety studies.
- Vaccine safety studies commonly exhibit a “healthy user bias.”
- Reductionist approach fails to incorporate the latest scientific findings (i.e., importance of the gut microbiome on the immune system and on brain health).

Problem #7: Problematic assertions that the body can handle ambiguous “high simultaneous levels of antigens” without issue
- Dr. Offit has claimed the body can handle “over 10,000 simultaneous antigens” without issue. This has been stated without empirical evidence to back it up.
- Dr. Offit’s statement ignores additional mechanisms in vaccines such as the use of non-inert ingredients like aluminum adjuvants and thimerosal, different pharmacokinetics, and adverse effects from delivery mechanism (i.e., mRNA technology).
- No research to establish safety limit from simultaneously administered vaccines or that matches up adverse effects vs. number of vaccines received.
- Dr. Offit’s statement has set back vaccine safety research by decades.

Problem #8: The ignorance of basic epidemiology history that leads to a systematic over-inflation of vaccine benefits
- Medicine commonly makes lies of omission that systematically over-inflate benefits of vaccination, ignoring such facts that infectious disease mortality fell by 90% prior to the modern vaccination program.

The above issues, not only come into play when assessing COVID vaccination, but vaccination in general. To maintain the integrity of the US vaccine program, these problems must be addressed.

Our group strongly endorses the use of vaccines to help prevent serious disease, hospitalizations and death.
