Thank you.

*FDA Fact Sheet: ---------------------------EUA FOR PEMGARDA------------------------------------
The U.S. FA has issued an EUA for the emergency use of the unapproved
product PEMGARDA (pemivibart), ...for the pre-exposure prophylaxis of coronavirus disease
2019 (COVID-19) in adults and adolescents (12 years of age and older weighing
at least 40 kg):
...• who have moderate-to-severe immune compromise due to a medical condition
or receipt of immunosuppressive medications or treatments and are unlikely
to mount an adequate immune response to COVID-19 vaccination. https://www.fda.gov/media/177067/download

**IDSA: Recommendation:
"In moderately or severely immunocompromised individuals 12 years or older at risk for progression to severe COVID-19, the IDSA guideline panel suggests pre-exposure prophylaxis with pemivibart when predominant regional variants are susceptible to the agent (conditional recommendation, low certainty of evidence)." https://www.idsociety.org/practice-guideline/covid-19-guideline-treatment-and-management/

Title: Comment from Anonymous
Comment:
Hello, my name is Stephen and I appreciate the opportunity to provide public comment to the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices.

I would like to briefly tell you about my experience with the affects of vaccines. In 1985, I nearly died as an infant from an unexplained condition called intussusception, which is a gut inflammatory response causing a blockage. I received my 3rd dose of the oral polio vaccine (OPV) on 11/25/1985, 1 month before I began projectile vomiting and I was rushed to the hospital Christmas day having emergency surgery to remove a small part of my colon and small intestine. I cannot know for sure whether my case was or was not caused by the OPV, but there was no other explanation given. The vaccine was, however, taken off the market for causing severe polio cases, as the live virus could mutate. Similarly, the rotavirus vaccine, RotaShield, was taken off the market in 1999 because it was actually proven to be causing intussusception. I did not receive the oral rotavirus vaccine, but I would imagine the possibility of similar sideaffects from a live-virus oral vaccine.

Asking my parents about whether they were told about the risks of vaccines, they said they never knew there were any risks, and therefore they never knew to look into the vaccines as a potential cause of my intussusception. Unfortunately, without testing at the time we will never know the cause of my case. It makes sense to me now why VAERS is an innacurate metric for counting vaccine injuries (they say between 1 and 10 percent of adverse events are ever reported), as I'm sure many parents of vaccine injured aren't aware of the risks either and would not think to investigate them since they are told over and over that vaccines are "safe and effective".

Now at 40 years old looking back, many of my health issues point to vaccine sideaffects: irregular bowel issues, severe allergies, trouble concentrating, and semi-regular headaches. I cannot that say for sure that my health conditions are due to my vaccines without having had testing done at the time of vaccination, but one thing that makes me curious is that my unvaccinated children have not had the same conditions, telling me that they were unlikely to be genetic.

My two children, my 7-year-old son and my 4-year-old daughter, have never been vaccinated. So far, they have never had a severe case of any childhood diseases, knock on wood. They neither have allergies nor gut issues (outside of the occasional belly aches that all children complain about). Neither of them have any signs of learning disabilities, ADHD, nor autism. They are healthy by all metrics. We have never even had an emergency room visit! Some might say that is coincidence or luck, but I don't think luck has anything to do with it. If you are familiar with Del Bigtree's documentary An Inconvenient Study, about the study of the vaccinated versus unvaccinated health outcomes, you will know that the those in the study showed that negative health outcomes in practically all categories had less incidence in the unvaccinated than in the vaccinated. I think we are just one more case proving that theory.

The Physicians For Informed Consent have a guidebook comparing disease by disease and vaccine by vaccine and showing how little data we have on the current vaccine schedule. This publication, called Vaccines And The Diseases They Target, has over 400 peer reviewed studies showing that our data is innacurate to prove vaccine safety and efficacy over getting the viruses themselves. I think they should be looked into and tested more before recommending to be given to anyone, much less our most precious and vulnerable population, our children. I am hoping you will consider this when making recommendations, and will recommend that the parents, who have the most knowledge about their childrens' health history and body makeup, have the right to make the best decisions that suit our children. I sometimes wonder if I had vaccinated my son would he be more prone to adverse reactions to vaccines like me and been another severely injured case. I am pleased with mine and my wife's decision not to vaccinate, and wish more people had the information to make an informed decision like we had had.

Thank you again for your time.

Stephen

Title: Comment from Anonymous
Comment:
I am submitting this comment as an immunocompromised patient with a diagnosed primary immune deficiency. Although I have received recommended vaccinations, including MMR and other routine vaccines, my immune system does not reliably produce protective antibodies. As a result, vaccination alone does not provide me with meaningful protection against vaccine-preventable diseases.

For patients like me, protection depends on two things: strong vaccination coverage in the community and access to immunoglobulin therapy for passive immunity. Unfortunately, after developing Long COVID I began experiencing allergic reactions and currently cannot receive immunoglobulin. This leaves me with little to no protection against infections such as measles and other vaccine-preventable illnesses.

When vaccination rates decline or public health messaging around vaccines becomes weakened or inconsistent, medically vulnerable people face immediate and serious risk. Many individuals with primary immune deficiencies, cancer, transplant history, or other conditions cannot rely on vaccines alone to protect them. We depend on evidence-based vaccine policy and high community vaccination coverage to stay alive.

Federal vaccine policy and advisory committee recommendations have direct consequences for people like me. Decisions that reduce vaccination uptake or undermine confidence in vaccines increase the likelihood of outbreaks and place immunocompromised Americans at risk of severe illness or death.

I urge federal health leadership and advisory committees to maintain strong, science-based vaccine recommendations for all vaccines and to prioritize protecting medically vulnerable populations who depend on community immunity for survival.

For patients who cannot rely on vaccines for protection, vaccination policy is a matter of survival.

Title: Comment from Forrestal, Doyle
Comment:
To the Members of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices,

My name is Doyle Forrestal, and I am submitting this testimony in memory of my husband, Doug Wilson, who suffered what we believe was a severe adverse reaction following repeated doses of the Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine.

My husband received a total of five COVID-19 vaccinations. After the third dose, he began experiencing severe and persistent dermatological reactions. His body developed widespread welts accompanied by relentless itching that covered much of his skin. These symptoms were not mild or temporary. They were constant, painful, and profoundly disruptive to his daily life.

He sought medical care from multiple physicians. Unfortunately, the doctors we consulted were unable to identify the underlying cause of his condition or provide an effective treatment that brought relief. Despite their efforts, his symptoms persisted and caused him significant physical and emotional distress. The itching and welts were debilitating and greatly diminished his quality of life during his final months.

Very soon after receiving his fifth Pfizer COVID-19 vaccination, my husband tragically passed away. He believed strongly in following the guidance of his doctors and took their advice seriously. When I found him unresponsive and called for help, the paramedics asked me how many COVID-19 vaccinations he had received. Based on the sequence of events and the severe reactions he experienced, our family believes the vaccine ultimately caused his death.

I appreciate that the Committee is taking time to consider the experiences of individuals who have suffered serious adverse reactions. People like my husband deserve to have their stories heard and taken seriously. I hope the Committee will use this opportunity to examine the many untimely deaths and unresolved illnesses that families across the country believe may be associated with vaccine injury, and to approach these cases with seriousness and compassion.

I respectfully ask the Committee to continue investigating vaccine injuries, to improve recognition and treatment of adverse reactions, and to ensure that individuals and families affected by these events are heard.

My husband suffered greatly in his final months, and our family continues to live with that loss. I hope that sharing his story contributes to greater understanding, transparency, and care for others who may experience similar reactions.
Sincerely,
Doyle Forrestal
Miami Beach, Florida

Title: Comment from Vaccine Choice Canada

Re: Public Comment Submission – Docket CDC-2026-0199

Thank you for the opportunity to provide comment as ACIP reviews vaccine safety, injury surveillance, and related recommendation frameworks. Your work remains essential to maintaining public confidence in the safety and efficacy of our health care system: confidence that must be grounded in transparency, accountability and the fair evaluation of evidence.

This submission respectfully draws attention to the evidentiary record compiled by Vaccine Choice Canada. Vaccine Choice Canada is Canada’s longest serving vaccine education, risk awareness, and accountability organization, established in 1982. We have gathered information and evidence for 44 years. The following is a high level summary of the evidence and concerns with current vaccine mandates and protocols.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

1.Vaccine mandates are a violation of fundamental rights and freedoms including bodily sovereignty, medical choice, religious freedom, and security of the person.
2.Vaccine mandates are a violation of the right and responsibility of parents to make medical decisions for their children.
3.Coercion is not consent.
4.No vaccine has been proven safe using the gold standard of safety testing - a true placebo-controlled trial.
5.Vaccine efficacy is based on surrogate measures rather than improved health.
6.No scientific evidence confirms the benefits of vaccination outweigh the risks.

RECOMMENDATIONS

1.Remove all vaccine mandates. Mandates are coercion and violate ethical medicine.
2.Make public declaration that no vaccine has been proven safer than non-vaccination.
3.Uphold the legal and medical ethic of informed consent.
4.Repeal 1986 Act. Hold vaccine manufacturers legally and financially liable for injury and death caused by their products.
5.Hold those who commit coercive vaccination practices accountable for failing to secure voluntary informed consent.
6.All vaccine safety testing must utilize a true placebo.
7.Vaccination must be proven to be safer than non-vaccination.
8.Cease all multiple vaccinations.
9.Mandate vaccine adverse events reporting with consequences for failing to report vaccine adverse events.
10.Increase transparency and public communication regarding safety concerns and evidence evaluation.
11.Independent review of emerging safety data to strengthen public trust.
12.End conflict of interest in decision-making bodies.

EVIDENCE and references attached.

Ted Kuntz, President
Vaccine Choice Canada

Title: Comment from S, Andrea
Comment:
I am submitting this comment in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199.

I am a person living with a COVID-19 vaccine injury. After my adverse reaction, my life changed in ways I never expected. I have had to navigate ongoing health challenges along with uncertainty about diagnosis, treatment, and long-term outcomes. Like many others in similar circumstances, I have found that there are limited clinical guidelines and few coordinated care pathways for patients experiencing serious post-vaccine complications.

One of the most difficult aspects of this experience has been seeking medical care. Many individuals living with vaccine injuries report that their symptoms are often dismissed or not recognized as potential adverse reactions. Even when clinicians believe patients and want to help, many acknowledge that there is little guidance available on how to diagnose or treat these conditions. This leaves patients without clear answers and physicians without the tools they need to provide effective care.

Living with this condition has affected my daily life, my health, and my ability to function with the stability I once had. It has also highlighted how invisible people living with vaccine injuries can feel within the broader healthcare system.

I respectfully urge the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to ensure that patient perspectives and real-world experiences are included in its deliberations. Policies related to vaccine recommendation guidelines, safety monitoring, post-adverse-event care, and research priorities have direct and lasting impacts on individuals and families.

To better support people affected by serious adverse events, I ask the committee to consider the following:

• Recognizing the importance of patient-reported experiences when evaluating vaccine safety and outcomes.
• Encouraging expanded research into the mechanisms, diagnosis, and treatment of vaccine-related adverse events.
• Supporting systematic follow-up of individuals who experience adverse events so researchers can better understand potential causes, track long-term outcomes, and identify effective diagnostic and treatment approaches.
• Supporting clearer clinical guidance and care pathways for patients experiencing serious or long-term reactions.
• Ensuring that discussions of vaccine policy include acknowledgment of individuals living with these outcomes.

Acknowledging people who have experienced harm does not weaken public health efforts. Transparency, compassion, and continued research are essential to maintaining trust in public health systems.

Including patient-reported experiences in research and policy discussions can help strengthen vaccine safety monitoring and improve care for individuals affected by serious adverse events.

Those of us living with vaccine injuries are not simply data points. We are real people whose lives have been altered and who are still searching for answers, care, and understanding. Our presence in this process reflects lived suffering, unmet needs, and the importance of inclusive dialogue.

We do exist, and our experiences matter. I respectfully ask the committee to ensure that individuals living with vaccine injuries are considered when shaping future recommendations, safety monitoring systems, clinical guidance, and research priorities so that patients like me are not overlooked.

Title: Comment from Young, MD, Clarence
Comment:
To: Members of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practicies

I am a Board Internal Medicine and Infectious Diseases physician and was licensed to practice medicine for more than 40 years.

I have spent the bulk of my career leading teams and organizations that develop new medicines including: infectious diseases (antibacterial, antiviral and anti-protozoal agents), resulting in numerous regulatory filings and approvals in the US (FDA)and other countries throughout the world.

It is worth considering the devastating consequences to individuals of the diseases that these vaccines prevent alongside the decline or near disappearance of these diseases.

Meningococcal Vaccine
Meningococcemia is a rare but lethal disease. Unvaccinated children who avoid natural infection in early childhood remain at risk for this disease. A typical story is that of a young person who, awakens one morning with headache and flu-like symptoms leading within hours is hospitalized and fighting for their lives

Measles
I spent my eight week Pediatrics rotation on the Adolescent Ward at a time when the goal to achieve 90% vaccination rates for measles in school aged children was new. As One or two children – in their early teens, were awaiting nursing/convalescent home placement due to the debilitating neurologic complications of measles. A retired physician, recently mentioned that one such convalescent home, dedicated to the care of children with chronic encephalitis due to measles had only recently closed in his town.

COVID-19
As an Infectious Diseases physician, the experience of seeing seriously ill patients with influenza, during annual seasonal outbreaks, and the emergence of avian influenza in the early 2000s was reminder that it was only a matter of time before we would experience respiratory disease pandemic on the scale of the 1918 influenza pandemic.
When the COVID19 outbreak emerged in late 2019, the US was well-prepared, as a consequence of recognizing that the SARS outbreak in the early 2000s, with a case-fatality rate of nearly 10%, was a harbinger of things to come and merited US government support of efforts to develop a vaccine to a known lethal agent.

The approach involved synthesizing the RNA “message” that carried the instructions for assembling a key viral protein and packaging this so that it could be taken-up by the individual’s macrophages. The macrophages assembles and conveys this spike protein to the cell surface to facilitate its interaction with the patient’s lymphocytes responsible for generating the appropriate immune response.

This approach to vaccine manufacture avoids the tedious and inefficient processes associated with standard approaches to vaccine production, including the need to isolate and purify viral proteins from the complex mixtures of these present in whole viruses which could produce their own immune effects. This enables modification of key components of the vaccine, enabling rapid manufacture and scale-up while avoiding the dangers of growing large quantities of infectious virus, posing a threat to anyone involved in the manufacturing process.

The benefits of mRNA vaccine development were borne out by the rapid initiation of clinical trials within months of recognizing the outbreak and identifying and sequencing the virus responsible for this disease – and completion of well-controlled clinical trials that unequivocally demonstrated substantial reductions in risks for hospitalization and death. None of the other COVID-19 vaccines that were developed using standard approaches have provided similar levels of protection.

The development of the COVID19 mRNA vaccines stand as one of the most significant achievements in modern medicine.

Conclusions
We should be grateful to the pediatricians in the United States, who represent the first line of disease prevention efforts. I have no doubt that they spend more time educating patients and their families, compared with other, more highly-compensated medical subspecialities. Success, for them is measured by the satisfaction of seeing their patients grow to become healthy and productive adults and by the absence of diseases that no one ever hears about.

These are difficult times for some pediatricians, who find themselves exhausted each day by conversations with some (but not most) parents regarding recommended vaccine, while making the effort to convince these parents that they will continue to provide care for their loved ones.

The Advisory Committee on Immune Practices has an obligation to provide the most up-to-date, and scientifically credible guidance for the use of vaccines to prevent disease - representing the consensus of knowledgeable experts who care for children and with experience in interpreting the accumulated data on their benefits and risks – and implications for patient care.

It was extremely scary to say the least, so I consulted with my primary care doctor in hopes of getting help. I was left to figure it out on my own. I connected with a group of victims who were likewise injured. We all had similar symptoms and we were all in the same boat. From the start, I was hesitant to take the Covid vaccine. I was pressured into believing it was my duty to do what I should for the greater good. I was assured it was safe and effective. It is a struggle physically and emotionally to live with the reality that my body feels nothing like it did before I was vaccine injured. I feel nothing close to normal. Life does not stop when you are dealing with difficult health issues. The responsibilities of life still require you to function.

Today, I am blessed with a great group of doctors who are trying to help as best they can. As time passes, they are seeing more and more people with vaccine injuries and acknowledging us. Unfortunately, like many, my symptoms have progressed over the prolonged period of time that I went without help. These symptoms are very difficult to treat. My days now consist of doing my responsibilities while coping with the symptoms that have continued to progress over the last few years.

Please consider our suffering and unmet needs on behalf of all vaccine injured. Policies on vaccine recommendations and post adverse reaction care are critical for everyone. Research and informed consent play a critical role in making health care decisions that can affect health outcomes for the rest of our lives.

Title: Comment from Vaughan, Patrick
Comment:
Hi, my name is Patrick Vaughan, I am a scientist and engineer, and I possess no conflicts of interest.

The anti-vaxxer stance on the lack of safety of the covid-19 vaccines has focused on 3 points. The first being myocarditis, the second thromboembolisms (calling it the “clot shot”), and the third being the anti-vaxxer belief that the covid-19 vaccine is the cause of Long Covid. These points either target an incredibly small fraction of people, have been recently explained, or have no supporting evidence behind them.

For the claim the covid-19 vaccines cause Myocarditis, the signal was only seen in very young male children between 14-21 years of age. Of the small proportion of male children that develop this, the vast majority have the issue pass within a few days. Myocarditis was transient (passing within a short period of time) in ~90% of young male children, where it resolved on its own within 24-72 hours. Of the small percent of male children where it didn’t resolve within a few days, it could not definitively be associated as a direct response to receiving the covid vaccine. Additionally, from the covid safety data from the past 3 years, there has been no single for myocarditis above background levels observed.

For the claim that the covid-19 vaccine is a “clot shot”, aka it causes thromboembolism and thrombocytopenia, these signals have also never been associated with either the mRNA (Pfizer/Moderna) or protein-based (Novavax) platforms. It has however been associated with adenovirus-based covid-19 vaccines (Astrezeneca and J&J). These covid-19 vaccines were immediately noted for having these signals in 2021/2022, were pulled from the market, and are not available within the US. Furthermore, we now have direct scientific evidence for the cause of this outcome, and it is specifically due to a genetic predisposition and immune system interaction with the adenovirus component of those two vaccines. Notably, neither the mRNA nor the protein-based covid-19 vaccines possess this component. This is therefore not an outcome that is possible with the current available covid-19 vaccines.

There is also a misguided anti-vaxxer stance which has long persisted, the belief that the covid-19 vaccine is responsible for the development of Long Covid. There is no scientific evidence for this belief.

First, thousands of cases of long covid have been documented to have occurred before a covid-19 vaccine was even made available. Second, all studies have demonstrated that Long Covid rates are significantly reduced in vaccinated individuals as compared to unvaccinated. If the vaccine were the cause, we would not be seeing a signal for Long Covid in the unvaccinated, and we would instead see a higher rate of Long Covid in the vaccinated, with multi-vaccinated individuals having the highest rate of occurrence. We see the opposite within all the data. In fact, the number of infections is strongly correlated with the Long Covid rate, where the greater the number of infections, the higher the chance any individual will have Long Covid. Furthermore, many studies have shown that covid-19 vaccination following a Long Covid diagnosis has contributed to increased recovery rates from the illness. Finally, due to a lack of precautions (mask usage) and a lack of testing (confirmation of disease), it is far more likely in every practical sense that a person with Long Covid developed the condition from either an asymptomatic or mild infection that they ignored. Then due to anti-vaccine hysteria, such as what is pushed by the members of this ACIP committee, they then falsely believe that the vaccine they received recently (or even years prior) is the cause of their new disability. We know it is not.

In terms of Long Covid recommendations, we should be using the NASEM definition for Long Covid – an infection-associated chronic condition that occurs after SARS-CoV-2 infection and is present for at least 3 months as a continuous, relapsing and remitting, or progressive disease state that affects one or more organ systems. Additionally, Long Covid can be broken down into subtype classifications (predominance via cardiovascular complications, neurologic complications, metabolic complications, respiratory issues, musculoskeletal issues, gastrointestinal issues, kidney issues, and etc.). We have direct scientific evidence that covid-19 impacts nearly every body system in negative ways, regardless of the severity of lack thereof of initial infection. This is the definition that should be utilized for Long Covid, and this committee should not try to alter what is considered Long Covid in an attempt to downplay its true burden on the US population at large.

Title: Comment from Rackmill, Nicole
Comment:
My name is Nicole R., and I am writing in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199. As someone living with a life-altering vaccine injury, I urge the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to meaningfully incorporate patient experiences and real-world outcomes into its deliberations and policy recommendations.
Before April 2021, I was a healthy and highly active 52-year-old vegan, marathon runner, and avid hiker who practiced power yoga daily while working in a high-stress, fast-paced job in the healthcare industry. I had never contracted COVID-19 and was rarely ill. Wanting to follow public health guidance and protect my elderly parents, my community, and myself, I chose to be vaccinated.
On April 2, 2021, I drove two hours to the Javits Center in New York City, one of the government’s mass vaccination sites, and received my first—and only—COVID-19 vaccine dose. Like millions of Americans, I trusted public health authorities and believed vaccination would help bring the pandemic to an end.
Six days later, I developed an intense burning sensation in both arms and a metallic taste in my mouth. Soon after, I experienced muscle weakness, tremors, twitching, numbness, visual disturbances, confusion, and memory loss. Within a month, I had lost 17 pounds and was unable to perform basic activities of daily living. I immediately had to stop working and file for disability because I could no longer perform my job.
My primary physician initially minimized my concerns. He only took my symptoms seriously after the Janssen vaccine was paused due to reports of deaths from blood clots—just nine days after I had been vaccinated. Only after I insisted did he test my platelets. I was referred to a neurologist, who ruled out cervical spine disease, multiple sclerosis, and Guillain-Barré syndrome but had no explanation for my symptoms. At times, my condition was dismissed as psychological. My case was never reported to VAERS by my providers; I had to submit reports to V-SAFE and VAERS myself.
In the years since, I have consulted more than 40 medical specialists, including infectious disease physicians, neurologists, immunologists, and rheumatologists. Many were reluctant to acknowledge the possibility of vaccine injury, likely fearing professional or institutional repercussions. The few who recognized post-vaccination syndromes had limited guidance for treatment.
Today, my diagnoses include small fiber neuropathy, cognitive dysfunction, visual convergence disorder, postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome (POTS), and ME/CFS. I have also participated in research studies at Ivy League universities investigating the immunology of Post-Acute COVID Vaccination Syndrome and its overlap with Long COVID.
Nearly five years later, the consequences remain profound. I lost my career, depleted my life savings paying for medical care, and was forced to sell my home.
Individuals injured by COVID-19 vaccines have very limited financial recourse. Vaccine manufacturers are shielded from liability, and COVID vaccines were designated countermeasures, meaning claims must go through the Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program (CICP) rather than the Vaccine Injury Compensation Program (VICP). In practice, the CICP has proven woefully inadequate to those seeking compensation. As of March 1, 2026, 14,129 COVID-19 vaccine injury claims had been filed, yet only 44 had been compensated, and nearly a quarter were rejected because the one-year filing deadline had already passed.
The politicization of vaccines has created deep divisions and made it even harder for affected individuals to seek care and support. Those of us who report injuries are often labeled “anti-vaccine” or conspiracy theorists. In reality, we are people whose lives were irrevocably altered and who are seeking recognition, research, and treatment.
Vaccines are an important public health tool, but no medical intervention is without risk. Ignoring safety signals will never conquer vaccine hesitancy, and dismissing patient experiences undermines public trust.
My experience is a reminder that behind every adverse event report is a person whose life, as they once knew it, is forever changed—it is “rare” until it happens to you.
I respectfully ask the committee to closely examine vaccine recommendations, post-adverse-event care, and research priorities, and to ensure that those harmed receive recognition, research, and meaningful support to restore trust in America’s health institutions.
Thank you for your time and consideration.

Title: Comment from Frye, Angela
Comment: To Whom It May Concern:  My name is Angela Frye and I'm from a rural, red county in Southwest VA.  FTR, I am politically a Progressive Social Democrat, an environmental chemistry professional of 30 years, currently retired. My husband and I live on a small horse farm with many animals, enjoy(ed) an active retirement gardening, hiking, yoga prior to the COVID era, but our lives, particularly mine, have been dramatically impacted by the Pfizer BNT COVID vaccine.   I am writing to express care and concern about the vaccinations I willingly received in 2021 and 2022 for the benefit of my immediate family (husband with past MI and brother with COPD) and community at large.  I received a total of 4 Pfizer BNT vaccines.  I have retained date, location, and lot numbers of all  for any interested.  I experienced no problems with the first vaccine series (shots #1 and #2) unless problems I had during that time (IBS and UTIs) that I assume were NOT related actually were.  I experienced little problems with the second (booster #1, shot #3) but did have exacerbating UTI type symptoms and a period of what felt like mild, transient hyperthyroidism, even hypomania even though I have never before or since been diagnosed with bipolar illness.  I also noticed, during that timeframe, that my "walking around" heartrate was around 20 bpms faster than baseline.  My last vaccine was the one that proved extremely problematic and from a little research, I've determined that the vaccine that I got at that time may have been different from the first series and first booster in that it was omicron bivalent; whatever the difference or reason, my life has not been the same since and my quality of life all but destroyed. Roughly three weeks post I broke out into an all over itchy rash and began having 4-5 PM adrenaline dumps and a fatigue whose best description amounts to a feeling of being crushed.   Beyond CFS/ME type challenges, I've experienced:  eye issues, apparent hyperadrenergic POTs (still waiting on formal diagnosis, but intermittent BP and HR spikes), hospitalized twice for tachycardia now under the care of an EP with an installed loop recorder,  a chronic seasick/drunk feeling, severe brain fog, intermittent depression and anxiety, inappropriate weight loss then gain, severe headaches, new ocular migraines,  and head pressure and vacillating ANA's.  In the beginning I felt as if I were moving through an encephalopathy of an autoimmune or disease origin.  I was alert, oriented to time and place, not delusional and coherent, but experienced derealization and depersonalization.  I have experienced a worsening of idiopathic polycythemia but paradoxically very low iron status.  I've experienced skin changes, including several new fatty lipoma deposits appearing, a basal cell carcinoma that necessitated removal, and pre-cancerous lesions on my forehead.  The challenges that have come with this series of vaccinations are unrelenting and unreal, it's as if I literally stepped into a hell portal or nightmare in July of 2022 and while I engage in any number of exercises and mindfulness practice in order to keep living life the best that I am able, I must admit there are times that I am extraordinarily frightened of what will happen next or happen to me.  I appeal to the ENTIRE DHHS to very carefully consider those of us that have made up the subset of people whose lives were decimated by this vaccine.  My hopes and desires center around finding out why we reacted the way that we did (in my case past Lyme? Title: Comment from STEPHENS, KATIE
Comment:
To the Members of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices,

Thank you for the opportunity to provide public comment.

As a healthcare professional with many years of experience working within healthcare systems and public health environments, I believe that the long-term effectiveness of any program ultimately rests on public trust, transparency, and respect for patient autonomy.

Over the past several years, it has become clear that trust in many health institutions has been strained. Rebuilding that trust will require more than communication strategies—it will require a visible commitment to scientific transparency, independent oversight, and respect for individual medical decision-making.

One issue that deserves particular attention moving forward is patient autonomy and self-determination.

During the COVID-19 pandemic, many individuals experienced significant pressure related to vaccination decisions. In some settings, people felt stigmatized, judged, or socially marginalized for asking questions or for making different personal risk assessments. While these dynamics may have emerged in the context of an urgent public health response, the downstream effect has been a profound erosion of trust among many members of the public.

Medical ethics has long recognized informed consent and respect for patient autonomy as foundational principles of ethical care. Public health policy should reinforce—not undermine—those principles.

For this reason, I respectfully encourage the committee to prioritize the following considerations:

1. Reinforce Respect for Patient Autonomy and Informed Consent
Patients must feel free to discuss risks, benefits, and uncertainties openly with their clinicians without fear of stigma or social pressure. Policies and messaging should emphasize informed choice rather than coercion.

2. Encourage Open Scientific Dialogue
Scientific credibility is strengthened—not weakened—when questions and differing interpretations of evidence are examined openly. Encouraging transparent scientific discussion will help rebuild confidence among clinicians and the public.

3. Strengthen Conflict-of-Interest Safeguards
Visible and rigorous conflict-of-interest standards are essential for maintaining confidence in advisory bodies responsible for national vaccine recommendations.

4. Continue Careful Evaluation of Vaccine Safety Signals
Post-marketing surveillance and transparent investigation of adverse events are critical components of responsible vaccine policy.

The success of public health programs ultimately depends on the trust and cooperation of the communities they serve. Policies that respect individual autonomy, uphold rigorous scientific standards, and demonstrate independence from financial influence will go a long way toward restoring that trust.

Thank you for your service and for considering these comments.

Respectfully,
Katie Stephens, DNP, RN, NEA-BC

Title: Comment from Sciandra, Lucyann
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Forti, Ann
Comment:
For the past five years, through my work with React19—the largest organization in the United States advocating for individuals injured by the COVID-19 vaccines—I have been in continual contact with people across the country whose lives were devastated following COVID-19 vaccination. Adults and children alike are now facing life-long disabilities. Some individuals—including children—have died after taking the vaccine. Many others lost their health, their careers, and in some cases their ability to ever work again. Over these years, I have heard their stories and witnessed firsthand the systemic failures they encountered while seeking help.

Families that were once stable and productive have been financially and physically destroyed. Some have lost their entire life savings searching for treatments that might restore even a fraction of their former health, while others have been forced to sell their homes simply to survive. The suffering they have endured—and the cruelty many have faced from government health agencies, medical institutions, legislators, and even journalists—has been beyond anything I would have believed possible in modern America.

Some injured individuals have faced such profound despair—living in bodies that feel as though they are attacking themselves, with no clear path to treatment and being left completely abandoned and censored—that they have taken their own lives. The magnitude of this suffering must be acknowledged.

Early in 2021, federal health agencies were aware that serious vaccine injuries were occurring. Some injured individuals were flown to the NIH in Bethesda to be studied, with the promise that their conditions would be investigated and that the public would be informed. Instead, the research was quietly abandoned and the injured were left without answers. That silence led to devastating consequences

At the same time, widespread censorship took place across the country. Government officials worked with media platforms and technology companies to suppress discussion not only from the vaccine injured themselves, but also from physicians, scientists, and researchers raising legitimate concerns. This prevented the public from understanding what was happening in real time and discouraged many physicians from speaking openly.

The chilling effect within the medical community was real. I personally know individuals in our area who experienced serious health problems following COVID vaccination, and I also know physicians who believe those injuries are vaccine-related. Yet many doctors have been reluctant to speak publicly because of the professional backlash faced by physicians who questioned vaccine policy or reported concerning observations.

As a result, many injuries were never fully documented or investigated. Numerous patients report that their physicians did not file reports to the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System, and even when reports were filed, there was little to no follow-up.

Those who sought help through the federal government’s Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program encountered overwhelming barriers. Approximately 97 percent of claims have been rejected, leaving the vast majority of injured individuals without compensation, medical support, or even acknowledgment of what happened to them.

If restoring public trust is truly a goal, it cannot happen without openly acknowledging the injuries that have occurred and the institutional failures that followed. Trust cannot be rebuilt by ignoring the suffering of those who stepped forward in good faith.

Immediate action is required. These injuries must be openly researched without delay. Physicians and scientists must be encouraged—not discouraged—to investigate possible treatments and therapies. Proper medical classification must also be established so these injuries can be accurately tracked. Appropriate ICD-10 diagnostic codes should be created so clinicians can document vaccine injuries consistently and researchers can properly study their prevalence and outcomes.

The American people deserve honesty, and the injured deserve recognition, research, and care.

Until the profound harm—and the horror—that many Americans have endured is openly acknowledged, trust will not be restored.

Ann Forti
Host, React19 Podcast
React19 Community Outreach

Title: Comment from Kazee , Donna
Comment:
Family clustering of vaccine reactions and repeated vaccine non-response highlight the need for individualized risk assessment and true informed consent.

Summary Statement for ACIP Submission

Name: Donna Kazee
Subject: Family history of vaccine reactions and the need for individualized risk assessment

I am submitting this comment to support the testimony of my sister, Lesley Billings, who experienced serious health events following the Janssen COVID-19 vaccine in August 2021.

In addition to her experience, I want to highlight a broader pattern within our family. Multiple family members have experienced significant reactions following various vaccinations, including anaphylaxis, seizures, severe rashes, respiratory symptoms, gastrointestinal symptoms, high fever, and developmental regression in infants. Despite this pattern, prior reactions were rarely considered when additional vaccines were recommended.

My own medical history may illustrate the importance of individualized risk assessment. After receiving multiple doses of the measles-mumps-rubella (MMR) vaccine due to repeated findings of absent rubella immunity, I developed autoimmune symptoms later diagnosed as mixed connective tissue disease with suspected lupus. Since my fifth MMR vaccination, I have also experienced recurring episodes of rash, joint pain, and low-grade fever several times per year for more than twenty-five years.

These experiences raise concerns about how individual susceptibility, genetic factors, and family history of vaccine reactions are evaluated in clinical practice.

I respectfully ask the committee to consider the importance of:
• recognizing family patterns of adverse reactions
• researching genetic and biomarker-based risk factors
• improving adverse-event monitoring and follow-up
• developing clinical guidance for patients with prior reactions
• supporting true informed consent and individualized medical decision-making

Understanding why some individuals experience adverse reactions is essential for improving vaccine safety, maintaining public trust, and protecting vulnerable populations. A detailed written testimony describing these experiences is attached.

Title: Comment from Kusha, Barmak
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Edwards MD MPH, Karen
Comment:
As a retired pediatrician and public health professional and a faculty member in pediatrics and public health, I think it is very important to look at how we assess the benefits and possible risks of vaccines.
We should remember that
1) The current COVID vaccines have been well-tested and found to be both safe and effective, 2) The monitoring system that is in place is able to pick up any small negative impacts of the vaccine and remedy them and
3) Whenever we ask whether a negative health impact is caused by a vaccine, we must compare the incidence of the negative impact in those who receive the vaccine with the incidence of the same negative impact in those who are ill with the COVID virus. This is the essential comparison: is this negative impact more common in those who received the vaccine than in those who get the disease?
4) The Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS) accepts reports from anyone. These reports are not clinically verified and they do not establish that the vaccine caused the reported adverse event. VAERs own website (https://vaers.hhs.gov/about.html) states: “VAERS is a passive reporting system, meaning it relies on individuals to send in reports of their experiences to CDC and FDA. VAERS is not designed to determine if a vaccine caused a health problem, but is especially useful for detecting unusual or unexpected patterns of adverse event reporting that might indicate a possible safety problem with a vaccine. This way, VAERS can provide CDC and FDA with valuable information that additional work and evaluation is necessary to further assess a possible safety concern.” Therefore, it is absolutely not valid to rely only on the reports; the reports submitted must be investigated.

On another note, I was surprised to see that, in light of the current increase in measles cases around the country, that the agenda for this ACIP meeting does not include a discussion of how to respond to this. Measles is a serious illness with a high rate of hospitalization. Children still die from measles. Measles diminishes immunity to other childhood infectious diseases. The measles vaccine is safe and effective. The ACIP should be discussing strategies:
1) To assure that the public is aware of the increased prevalence of measles, of the serious negative short and long term consequences of childhood measles infection, and of the safety and effectiveness of the vaccine.
2) To make it easy for parents to get their children immunized against measles
3) To stop false negative messages about the measles vaccine coming out of the federal government
Thank you for your consideration on this matter

Title: Comment from The PAMAH Foundation
Comment:
Public Comment Submitted to the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP)
Submitted by Danielle Hodge – PAMAH Foundation

Introduction
My name is Danielle Hodge. I am submitting this comment on behalf of my family and the PAMAH Foundation, an organization dedicated to raising awareness about meningitis and supporting families affected by this devastating disease. Our family’s work is deeply personal. On July 26, 2018, we lost my daughter-in-law, Percilla Ann Marie April-Hodge, to pneumococcal meningitis.

Background
Percilla was a young mother, wife, daughter, and sister. She left behind three children who were only seven years old, five years old, and three months old at the time of her death. Her illness progressed with terrifying speed. What appeared to be a migraine quickly became a life-threatening emergency.
She woke up unable to recognize her husband and confused about who she was. She was rushed to the hospital where doctors quickly determined she had pneumococcal meningitis. Within hours she had suffered multiple strokes and was placed into a coma. Just twenty-four hours later, she passed away.

Analysis
Bacterial meningitis remains one of the most aggressive infectious diseases, capable of progressing from early symptoms to death in a matter of hours.
Even when patients survive, they may face lifelong complications such as:
Hearing loss
Neurological impairment
Vision loss
Amputations
Cognitive disabilities
Vaccination remains one of the most powerful public health tools available to prevent many forms of bacterial meningitis.
Strong immunization recommendations, public awareness, and access to vaccines are essential in preventing tragedies like the one our family experienced.

Recommendations
Based on our experience and our work through the PAMAH Foundation, we respectfully urge ACIP to:
Maintain strong evidence-based vaccine recommendations for pneumococcal and meningococcal vaccines.
Continue prioritizing public education regarding the severity and rapid progression of bacterial meningitis.
Support initiatives that improve vaccination awareness among parents, caregivers, and communities.
Encourage policies that increase vaccine accessibility and uptake.

Conclusion
Behind every meningitis statistic is a family like mine. Children growing up without their mother. A husband raising children alone. Parents burying a daughter far too soon. Our family cannot change what happened to Percilla, but by sharing her story we hope to prevent other families from experiencing the same devastating loss.

Thank you for considering this comment and for your continued work to protect public health.

Title: Comment from Kee, Charles
Comment:
My name is Charles Kee, and I am submitting this public comment in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199.

I am a person living with vaccine injury, and I urge the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to consider patient perspectives and real-world experiences in its deliberations. Our presence here reflects lived suffering, unmet needs, and the importance of inclusive dialogue. We do exist, and our voices deserve to be heard. I respectfully ask that you consider how policies on vaccine recommendation guidelines, post-adverse event care, and research priorities affect real people every day.

I received a single dose of the Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine on April 26, 2021. Within a short period of time afterward, I experienced severe medical complications and was hospitalized. In just over a month it will mark five years since that hospitalization, where I was diagnosed with pulmonary embolism, deep vein thrombosis (DVT), myocarditis, acute inflammatory response, neuropathy, vertigo, rapid weight gain, insomnia, and persistent brain fog.

Through my own research and connection with others who have experienced similar conditions, I have learned that these symptoms are not as rare as they are often portrayed. However, they are extremely difficult to treat and have had a profound impact on my life—physically, emotionally, and financially.

While I am grateful to have medical insurance, it does not cover many of the treatments that are known to help improve my condition. As a result, I have received little to no financial assistance in addressing the long-term consequences of these health issues.

As you consider new vaccine policies and protocols, I respectfully ask that patient safety remain the highest priority. This includes strengthening informed consent so individuals can make truly informed decisions, improving transparency in the approval process, and ensuring that systems are in place to support those who experience serious adverse events.

Rebuilding public trust requires acknowledging patient experiences, investing in research for treatment and recovery, and providing meaningful support for those who have been affected.

Thank you for considering my perspective and the experiences of others living with vaccine injuries.

Respectfully,

Charles Kee
Email: CKEE1370@gmail.com
Phone: (760) 420-7179

Title: Comment from UKCVFamily registered charity in the United Kingdom 1207178
Organization: UKCVFamily registered charity in the United Kingdom 1207178
Comment:
Comment in relation to: Department of Health and Human Services
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
[Docket No. CDC-2026-0199]
My name is Charlet Crichton, and I founded the UKCVFamily charity in 2021, after suffering an adverse reaction to a Covid-19 vaccination myself. Previously fit and healthy, I ran my own Sports Therapy Clinic, and was volunteering at a mass vaccination centre during the Pandemic. I now dedicate myself to running UKCVFamily, alongside 4 other trustees and over 20 volunteers, all of whom are directly affected themselves. Representing the vaccine-injured and -bereaved extensively to NHS bodies, government inquiries, scientists, researchers and politicians, the charity aims to raise awareness, and better support for our community. I attach a copy of the UKCVFamily Information Pack for your perusal.

I also represent the UK as a member of CVIA, alongside Caroline Pover ( UKCVFamily's Chair of Trustees ). The Covid Vaccine Injury Alliance (CVIA) is a coalition of national organisations and groups from around the world, each made up of citizens who have suffered harms or lost loved ones due to the COVID-19 vaccines. Collectively, our organisations represent hundreds of thousands of citizens who chose to get vaccinated upon the advice of their national public health authorities and in the interests of protecting themselves, their families and their communities.

UKCVFamily currently supports around 3,000 people in the United Kingdom who are affected, filling a void where, historically, there has been little recognition or support.

Neglect and discrimination has been brought to light through the Covid vaccination rollout and is now resulting in serious mistrust of institutions — especially government and healthcare. In order to rebuild trust from the general public — vital in the event of future health crises — we urgently need to have an effective and compassionate means of medically, practically, financially, and emotionally supporting the vaccine-injured and bereaved.

Thankyou to the CDC and ACIP for addressing the subject of vaccine-injury.

Title: Comment from Van Ackeren, Brenda
Comment: My husband, Dan Van Ackeren Jr., was permanently disabled after receiving the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine in late 2021. Before that shot, Dan was thriving?athletic, successful, and full of life. He was a Doctor of Chiropractic with an MBA whose company had once been on the Inc. 500 list. When I met him, he was constantly playing sports, working hard, and being an incredible father to our blended family. Life was good! Then COVID arrived. Dan was hesitant, but he trusted what our government and medical system told him, that the vaccine was "safe and effective". So he took the two Pfizer shots. After the second dose, everything changed. He began screaming in pain daily. He vomited constantly. He lost bladder control. His hands and feet would turn blue. We went to ER after ER, doctor after doctor?dozens in total. They dismissed him. In November 2021, I found him unresponsive. He was rushed to the ICU and intubated for three days. I told the doctors this began after the Pfizer shot, but instead of investigating we were dismissed and gaslit. Two days after leaving the ICU, they sent him to the psychiatric floor for evaluation and claimed he was trying to harm himself. We were in disbelief of how he was being treated. The man I married?a Texas tennis champion?is now completely bedridden, living in a dark room alone. He cannot drive. He cannot work. He cannot even put on his own shoes or sit in a chair longer than 10 minutes. After dozens of specialists, multiple spine surgeries, and endless testing, doctors have diagnosed severe autonomic failure and neurological damage, including dysautonomia, POTS, EDS, microclots, chronic fatigue, cervical instability, and brain changes associated with Parkinson?s and dementia. The financial toll has been devastating. My husband was forced to file bankruptcy, and after years of medical bills and lost income, I am now on the verge of bankruptcy myself. We have even lost our health insurance. Every day he suffers extreme pain, sweats, chills, and relentless fight-or-flight symptoms. I am watching the man I love suffer and slowly die. The injured deserve acknowledgment. They deserve their medical care covered, access to real treatments, and compensation for the lives and careers taken from them. My husband trusted his government. Now I?m asking: will our government take responsibility for the injured and dead who were left behind?

Title: Comment from Vaccine Integrity Project
Comment:
This COVID vaccine summary reflects findings from the Vaccine Integrity Project’s systematic review and meta-analysis published in the New England Journal of Medicine titled “Updated Evidence for Covid-19, RSV, and Influenza Vaccines for 2025–2026.” The review evaluated peer-reviewed evidence on vaccine efficacy, effectiveness, and safety relevant to vaccines used in the United States. The evidence base leveraged randomized trials, observational effectiveness studies, and vaccine safety surveillance analyses published through mid-2025, reflecting data accumulated since CDC’s Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) last review.

Effectiveness
Across multiple study designs and populations, COVID-19 vaccines continue to provide meaningful protection against severe disease outcomes. Observational analyses during recent respiratory virus seasons show that updated vaccines reduce the risk of COVID-19–associated hospitalization by approximately 46% to 68% (depending on the study and the variant), with somewhat lower effectiveness among immunocompromised adults.

Additional analyses of the 2024–2025 vaccine formulation found protection against severe clinical outcomes, including emergency department visits, hospitalization, and death. These benefits were observed across age groups and clinical risk profiles, indicating that vaccination continues to add protection even in populations with substantial pre-existing immunity from prior infection or vaccination.

Although protection against infection wanes over time and may vary with circulating variants, the most consistent and clinically meaningful benefit of vaccination is protection against severe outcomes such as hospitalization and death.

Safety
Extensive safety monitoring systems in the United States and internationally—including large observational datasets and pharmacovigilance systems—continue to support a favorable safety profile for COVID-19 vaccines. For example, in 2021, among millions of people who received the Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine, the system detected a rare but potentially fatal condition that forms blood clots, causing the CDC and FDA to recommend a pause in its use and ultimately issue a preference for mRNA vaccines.

Rare adverse events have been identified and quantified through these monitoring systems. For example, myocarditis following mRNA vaccination was observed primarily in adolescent and young adult males, with estimated incidence rates of approximately 1.3 to 3.1 cases per 100,000 doses. Most reported cases have been mild and resolved with treatment, and risk was reduced with longer intervals between doses, which now stands as the current recommendation. Findings from our most recent review, for example, noted that for the Pfizer mRNA vaccine, “the adjusted odds ratio decreased from 6.5 (95% CI, 3.8 to 11) when the third dose was given less than 153 days after the second dose to 1.6 (95% CI, 0.61 to 4.2) when the third dose was given more than 213 days after the second dose; findings were generally consistent with the results with [the Moderna mRNA vaccine].” These estimates suggest that a longer interval between repeated mRNA vaccine doses decreased the odds of developing myocarditis.

No credible evidence from large population studies or surveillance systems indicates widespread serious safety concerns attributable to COVID-19 vaccines. Among over 420 studies reviewed in the 2025-26 season on COVID-19 vaccines–in addition to the prior evidence reported by ACIP–overall safety findings across randomized trials and post-authorization monitoring continue to support a favorable benefit-risk balance for recommended populations.

Summary
The totality of peer-reviewed evidence through July 2025 demonstrates that COVID-19 vaccines continue to provide meaningful protection against severe disease while maintaining a strong safety profile. While effectiveness against infection may vary with viral evolution and population immunity, vaccination remains an important tool for reducing severe COVID-19 outcomes. Continued monitoring and transparent evidence review are essential components of vaccine policy and public health decision-making.

Title: Comment from McAnally, Jennifer
Comment:
Both of my sons have a diagnosis of autism spectrum disorder. I haven’t read most of the comments posted here, but I suspect that they largely consist of parents who strongly believe that vaccines had something to do with their child’s autism diagnosis. They may be right but I want to add our experiences for the record.

I used to be one of those parents. So convinced was I that vaccines might have had something to do with my older son’s autism that decided to forego vaccinations for my younger son.

He seemed to progress normally until he was around 22 months, when he developed a high fever with vomiting and diarrhea. For two days he ate nothing and threw up often. I had some acetaminophen suppositories from a previous illness, so I decided to give him one. Within a few hours, he was back to his normal playful self…but I also noticed that the things he had enjoyed eating previously he no longer seemed interested in. I figured after being sick, he would just need some time for his appetite to return.

Over time, though, he quit meeting developmental milestones and the picky eating and behavioral outbursts worsened.

At the age of five, when his speech delays, behavioral outbursts and difficulties in school were starting to cause real problems, and his doctors confirmed that he did indeed fall on the autism spectrum, I could no longer deny that blaming vaccines for my older son’s autism might have been a mistake.

So I put the idea out of my mind and quit visiting those anti-vaccine sites and focused on helping my boys.

I enlisted the help of a behavioral analyst. She suggested that we use a reinforcer of some sort during the therapy sessions and I chose skittles. I knew that the boys loved them but I had eliminated these from our house a couple of years prior because the boys had both developed several cavities.

Things seemed to be going well until a few days went by and my older son with autism who was 14 at the time, found the bag of skittles and ate the whole thing. Over the course of a few days, his behaviors began to change. Ordinarily very passive and quiet, he suddenly became aggressive, pulling my hair, shrieking, throwing objects and pounding his head into the floor, drenched in sweat, pupils dilated. I was terrified. I suspected immediately that the skittles had something to do with this sudden onset of behaviors since it was the only new thing in the environment, so of course I turned to the internet and found a few studies that confirmed that there was an association between food dyes and behavioral outbursts. Ah…ok.

So we implemented some dietary changes, focusing mainly on eliminating dyes. Off and on over the course of a few weeks, however, my son continued to have these outbursts and I suspected he might actually be having pain, so we tried Tylenol for what I believed must have been headaches. His pediatrician even seemed to agree that his rapid onset of behaviors might be pain related and prescribed him Tylenol #3. The behaviors seemed to lessen somewhat, so we continued the Tylenol as needed until the behaviors actually started to worsen, resulting in a trip to the state hospital.

This would be when I would enter “Tylenol and autism” into the search engine and discover Schultz’s 2008 study and then suddenly, everything made sense.

Reading more, I would learn about the work of Rosemary Waring and her research on sulfation problems in autistic children. What she described was exactly what I observed in my boys.

I began to understand why parents blamed vaccines, as I remembered that Tylenol was administered before and after my older son’s vaccine appointments. My older son was born prematurely, constantly sick and received Tylenol often in his first year of life.

I always thought it odd that medication should be given to a baby for fevers that hadn’t even occurred yet but I trusted that my son’s doctors knew what they were doing.

Years later, I would learn about the television show “Vaccine Roulette” and the flood of lawsuits from parents claiming that the old DTP vaccine caused permanent brain damage in their children. Vaccine manufacturers and public health officials, were understandably alarmed about lawsuits, decreased uptake of the vaccine and an increase in whooping cough.

Notably, in 1987, the ACIP made the recommendation for prophylactic acetaminophen for children with a family history of seizures. The reasoning seemed to be if the fever could be prevented, this might also prevent the seizure.

These recommendations coincided with a confirmed causal association between aspirin and Reye’s Syndrome in 1986 with a sudden shift to Tylenol.

Schultz’s study on acetaminophen use following the MMR vaccine was immediately dismissed by most and largely forgotten about. But he raised an important question that remains unanswered.

Is the increased use of Tylenol in babies & young children what triggered the autism epidemic?

Title: Comment from Ernst, Karen
Comment:
In the current public conversation, there are increasing claims that COVID-19 vaccines cause widespread injury. The evidence does not support that claim. Serious vaccine-related adverse events remain rare. Severe allergic reactions occur in only a few cases per million doses. Myocarditis, the most frequently discussed serious adverse event, occurs at a rate of roughly 1–3 cases per 100,000 doses in the demographic group at highest risk. Even in that group, the vast majority of people who receive the vaccine do not experience this complication, and the risk can be further reduced through dosing strategies.

At the same time, updated COVID-19 vaccines continue to provide meaningful protection against severe outcomes. Real-world data consistently show that updated vaccines reduce the risk of hospitalization by roughly 40–50% among adults. Among adults aged 65 and older, who remain at the highest risk of severe disease, protection against hospitalization is even higher, around 56%. Vaccination also provides measurable protection against death, particularly among older adults and people with underlying health conditions.

Although the acute phase of the pandemic has passed, COVID-19 continues to cause significant illness and hospitalization each year. Removing or restricting access to vaccination would not eliminate that risk; it would simply remove a layer of protection that many people still depend on, especially older adults, immunocompromised individuals, and those living with chronic disease.

Equally important, adults deserve the ability to make informed decisions about their own health. Vaccine policy should preserve access to safe and effective preventive tools so that individuals and families can decide what level of protection is right for them. For many people, particularly those caring for vulnerable family members, having the option to vaccinate provides reassurance and control over their health risks.

The appropriate response to rare risks is transparent communication, careful monitoring, and evidence-based recommendations. Ongoing safety surveillance systems are functioning as intended and continue to provide important data that can guide policy. That transparency strengthens public trust.

For these reasons, I encourage ACIP to maintain recommendations that preserve access to updated COVID-19 vaccines for adults and children, particularly for populations at higher risk of severe disease. Ensuring continued access enables individuals to make informed choices while maintaining a strong, evidence-based public health response.

CDC-2026-0199. I would like to report a series of adverse health events that began shortly after receiving a COVID-19 vaccine. I am sharing this experience so that it can be documented and potentially contribute to better understanding of vaccine reactions.

Before 2024, I had chosen not to receive a COVID-19 vaccine. My decision was based on prior adverse reactions I had experienced from two other vaccines earlier in my life. Despite this, I was required by U.S. immigration authorities to receive a COVID-19 vaccine in January 2024 as part of my green card process. I was informed that without receiving at least one dose within approximately three weeks, I could face deportation despite being married to a U.S. citizen.

Because of concerns about safety, I chose the Novavax COVID-19 vaccine, as it was presented as a more traditional protein-based option.

Approximately three days after receiving the vaccine, I began experiencing a cascade of symptoms that developed over the following weeks and months. In total, I experienced more than 20 different symptoms affecting multiple systems in my body. These included significant fatigue, neurological symptoms, inflammatory reactions, and other health changes that were not present prior to vaccination.

When I sought help from conventional medical providers, my concerns were often dismissed and several doctors told me they did not believe the symptoms could be related to the vaccine. Because of this, I had difficulty receiving medical support through traditional channels.

As a result, I sought help through naturopathic and homeopathic practitioners. Over the course of the two years, I have spent several thousand dollars on consultations, testing, and supplements in an effort to manage the symptoms and regain my health.

Although some symptoms have improved, I continue to struggle with ongoing adverse effects to this day.

Beyond the physical health impact, this experience has also had a significant emotional and social toll. It has been extremely difficult to cope with symptoms that are not widely recognized or believed. Most of my friends and family members have dismissed my experience, laughed at my concerns, or avoided discussing the issue entirely. This has been isolating during an already challenging health situation.

I am submitting this comment so that experiences like mine are documented. I believe it is important for health authorities to continue investigating adverse reactions and to ensure that individuals who experience them are heard, supported, and taken seriously.

Thank you for considering this report.
