Title: Comment from Pennsylvania Immunization Coalition
Comment:
The Health and Human Services (HHS) and Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) movement hope to restore American faith in public health agencies, yet that trust appears to be worsening. The Annenberg Public Policy Center’s recent poll shows that two-thirds of Americans (67%) have confidence in career scientists working at U.S. federal health agencies, compared with just 43% confidence in agency leaders overall. Public trust in the Centers for Disease Control (CDC), Federal Drug Administration (FDA), and National Institutes of Health (NIH) dropped significantly from 2024 (74%-76%) to 2026 (60-62%).

Trust relies upon transparency, competence, consistency, integrity and caring/benevolence. The January 5th sudden unilateral changes in the childhood immunization schedule, the 2025 abrupt removal of all 17 standing Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) members and replacements with hand-picked individuals and the changes in how ACIP meetings are led are eroding the trust of the American public regarding CDC/ACIP ability to protect and promote their health.

The ACIP is meeting today to discuss COVID-19 vaccine injuries, long COVID, and changes to the committee's recommendation methodology. The Pennsylvania Immunization Coalition (PAIC) requests ACIP follow its charter and not change methodology, and that it evaluate vaccine “injuries” with regard to current vaccines (not former), as well as clinically evaluating for cause, not just use public reports. PAIC also asks that ACIP look at the evidence and impact of vaccines in lowering the incidence of long COVID.

Grading of Recommendations, Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) and Evidence to Recommendations (EtR) methodology of evaluating vaccines is in the ACIP charter as the standardized process for determining the quality and strength of evidence for ACIP recommendations. It enhances transparency, consistency, and communication as reported on cdc.gov’s website and the 2023 ACIP Charter. The ACIP meetings should reflect a transparent and honest discussion of all of the evidence which should be available in advance of the meeting for the public to view, and follow GRADE methodology. Adult and childhood vaccine schedules should follow the ACIP Charter as well. Schedules should not be changed without votes from the committee and without public transparency. Per its charter, ACIP recommendations are focused on the control of vaccine-preventable diseases, whereas FDA generally focuses its review on the safety and efficacy of vaccines and the processes used to manufacture them. The U.S. has multiple surveillance systems in place to evaluate vaccines post licensure, including V-safe, the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS), the Vaccine Safety Datalink (VSD), and the Clinical Immunization Safety Assessment Network (CISA), among others. Remember that VAERS accepts reports from anyone. They are not clinically verified. They do not establish that a vaccine caused the reported event.

Myocarditis following original mRNA COVID-19 vaccination occurred. Rates were highest in males aged 16 to 17 and with the 2nd and booster doses of the original vaccine. They were mild and self-limiting. Infection with Covid-19 caused higher rates of myocarditis that sometimes were more severe. Covid-19 illness also resulted occasionally in multisystem inflammatory syndrome in children (MIS-C), which was not seen after vaccination. The cardiac manifestations due to the COVID infections whether in the form of viral myocarditis or due to MIS-C has caused many hospitalizations in children, some severe enough to need ICU treatment.

The present environment has changed with regard to Covid-19 population immunity, circulating variants and current vaccines. Data presented at the June 2025 ACIP meeting confirmed no increased risk of all-cause mortality, cardiac mortality, or non-COVID mortality. PAIC requests that ACIP follow current vaccines and not look to former (and no longer available) vaccines when discussing Covid-19 vaccine “injuries”. Long Covid is also real. Vaccination protects against that.
The mission of the Pennsylvania Immunization Coalition (PAIC) is to encourage all recommended, age-appropriate immunizations for all Pennsylvania residents across their life span. The science in support of immunizations has not changed. Vaccine preventable diseases are on the rise in the USA, while vaccine uptake continues to decline. Vaccines are the best tool that we have to prevent their counterpart diseases, and recommendations by health care providers and public health leaders are key to vaccine uptake. Multiple changes in vaccine policy which is not backed by scientific evidence causes confusion, hesitancy and erosion in trust. PAIC requests ACIP support current vaccines using evidence and transparency.

Title: Comment from Williams Keeshin, Susana
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Vaughan, Patrick
Comment:
Hi, my name is Patrick, I am a scientist and engineer, and I possess no conflicts of interest.

The anti-vaxxer stance on the lack of safety of the covid-19 vaccines has focused on 3 points. The first being myocarditis, the second thromboembolisms (calling it the “clot shot”), and the third being the anti-vaxxer belief that the covid-19 vaccine is the cause of Long Covid. These points either target an incredibly small fraction of people, have been recently explained, or have no supporting evidence behind them.

For the claim the covid-19 vaccines cause Myocarditis, the signal was only seen in very young male children between 14-21 years of age. Of the small proportion of male children that develop this, the vast majority have the issue pass within a few days. Myocarditis was transient (passing within a short period of time) in ~90% of young male children, where it resolved on its own within 24-72 hours. Of the small percent of male children where it didn’t resolve within a few days, it could not definitively be associated as a direct response to receiving the covid vaccine. Additionally, from the covid safety data from the past 3 years, there has been no single for myocarditis above background levels observed.

For the claim that the covid-19 vaccine is a “clot shot”, aka it causes thromboembolism and thrombocytopenia, these signals have also never been associated with either the mRNA (Pfizer/Moderna) or protein-based (Novavax) platforms. It has however been associated with adenovirus-based covid-19 vaccines (Astrezeneca and J&J). These covid-19 vaccines were immediately noted for having these signals in 2021/2022, were pulled from the market, and are not available within the US. Furthermore, we now have direct scientific evidence for the cause of this outcome, and it is specifically due to a genetic predisposition and immune system interaction with the adenovirus component of those two vaccines. Notably, neither the mRNA nor the protein-based covid-19 vaccines possess this component. This is therefore not an outcome that is possible with the current available covid-19 vaccines.

There is also a misguided anti-vaxxer stance which has long persisted, the belief that the covid-19 vaccine is responsible for the development of Long Covid. There is no scientific evidence for this belief.

First, thousands of cases of long covid have been documented to have occurred before a covid-19 vaccine was even made available. Second, all studies have demonstrated that Long Covid rates are significantly reduced in vaccinated individuals as compared to unvaccinated. If the vaccine were the cause, we would not be seeing a signal for Long Covid in the unvaccinated, and we would instead see a higher rate of Long Covid in the vaccinated, with multi-vaccinated individuals having the highest rate of occurrence. We see the opposite within all the data. In fact, the number of infections is strongly correlated with the Long Covid rate, where the greater the number of infections, the higher the chance any individual will have Long Covid. Furthermore, many studies have shown that covid-19 vaccination following a Long Covid diagnosis has contributed to increased recovery rates from the illness. Finally, due to a lack of precautions (mask usage) and a lack of testing (confirmation of disease), it is far more likely in every practical sense that a person with Long Covid developed the condition from either an asymptomatic or mild infection that they ignored. Then due to anti-vaccine hysteria, such as what is pushed by the members of this ACIP committee, they then falsely believe that the vaccine they received recently (or even years prior) is the cause of their new disability. We know it is not.

In terms of Long Covid recommendations, we should be using the NASEM definition for Long Covid – an infection-associated chronic condition that occurs after SARS-CoV-2 infection and is present for at least 3 months as a continuous, relapsing and remitting, or progressive disease state that affects one or more organ systems. Additionally, Long Covid can be broken down into subtype classifications (predominance via cardiovascular complications, neurologic complications, metabolic complications, respiratory issues, musculoskeletal issues, gastrointestinal issues, kidney issues, and etc.). We have direct scientific evidence that covid-19 impacts nearly every body system in negative ways, regardless of the severity of lack thereof of initial infection. This is the definition that should be utilized for Long Covid, and this committee should not try to alter what is considered Long Covid in an attempt to downplay its true burden on the US population at large.

Title: Comment from Lansky, Amy
Comment:
I have no conflicts of interest to disclose. I am writing to express my concerns about the potential for ignoring or changing the methodology used by ACIP.

I am a scientist who served for nearly 30 years at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). From 2023-2025 I was the Director of CDC’s Office of Scientific Evidence and Recommendations, which had as its mission to develop and apply agency-wide standards for development of guidelines and recommendations. Based on my experience with GRADE and Evidence to Recommendation frameworks for guidelines development, the rigor and acceptability of these methodologies are essential for effectively addressing vaccine preventable diseases and ensuring the health of all Americans. ACIP should reaffirm its commitment to a recommendation methodology that is grounded in a long-standing, formal evidence-based framework, not personal anecdotes or ad hoc judgement.

ACIP adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework in 2010 to ensure its vaccine recommendations are based on systematic evidence review and transparent evaluation of benefits and harms. Then, in 2018, ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework, adding a structured process to evaluate factors such as the public health problem, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives, and equity before making recommendations. These frameworks were adopted precisely to improve transparency, consistency, and communication.

ACIP should preserve use of GRADE to ensure that vaccine recommendations are based on a systematic assessment of the certainty and quality of evidence. ACIP also should preserve use of EtR to ensure that the committee explicitly addresses each decision domain, with data, before making a recommendation.

ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework.

Title: Comment from Hopkins, Jodi
Comment:
CDC Public Comment Letter
Re: Docket No. CDC-2026-0199
Subject: Patient Perspective on Severe Post-Vaccination Adverse Events

My name is Jodi., and I am submitting this comment in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199.

My husband is living with a severe COVID-19 vaccine injury, and I urge the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices to fully consider the experiences of patients and families affected by serious adverse events when making vaccine recommendations.

Watching my husband’s health collapse after receiving the COVID-19 vaccine has been the most difficult experience of my life. He is only 47 years old, yet he now depends on a pacemaker to keep his heart functioning. The injury has affected nearly every system in his body. His nervous system is severely dysregulated, his body’s electrical system misfires, and he lives in a constant state of sympathetic overload and PTSD. Each day he struggles with pain, weakness, relentless fatigue, depression, and cognitive impairment that has taken away parts of the person he once was.

Within months of receiving the COVID-19 vaccination, my husband began experiencing significant adverse health reactions. Since his symptoms began, he has required care from numerous medical specialists—including cardiology, cardiac electrophysiology, pulmonology, gastroenterology, naturopathic medicine, chiropractic care, and a doctor of natural medicine—and has required multiple emergency room visits. Despite extensive medical evaluation across multiple specialties, his health has not returned to what it was before vaccination.

Genetic testing has also shown that my husband carries a double variant of the MTHFR gene, inherited from both parents. This raises additional questions about whether certain individuals with underlying genetic differences may respond differently to vaccination.

As medicine increasingly recognizes the importance of genetics and personalized health, it is important that vaccine safety research also examine whether certain genetic profiles—such as variants in the MTHFR gene—may influence how individuals respond to vaccination.

This injury has not only affected his health—it has reshaped our entire lives. What once felt like a normal future has been replaced by constant medical uncertainty, daily limitations, and the emotional weight of watching someone you love struggle to live in a body that no longer functions the way it once did.
Behind every vaccine injury statistic is a real person whose life has been permanently changed. My husband is one of those people.

My husband received the COVID-19 vaccine because it was required in order for him to keep his job. As a result of that mandate, he is now living with severe and ongoing health consequences. Our experience illustrates the profound human impact that public health policies can have when adverse events occur.
Policy discussions about vaccine recommendations, adverse event monitoring, and research priorities are not abstract topics. They determine whether people who suffer serious reactions are acknowledged, studied, and supported—or overlooked.

I respectfully ask the committee to ensure that:
• Patient experiences and vaccine injury cases are meaningfully considered in future deliberations
• Research into serious adverse events is expanded and prioritized
• Care pathways and medical support are improved for individuals experiencing post-vaccine injury

Families like mine are living every day with the consequences of these policies. My husband followed public health guidance, and our lives were permanently changed as a result. Public health must include those who are harmed, not only those who are protected.

Thank you for taking the time to consider the perspective of patients and families affected by vaccine injury.

Sincerely,
Jodi

Title: Comment from Caregiver Action Network
Comment:
March 11, 2026

Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices
Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
Department of Health and Human Services
1600 Clifton Road NE, Mailstop H21-12
Atlanta, Georgia 30329-4027

Re: [CDC-2026-0199]

Dear Members of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices,
On behalf of Caregiver Action Network (CAN), the nation’s leading family caregiver organization, I appreciate the opportunity to provide comments in advance of ACIP’s upcoming meeting on March 18–19, 2026, to discuss COVID vaccine injuries and Long COVID and ACIP recommendation methodology.

Family caregivers are the unheralded backbone of our nation’s health system. More than 63 million Americans provide essential, largely unpaid support to family and loved ones with chronic, disabling, or serious health conditions.

While caregivers are always crucial, their role on the frontlines was never more evident than during the COVID pandemic. Managing complex medical needs at home and shifting public health guidance, many caregivers were charged with making decisions to protect immunocompromised loved ones while navigating incomplete information, a strained system, and urgency from third parties.

As the CEO of CAN, I proudly advocate on behalf of these individuals — all of whom have a unique and personal experience navigating caregiving. And what I have learned through my time at CAN is that we cannot rely on a single tool to address the multitude of health care challenges our nation’s caregivers face.

With the extensive support our caregivers provide, we owe it to them to expand access to all of the treatment options they and their loved ones need to stay healthy. While vaccines are one technology, other innovative, preventive approaches such as monoclonal antibodies (mAbs) are emerging as a critical part of the public health solution.

These innovations offer another layer of protection for our seniors, immunocompromised patients, caregivers, and individuals who may not want or be able to take a traditional vaccine. A system that supports communities more susceptible to viral illnesses may also reduce disease burden, health care costs, and health care utilization.

Our caregivers and their loved ones deserve options in how they choose to stay healthy and protect themselves from diseases such as COVID, and we need a regulatory and advisory framework that supports individual health care needs.

Clear, evidence-based recommendations from ACIP play a pivotal role in determining approval, insurance coverage, patient access, and public confidence. Without strong and inclusive recommendations that accommodate both traditional vaccines and innovative technologies like mAbs, families and caregivers may be inadvertently pushed out of the preventive care system.

On behalf of our nation’s caregivers, I urge the Committee to adopt a framework that includes emerging preventive options, like monoclonal antibodies, that enable families to make the choice that best suits their unique health needs.
Thank you for your consideration of these important issues.

Marvell Adams
CEO, Caregiver Action Network
Washington, DC

Title: Comment from Anonymous, Anonymous

I'd like to speak on my family's experience with Covid vaccination since that is a focus of this meeting. The most heartbreaking story is about my mother-in-law. She'd had cancer and had gone into remission about 12-13 years prior. Every year following, she'd get scanned to be sure the cancer was staying at bay, all were clear. She'd had a scan in January of 2021, with all clear results. She was vaccinated with Moderna in April and May of that year and had symptoms of her cancer's return by June. It came back and with a vengeance. She hung on until we lost her in January of 2023, she was only 66-years-old.

My father received the Pfizer shots in April and May of 2021 as well. Later that summer he ended up with a heart attack and triple bypass surgery. Did the vaccines have anything to do with that? Not sure, though the timing is curious.

My brother-in-law was diagnosed with vasculitis in late 2021 after coming down with an awful full body rash. His doctors told him it was due to Covid the illness.

My brother started to develop some neurological symptoms in 2020, mostly having to do with his voice. This couldn't have been contributed to the Covid shots due to the timing, but he did receive at least 4 doses starting in the spring of 2021, I'm not sure how many total he has had. I'm wondering if his initial issues were from his yearly flu shot, and I'm sure the subsequent Covid shots didn't help his condition. He was eventually diagnosed with PLS, a slower form of ALS, muscle weakness and balance issues ensued.

I know of so many more friends and acquaintances with similar stories. This product should never have gone on the market in the first place. It needs to go. The harm greatly exceeds any tiny benefit it may have. Thank you.

Title: Comment from Anderson, E. Andria
Comment: My name is Andria and I’ve been hit hard by the C-19 Moderna vaccine. I’ve lost my job, and haven’t driven in several yrs now. I have Lyme, mold/mycotoxins, CIRS, EBV, POTS, MCAS, tinnitus, neuro issues, had teeth extracted, have vitreous eye detachments, abdominal pain, entire body is in pain, head pressure and hip and neck pain, amyloid fibrin microclots, internal vibrations and more. I’ve had three ambulances called, around seven ER visits and been from Dr to Dr for answers and trying to get help. I pulled out retirement to use instead of using monthly pymts, as I needed medical help, and that went quickly. We all need help, compensation and justice of something we were told was safe and effective., that should have never been mandated. We just need our lives back, and to be this kind of sick, isn’t normal, it’s like your body has been poisoned. I just need me back before I don’t make it. Please get proper testing and help for all those suffering. Please do something, anything please.

Title: Comment from Larson, Rebecca
Comment:
I am commenting on Docket No. CDC-2026-0199.

I'm a mother of several children who have asthma. Asthma is a risk factor for severe outcomes from respiratory diseases, including covid-19. Each of my children received the initial covid-19 vaccine series, and they keep up-to-date on their annual covid-19 booster shots. My children, along with the larger public, deserve access to covid-19 vaccines to protect themselves from serious disease. Research studies have shown that the updated covid-19 vaccines reduce urgent care visits and hospitalizations in children by 65 - 80%. I have confidence in our vaccine monitoring systems to track real-time outcomes. Those monitoring systems have shown that myocarditis is a rare outcome in young children. Further, young people face a higher and longer-lasting risk of developing myocarditis after covid-19 infection than they do from vaccination against the disease. I strongly urge you to preserve access to the covid-19 vaccine, for my children, and for those in the public who likewise choose to be vaccinated against covid-19.

Title: Comment from Blum, Jonathan
Comment:
Three months ago, the FDA announced that at least 10 individuals had died due to the COVID-19 vaccine. Since then, no information has been shared with the public to allow assessment of this claim. It is stated that this conclusion was derived from VAERS data. It is well known that this database cannot be used to establish association between a vaccine and an outcome, let alone causation. The plural of anecdote is not evidence.

There is also no indication that this data has been reviewed by actual vaccine safety experts.

If many of these deaths occurred in 2021-2022, and if they are attributed to myocarditis (both speculation on my part due to lack of public access to the evidence), the committee should note that the rate of myocarditis has plummeted in subsequent years, as presented to you in June, 2025. Using pre-2023 data to formulate policy would be unwise.

Title: Comment from Wingate-Gaiser, Diana
Comment:
Dear Members of ACIP,

For the first time ever I am encouraged to write to this committee. Twenty-two years ago my 15 month old healthy son took the MMR bundle and within weeks regressed in all motors skills and lost his language. Twenty years ago my youngest son received the Dtap and within hours regressed and lost ALL skills. You see I didn't know enough, quick enough as a first time
vulnerable mother. Our life trajectory changed forever by going to a "well visit".

Now it's 2026 and what's happened between then and now is nothing short of a life robbed of health, finances, a stable job and benefits and a decade of trauma, heartache, therapy, functional medicine appointments, specialized diets and mental health challenges. Let's add isolation to this list; people
aren't knocking or calling offering any help after the infant stage. I can count on one hand. Bobby Kennedy is one of them. Although, I don't have a personal relationship with him it was as if he was family. He listened to mothers and never turned back. Thank God he is now the HHS secretary.

This brings my opening full circle. For the first time ever I am encouraged to write to this new ACIP committee. Thank you for graciously accepting this appointment and serving with dignity and respect and in truth and credibility. Critical thinking skills are finally back! I'm grateful for your service.

I'd like to share my comments from Oct 18, 2022. Please understand this was written after my own lived life experience with my sons coupled with the insanity of COVID and years of watching ACIP push vaccines even though they openly admitted "we don't know" often:

"It’s incomprehensible to me that ACIP continues to have these quarterly meetings and unanimously votes to add more vaccines to an already overzealous schedule. For years I’ve been watching you all do this.

You must know that the public is watching you and scrutinizing data and reporting now more then ever. The director of the CDC admits being overconfident in her endorsement of the Covid vaccines. Fauci admits it. You can’t possibly believe your recommendations if you are doing your job thoroughly as a voting member on this committee. Anyone can look at skewed data and its bottom line. It takes a critical thinker to dive into it and I am confident that none of you have done your due diligence. People are dropping dead left and right daily.

Have you taken the entire adult schedule you have recommended? Have your kids and grandchildren taken the entire children’s schedule? I think not because you wouldn’t still be alive on this panel. Shame on all of you. The public has zero confidence in the CDC and ACIP. You have zero credibility.

I’ll do you a favor and save you some time. You can glance at the quick filtered data from VAERS on youth and myocarditis which I’ve attached. Look at this before you vote. If you can still vote for this vaccine to be added to the schedule after the review of this data from the government’s own reporting system… just know the world is watching. It’s between you and God. What is the best decision for our kids? To avoid this toxic vaccine at all cost. This a virus that has a 99% survival rate… until you get the vaccine. The public knows about the CARES Act language also. What you’re doing is simply wrong."

Wishing you the very best as you represent so many of the voiceless. May God guide you and protect you. Finally, let's get the C19 vaccines pulled. The world hasn't begun to understand the scope of damage it's caused. We all know if this was an OTC medication it would have been black boxed.

Sincerely,

Mrs. Diana Wingate-Gaiser

Title: Comment from Costa, Loreen
Comment: We have been living with the fallout from vaccine injury since 2019, when our son, then aged almost 10, was first damaged by the MMR vaccine.  After an unwanted flu shot during pregnancy triggered a 12+ hour pre-term labor event, my husband and I decided that the best course of action would be to delay all vaccination for our son until we better understood his neurology.  We now believe that it was a result of that decision - even as he was born one month before he was expected - we were gifted almost 10 years of healthy development and almost 8 years of school experiences to show us that our son was fully physically, intellectually and emotionally above average.  He read well above grade level and wrote argumentative papers by age 9; he was competent with stick sports by age 3; he was a kind, empathic classmate who assisted other children to learn their tech, and who had a clear sense of justice.  He only ever visited his doctor for well checks!  But all of this changed in April 2019 after his first MMR when he immediately showed neurological symptoms we only later understood as being related to neuro auto-immune issues and encephalopathy: he had skin crawling sensations the moments after injection, he had a rash on his trunk and cried for more than 12 hours on the same day; within the following days he lost his ability to hold a pencil and write; he lost coordination; he developed severe anxiety and OCD, ultimately being unable to tolerate changes to his space or to leave his room.  And while we were told that this was a result of us "creating an environment of anxiety by delaying vaccination" we were clear that these symptoms all occurred only after this first vaccination.  We will forever regret listening to the pediatrician and to the school which demanded -even after we clearly described and documented these symptoms - that we continue MMR series vaccination the following June or have our son be removed from his beloved school. It is notable that his symptoms deepened and expanded, to the point that we were seeking help from specialists by the summer.  It was only after many months of fear and confusion that we finally found neurologists willing to consider the truth of our story, and that we finally received a PANS diagnosis - for what this is worth!  We have been more or less on our own in the years since, educating ourselves about auto-immune and inflammatory processes, and working with functional medicine professionals to help us manage the ebb and flow of our son's neuro damage.  He has missed 2 different years of school.  We were told more than once that had our son suffered his injury as a pre-verbal baby, that we would have likely had heard a diagnosis of "sever autism" instead of the PANS info we've been working with.  Our lives have been turned upside down, and we have fears that our child will never attain the success and independence his intelligence and capabilities would have lead us to expect.  While we realize our blessings - that we had 10 amazing years with our boy, and that we still have wonderful days where he is present with us, we also recognize that had the true work of safety studies been done on the ingredients, the timing, even the necessity of all these childhood vaccines, our son might already be on his way to an intellectually stimulating college.  Instead, we wonder if he will ever be able to live on his own, never mind complete a degree.  In goes without saying: We feel that a thorough safety review of all aspects of the childhood vaccine schedule is long overdue.  Specifically, as we are aware that no vaccine on the childhood schedule has ever been studied against a true, inert placebo, we would expect to see the introduction of such studies ASAP, for any new injections as well as for anything already recommended.  And we would expect that a revocation of indemnification for the manufacturers should be passed, such that families like ours could hold manufacturers (and doctors and schools who demand the injections!) responsible for injury and lost life value; only in this way might we incentivize the industry to produce truly safe, truly effective vaccinations and the culture to recognize the absurdity of one-size-fits-all medical intervention.  It is difficult to speak of this after years and years of people telling us we were crazy, deluded, anti-scientific.  We can only hope that true scientific method will return to ACIP after years and years of industry captured analysis and authoritarian utilitarianism which has damaged generations of our children.

Title: Comment from Middaugh, Bethany
Comment:
To whom it may concern:
(Regarding Docket CDC-2026-0199 for new ICD medical code T50.B25)

Without sovereignty over our own bodies, what real freedom do we possess?

VAERS now contains over one million reports of adverse events directly related to the COVID vaccine, yet this reporting system is universally believed to only contain 1%-5% of raw data. This indicates the true adverse event and death count could be catastrophically higher.

The accurate statistics of death and injury behind the experimental shot forced upon humanity may never be known. I will express that statement again, in alternative wording. The true number of wrongful deaths, life-long injuries, turbo cancers, chronic health catastrophe, vaccine shedding, and life-altering physical symptoms from the COVID vaccine may not ever be known. We are now 5 years beyond the rollout of the COVID vaccine, yet we still lack true insight into the effects of it upon the human body. Will we ever truly know?

Here are a just a handful of tragedies from my own personal world, and I am but one person.

We had a family friend who died after receiving the COVID vaccine. A 35-year-old mother of two, very healthy and active nurse of whom was forced to take the COVID shot, in order to keep her job. She was found dead the following morning post-shot. Her entire body and organs shut down. Her family left stunned, children and husband left in utter grief.

Another neighbor and family friend, a 42-year-old respected psychologist, who was rushed to the ER after the 2nd booster shot with cardiac arrhythmias and abnormal lung behavior. He still suffers to this day from cardiac abnormality, reduced physical capability and lessened lung capacity. He is lucky to be alive.

Two former colleagues, both in their 40s, who suffered dangerous blood clots in their legs traveling up to their hearts after booster shots. Very thankful they went into visit their family physicians when physical symptoms began, as the outcome could have been devastating. Both individuals are still negatively impacted to this day, with less physical activity and energy, and living in daily pain.

Our grandmother was diagnosed with a rare liver and lung cancer within 3 months after receiving the COVID shot and boosters. She was gone within a year, and we miss her joyful laugh and bubbly energy every single day of our lives.

Our grandfather was diagnosed with a rare blood cancer; he is still with us yet living in assisted care and unable to walk. I have many other stories of injury and impact to others I personally know, as we all do.

Please consider every entry, every story, and every comment shared for the truth and tragedy that they are. This is not to be dismissed or ignored. Justice and accountability must be pursued, in honor of all of those we have lost, all of whom still suffer, and all of the loved ones left behind.

We will not ever, ever forget. We will not be ignored. And, we will not stay silent.

May God bless each of you today with wisdom and endurance to continue on in the pursuit of truth, justice and protection of the American people. Thank you for your time and commitment to these tasks.

Please consider the importance of recognizing and researching vaccine injury as well as an improved method for compensating the injured. Thank you.

Title: Comment from J, J
Comment:
I had two "hot lots" (according to VAERS data) of Moderna Covid 19 vaccine in March and April of 2021. I was constantly tired after the first shot, but after the second one I had the worst headache of my life and probably should have gone to the ER. This would be just the beginning of a horrendous decline in my health. Pain and headache devolved into severe POTS and heartbeat irregularities along with vein inflammation. CMV and EBV came up in testing as reactivated and I was a wreck. I finally got a cMRI in late summer after ongoing chest pain and a stress test revealed severe shortness of breath. The cMRI was positive for myopericarditis. The cardiologist said it was a smouldering case. I was treated but not recovered so more testing the following year led to cardiac attr wt amyloidosis diagnosis. It should be noted that I had no serious heart issues prior to the vaccine. I reported to Moderna but nothing was done. I don't believe that they reported to VAERS as I cannot find a case of myopericarditis that matches me. I am apparently invisible.

What I DID have prior to the vaccine was a multitude of conditions that were very STABLE due to a dx of CVID in 2019 that allowed SCIG treatment. I lost weight, felt great, and was living life before March of 2021. I now believe it was the SCIG that probably saved my life in 2021 from the adverse reaction to the vaccine.

I was advised by my medical team to get the Covid vaccine to protect my health and in the end, it literally destroyed all the health gains I had made and gifted me with new conditions and new severity for every condition I ever had. As time went on, severe pain in my achilles tendons and elsewhere led to testing for lyme/coinfection reactivation and it became clear that every infection I had once fought was active again. I began the long trudge of battling lyme/coinfections once again.

I became aware a few years ago of testing for covid vax igg and my doctor agreed to run it. We have been checking periodically since that time and it is ALWAYS greater than the upper limit of the test (>2500 all 4 times). We have no idea how high it actually is. My immune system is in constant hyperdrive. Fast forward to August last year and I began to have new and frightening neurological symptoms. I thought my time was short.

As things stand now my inflammatory markers are all high, I have developed a functional problem with absorbing iron into the cells and I have acidosis. My liver has turned "fatty" (NASH) and my lyme treatments take on a new level of dangerous so we are pulsing with many days off to protect me. I must continue to take Lyrica to subdue the neurological symptoms. I take a 250k drug for the cardiac amyloid in addition yo very expensive SCIG. I must take iron infusions for the functional deficiency. I must inject rocephin and take multiple lyme/Bartonella/Babesia antibiotics and other drugs. I don't know how long my body can take it all, yet life is precious and I fight on.

My CICP was filed in 2022 with hopes that it would get transferred to something of real help. It's over 4 years pending now and of course will be worthless even if granted. I need care outside the box of mainstream but have to work inside the system where insurance pays. With an inflammed brain and constant pain, I am expected to navigate this system and try to get some benefit out of it when many health care professionals are incentivized to look the other way. If not for my constant pushing and a functional doctor in the system, I don't know where I would be.

Please help the injured! Identify us, study us, and develop treatments for us. Teach mainstream doctors that complex injuries are real and show them that we are worth their time by compensating them appropriately for complex care. Get our injury cases in front of a fair court. I need real compensation so that I can get treatments like hyperbaric oxygen and EBOO that are not covered in the traditional system. Even much of my chronic lyme treatment is not covered in the system! You all promised to fix that!

People are slowly dying out here. Me included. We matter. We have families that love us and need us. Please help.

Title: Comment from Sorrenti , Danielle
Comment:
Former San Francisco firefighter and EMT. After more than two decades of service, she now lives in Colorado, far from family, coping with Long COVID and the lasting impacts of vaccine injury and forced retirement.
I’m a dual national, born and raised in the UK, and served over 20 years in fire suppression with the San Francisco Fire Department. I was a lieutenant, firefighter, and EMT—extremely fit, and a single mother who worked 24–48 hour shifts through the pandemic, often clocking 70+ hours a week.
San Francisco first responders were mandated to get vaccinated or face termination. While I’m not anti-vaccine, I had serious concerns about how little was known at the time. But being somewhat close to retirement, I couldn’t afford to lose my job.
Both doses, taken within three months in mid-2021, made me sick. Then in December 2021, I contracted COVID on duty—despite being fully vaccinated—and my life changed. I never recovered from that initial infection. I developed crushing fatigue, shortness of breath, cognitive issues, rashes, heart palpitations, and more. The symptoms persisted, and I was diagnosed with Long COVID by a workers’ compensation doctor.
Two months later, despite medical advice to postpone, I was told by the SFFD doctor that I had to get a third vaccine or risk losing my job. That dose cemented my decline. Four months into this hell, with no real support, I became suicidal. The stigma around Long COVID was brutal—some colleagues assumed I was faking it. Workers’ compensation doctors dismissed it as menopause, despite letters from my endocrinologist and gynecologist—who had treated me for over 10 years—stating I had always been in excellent health and had clearly declined since contracting COVID.
I was forced to attend two separate three-hour Zoom hearings with the San Francisco City Attorney. They combed through 30 years of my medical history and fixated on notes where I had once reported being “tired” and that I was menopausal—as if that explained it all. The truth is, I had successfully managed menopause for years while doing a physically demanding job. And let’s be honest—if I were a man, menopause wouldn’t have even been mentioned.
I was assessed by four different insurance doctors, each for several hours, and all reported extensive damage. Still, the system denied functional or integrative treatments, offered no modified duty, and ultimately forced me into early retirement. In over a year on disability, SFFD contacted me only once.
I had to drain my savings to move out of state—San Francisco was unlivable on disability pay. I relocated to Denver and now face over $30,000 in legal fees. I live with three heart conditions, post-exertional malaise, MCAS, cognitive impairment, constant fatigue, and persistent depression. I’ve lost my health, my career, my community, and my financial stability. Though I’ve been granted 80% disability, it’s done little to improve my situation. I’m still waiting for a Fire Commission Disability hearing—three years and eight months after I tested positive and had to stop working.
I receive no medical care at this point. I cannot work. I’m extremely isolated, far from my family in the UK, and don’t see how I’ll ever afford to return home. I’m not hopeful for my future. I used to love helping people. I had a career I was proud of, friendships, a social life, financial security, and a body that could carry me through anything. I am no longer that person. It’s hard to find joy when you’re grinding through every day.
Unless you’ve lived this, you can’t fully understand the fallout. Being mandated forces to choose your health and loose your job, or a vaccine you know nothing about and keep your job/fiscal security. This is no fair choice. Then relentless government assessments with no working medical help, layered on top of being really sick and not knowing what’s happening, just crushes your will to live. Medical gaslighting is so real. I feel abandoned by the city I served for over two decades.
I’m so very grateful React19 and wait for the goverment to be accountable to first responders and military who were forced to choose our careers or a poorly tested and lack of transparency vaccine. I’ve lost my health, career and fiscal security because of these mandates.

Title: Comment from Drews, Janet
Comment:
My name is Janet Drews and I am writing in response to Docket No. CDC-2026-0199.
My daughter was recently diagnosed with Covid vaccine injury after over 4 years of dealing with what I thought to be long-Covid symptoms.
Within weeks of her first COVID-19 vaccination in 2021 she began ER visits for shortness of breath, dizziness, chest pain, and heart palpitations. She was 27 years old when it started.

Since then and after going through countless visits to ER and Urgent Care with tests repeatedly showing normal, she was sent to specialists in every possible area within modern medicine – only to be inevitably referred, by 3 doctors, to the Mayo Clinic here in Minnesota, a world-leading academic Medical Center.
My daughter was denied treatment there (twice) despite 4+ years of medical records supporting her life altering symptoms.

My daughter managed to not lose her job but is now on a medical leave.
Four months ago on November 15th she suffered an acute episode of not being able to breathe at all and actually fainted, as if something snapped and her body just gave out.
There was a 911 call and back to ER with normal test results.

The following several weeks were horribly debilitating for us- my daughter actually felt like she was dying when barely able to breathe and too dizzy to walk.
I had to wheelchair her into the hospital and to more specialists, where again, they couldn't help her.
We felt lost and seriously afraid.

Title: Comment from Green, Jennifer
Comment: My name is Jennifer Green and I am a public health nurse who became aware of vaccine injuries as a nurse in 2019, primarily in children and young adults. This knowledge gave me a healthy dose of skepticism once the COVID vaccines were being produced. My job required me to learn the mechanism of action behind the two vaccines so I could better convince the public that they were safe and effective. I immediately had reservations behind mRNA technology - why would we encourage anyone to take a product that instructs the body to create untold amounts of spike protein, the protein that makes SARS-COV-2 a deadlier virus in general? Why would we want these untold amounts of a key (spike protein) to bind to the vast system of locks (ACE-2 receptors) in our body - throughout multiple organ systems and our vasculature? This told me all I needed to know...that we were in for a HORRIFYING ride ripe for MASSIVE harm. I work with brilliant people and I can say that I was soon in the minority in my line of work, much to my dismay. I couldn't even convince any of my own family members to stay unvaccinated. However I am not here to harp on the cognitive dissonance, that still continues to this day. I am here to report on the various injuries I bore direct witness to as a nurse. During the rollout, medical personnel around me complained of minor maladies: one with a golfball sized axillary lymph node; another with generalized fatigue for two weeks post their initial vaccination; a good nurse friend with shingles on her face that she attributed to stress; and another with myocarditis that the hospital she worked for said was unrelated to the vaccine. A few months later the vaccines became available to the general public. I can say that out of the few patients in my line of care that consented to vaccination that every single one of them experienced an extreme inflammatory response. One patient with Kaposi's sarcoma (cancer) was advised to take the vaccine after he initiated chemotherapy and he took it on the day of discharge, as he went inpatient to begin chemo being HIV infected as well. Less than 24 hours after being discharged he was readmitted to the hospital for over 2 weeks due to his initial mRNA vaccine causing the cancer lesions in his lungs to grow overnight that led to extreme shortness of breath requiring 20+ liters of oxygen to breathe. This same patient also went on to develop severe lymphedema in one leg that took several months to resolve. Another patient with rheumatoid arthritis in his lungs suffered a similar fate - he too wound up in the hospital for an extended period of time due to the inflammatory response exacerbating his autoimmune lung disease. A third patient developed an enlarged heart which led to a cardiology referral - her serial chest x-rays prior to vaccination and post vaccination clearly painted the picture for root cause, as her age and prior medical history provided no obvious contribution. My own grandfather experienced a myocardial infarction within 5 days post his first Moderna mRNA injection. He continued to receive boosters as recommended by his PCP and within two years developed atrial fibrillation. We believe this final condition took his life as he died suddenly less than a year of being diagnosed with A-fib. An autopsy was not performed due to his age, presumably died from cardiac arrest or pulmonary embolism. My aunt who received multiple mRNA injections developed a carotid aneurysm of unknown cause, in addition to severe anemia with no known cause identified. I don't know what's sadder because these are just the obvious injuries I witnessed and there are SO many undiagnosed vaccine injuries in the wake due to doctors not being trained to identify them for what they are. It is no exaggeration that we are currently experiencing a cancer epidemic with no concern for the mRNA vaccines contributing whatsoever. We need to be investigating these various cancers at the genomic level to determine the epigenetic influence these vaccines may or may not be contributing. We direly need to BELIEVE the brave doctors and nurses that are coming forward to share their experiences and case studies from the last 5 years. These injections do not belong in our bodies and they certainly do not deserve to remain on the market due to the multitude of injuries occurring even to this day. For what it's worth, I must emphatically say that I refused to participate in the covid vaccine roll out - I was offered overtime to boot. But you know why I refused? Because I knew I couldn't live with myself knowing I could potentially harm a person with an experimental vaccine that had ZERO liability for harm. And yet, here we are. Thank you for your time in hearing my story of moral injury as a nurse during the last 6 years. Happy pandemic anniversary to us all - may we learn from it and never make the same mistakes ever again.

Title: Comment from Boyce, Thomas
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Dahlstrom, Jan
Comment:
To the Members of the CDC Vaccine Advisory Committee,

Thank you for the opportunity to submit public comment.

I am writing as the mother of Shannon Safford, a Minnesota National Guard Staff Sergeant who proudly served our country. In May of 2021, while deployed to Kuwait, Shannon received the COVID-19 vaccine. At that time, she was in the best shape of her life—strong, disciplined, and committed to the military career she loved.

Today, my daughter is 100% disabled and has been medically discharged from the National Guard.

Shannon is also a single mother to a beautiful 9-year-old daughter who now watches her once vibrant and energetic mom struggle daily with severe and debilitating health challenges. The ripple effect of this injury does not stop with Shannon—it deeply affects the life and future of her child as well.

Our family spent nine long months caring for her while desperately searching for answers. There were many emergency room visits, 911 calls, and terrifying episodes where she truly believed she was dying. We watched a vibrant young woman—someone who had dedicated her life to serving her country—suddenly fighting simply to survive each day.

Those months were filled with fear, confusion, and heartbreak. And yet, even now, years later, we are still struggling to find real help. There are few answers, very limited recognition, and little financial support for treatments that could help relieve her chronic illness and debilitating pain and suffering.

The reality is that vaccine-injured individuals are suffering in silence across this country. Many feel abandoned by the very systems they trusted. The despair is real, and without meaningful recognition, treatment options, and support, some are losing hope entirely. Immediate attention and resources are urgently needed to prevent further tragedy, including suicide among those who feel they have nowhere to turn.

We are asking for leadership and courage from this panel.

First, acknowledge that vaccine injuries do occur and that those affected deserve to be seen, heard, and medically supported.

Second, urgently fund and prioritize research into post-vaccine injury conditions so physicians can develop real treatments and pathways to recovery.

Third, create meaningful support systems and financial assistance for those whose lives have been permanently altered.

Fourth, ensure accountability and transparency from pharmaceutical manufacturers so the public can have confidence in vaccine safety.

Finally, protect the fundamental right of informed consent and medical choice—especially for our military service members and for parents making decisions for their children. Our children deserve vaccines that are unquestionably safe, and parents deserve the ability to make informed choices about their care.

Public health must include compassion, honesty, and responsibility for those who were harmed while following guidance and orders. Ignoring these individuals only erodes trust and deepens the suffering of families like ours.

Our daughter Shannon served her country with honor. She followed orders. Now she and her daughter are living with the lifelong consequences.

We are asking our public health leaders to show the same level of honor by recognizing the injured, helping them heal, and ensuring that future generations receive vaccines that are both safe and transparent.

The injured cannot wait any longer.

Respectfully,

Jan Dahlstrom
Mother of Shannon Safford
Minnesota

Title: Comment from Patterson , Trinna
Comment:
Subject: Public Comment – Severe Neurological Injury Following COVID-19 Vaccination

To the Members of the Advisory Committee,

My name is Contrinna (Trinna) Patterson, and I am submitting this statement for the public comment record regarding COVID-19 vaccine safety.

I received the Janssen COVID-19 Vaccine on November 8, 2021. Prior to vaccination, I was a healthy, high-functioning professional with a successful career spanning decades as a pharmaceutical representative in the veterinary industry. I had no history of neurological illness, cognitive impairment, or functional disability.

Shortly after receiving the vaccine, I developed encephalitis, a severe inflammatory condition of the brain. This acute neurological event marked the beginning of a dramatic and life-altering decline in my health.

In the months that followed, I developed significant neurological symptoms, including profound cognitive decline, neurological dysfunction, and systemic complications that had never existed before. My condition progressed to the point that I could no longer perform the cognitive demands required in my profession.

Medical imaging later revealed significant white matter abnormalities in my brain, documented on multiple MRIs beginning in early 2022. These findings were unexpected and deeply concerning given my prior health history.

As my condition progressed, physicians ultimately documented that I have permanent cognitive impairment preventing me from returning to work. I was forced to leave the career I had spent decades building.

Because of the severity of my illness, I was physically and cognitively unable to file a claim with the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program within the required timeframe. However, I did contact **Janssen Pharmaceuticals to report the adverse reaction and seek acknowledgment of what had occurred.

Over the past several years, I have undergone extensive medical testing and treatment in an effort to recover neurological function. This has included advanced therapies such as hyperbaric oxygen therapy and ongoing neurological evaluation.

While I remain hopeful for continued improvement, the reality is that this injury has permanently altered the course of my life. It has taken my health, my independence, and the career I worked decades to build.

I share my experience not out of anger, but out of concern that individuals who experience serious adverse events must be acknowledged in discussions about vaccine safety. Public trust in vaccination programs depends on transparency, rigorous investigation, and compassion for those who experience rare but devastating outcomes.

My request to this committee is simple:

Please ensure that serious neurological adverse events following vaccination are thoroughly investigated, openly discussed, and incorporated into ongoing safety monitoring and research.

Patients like myself deserve recognition, continued medical investigation into these mechanisms, and support for recovery.

I supported medicine and vaccination for much of my professional career. I simply ask that the medical system now show the same commitment to those who were harmed.

Thank you for taking the time to hear the voices of those who have been profoundly affected.

Respectfully,

Contrinna (Trinna) Patterson
United States

Title: Comment from Billings, Lesley
Comment:
Case Summary
On August 11, 2021, I received the Janssen (Johnson & Johnson) COVID-19 vaccine, lot 207A21A.
Within minutes of vaccination I experienced muscle spasms in my left breast and throat, sudden numbness of
my tongue, severe shortness of breath, and oxygen saturation dropping into the 60% range.

Over the following months I experienced rapid unintentional weight loss (approximately 40 pounds), severe
muscle loss, and cardiac evaluation indicating Stage 1 heart failure. My medical chart reportedly lists an
allergic reaction to the COVID-19 vaccine.

On May 17, 2023, an ultrasound-guided biopsy confirmed malignant invasive mammary carcinoma in my left
breast, and on May 20, 2023, I was formally diagnosed with invasive ductal carcinoma. I later underwent a
double mastectomy on August 25, 2023.

I submit this testimony so my experience may contribute to continued review of serious adverse events and the importance of informed consent. Even when causation cannot be
definitively established, documenting unusual or serious medical events following vaccination can contribute to improved safety monitoring and understanding of adverse reactions.

Title: Comment from Gupta, Vikas
Comment:
As an infectious diseases medical professional, I urge ACIP to once again serve as a source of credible, evidence-based information to inform vaccine coverage. Current members lacking vaccine expertise and appointed without transparency should be dismissed, and new members with the necessary qualifications should be appointed per the ACIP charter and federal law. Until ACIP is trustworthy and scientifically sound, it should not meet.

ACIP must base recommendations on high-quality, objective, scientific data analyzed by CDC scientists and experts in infectious diseases and vaccinology. Recommendations made by ACIP impact my patients, their communities and health care nationwide, and should be grounded in evidence, not politics. 

In response to the IOM, in 2010 ACIP formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework for systematic evidence review and evaluation of benefits and harms. ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding structured evaluation of public health, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives and equity.

ACIP should adhere to the systematic, objective and transparent process of GRADE and the EtR framework for decision making during work group meetings and at its meetings. Any changes to the ACIP recommendation methodology should be released publicly and developed using input from ACIP liaisons, CDC subject matter experts and other stakeholders. ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework. Any changes to methodology should not compromise decision making based on scientific evidence and research conducted by experts in the field.

COVID-19 vaccine injuries and long COVID:
I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. While the Committee should consider vaccine risks and demonstrated side effects while deliberating on recommendations, vaccine injuries themselves do not directly fall within the mandates of ACIP. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from Moore, Zack
Comment:
The Federal Register notice says the agenda includes updates on ACIP workgroups and discussions on COVID-19 vaccine injuries and Long-COVID and ACIP recommendation methodology. ACIP should reaffirm that its recommendation methodology is grounded in a long-standing, formal evidence-based framework, not personal anecdotes or ad hoc judgement.

Between 2008 and 2011, the Institute of Medicine (now the National Academy of Medicine) issued reports calling for stronger, more transparent methods for developing clinical guidelines, including systematic evidence review and clear ratings of evidence quality and recommendation strength. In response, the ACIP created an Evidence-Based Recommendations Work Group and formally adopted the Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation (GRADE) framework in 2010 to ensure its vaccine recommendations are based on systematic evidence review and transparent evaluation of benefits and harms.

After the adoption of GRADE, ACIP recognized that recommendation decisions also require consideration of additional factors such as implementation and resource use. Following work by the ACIP Evidence-Based Recommendations Work Group, ACIP adopted the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) framework in 2018, adding a structured process to evaluate factors such as the public health problem, benefits and harms, costs, feasibility, stakeholder perspectives, and equity before making recommendations.

ACIP should preserve the core purpose of GRADE: ensuring that vaccine recommendation deliberations are based on a systematic assessment of the certainty or quality of evidence.

ACIP should also preserve the core purpose of EtR: ensuring that the committee explicitly addresses each decision domain before making a recommendation (benefits and harms, values, acceptability, feasibility, resource use, equity, and the public health problem).
These frameworks were adopted precisely to improve transparency, consistency, and communication.

ACIP should reject any methodology change that would permit recommendations to proceed without a documented GRADE evidence assessment and a completed EtR framework.
If ACIP is reconsidering its methodology, it should do so in a way that is fully documented in public materials, clearly identifies the workgroup or body proposing the change, and explains how the revised process will remain consistent with ACIP’s evidence-based standards.
