I encourage ACIP members to ensure they are remaining true to the purview of the Committee, and the specific responsibilities they have to vaccines and immunizations, as stated in the ACIP charter "[to] provide advice for the control of diseases for which a vaccine is licensed in the U.S." The proposed topic of long COVID falls outside of this purview, unless the Committee plans to discuss the clear and consistent evidence that the COVID-19 vaccine reduces the risk of long COVID. ACIP should fulfill its mandate in focusing on evidence-based vaccine review and recommendations and utilize existing safety systems to identify validated safety signals.

The Countermeasures Injury Compensation Program administered by the Health Resources and Services Administration is the best avenue for the small percentage of patients with severe adverse reactions to the COVID-19 vaccination. While these injuries should be considered as an aspect of recommendations, it must be in the context of the widespread benefit COVID-19 vaccines have provided in the United States and avoidance of severe disease and hospitalizations from COVID-19. Rigorous data have demonstrated the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines across age groups and populations. Casting doubt on their safety will only cause more confusion for patients and could depress vaccine uptake, leaving more people at risk of long COVID, hospitalization and death.

I object to changes being made to COVID vaccine recommendations or any other vaccine recommendations without proper consideration and evaluation by vaccine experts.

Thank you for your attention.

Title: Comment from National Association of Pediatric Nurse Practitioners
Organization: National Association of Pediatric Nurse Practitioners
Comment:
The National Association of Pediatric Nurse Practitioners (NAPNAP) supports all evidence-based efforts to have every infant, child and adolescent receive all age-appropriate immunizations on time. We are deeply concerned that the Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS) and its Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) continue to disable decades-long expert, systematic review for long-studied, widely accepted, evidence-based immunization practices. As a result, NAPNAP now endorses the American Academy of Pediatrics’ 2026 Child and Adolescent Immunization Schedule.

Unfettered misinformation and disinformation are contributing to declining vaccination rates in school-aged children, and we are now seeing the predictable, preventable consequences. Measles, which is highly contagious and can cause severe respiratory and neurological complications or death, was nearly eliminated in the U.S. after the vaccine became available in 1963, but sadly measles transmission has returned at alarming levels. As of March 5, 2026, CDC reports 1,281 confirmed measles cases in the United States in 2026 risking the loss of the United States’ measles elimination status by the World Health Organization.

We are also seeing the return of other vaccine-preventable diseases. Pertussis has resurged in recent seasons and remains especially dangerous for infants. CDC surveillance notes that U.S. pertussis cases rose substantially in 2024 and remained elevated thereafter compared with immediate pre-pandemic levels.
We must recognize that vaccine-preventable disease threats are not limited to measles and pertussis. Polio is rising again globally, through both wild poliovirus type 1 (WPV1) and circulating vaccine-derived poliovirus (cVDPV) in under-immunized communities. The Global Polio Eradication Initiative’s update as of March 4, 2026, reports ongoing poliovirus detections, a reminder that polio eradication remains unfinished and that low coverage anywhere poses risk everywhere. Now is not the time for ACIP to weaken support for life-saving vaccines for all eligible people.

In this environment, clear, consistent, and evidence-based recommendations are essential. Based on member feedback, NAPNAP strongly encourages ACIP to issue recommendations that promote vaccine uptake and reduce confusion for health care providers and families. Our members have expressed significant concerns with a “shared clinical decision-making” approach when it results in vaccines being perceived as optional or lower priority, particularly when families struggle to understand why certain immunizations are not positioned with the same clarity as routine recommendations.

We urge ACIP to continue to anchor recommendations in transparent, structured, evidence-based methods, including GRADE and the Evidence to Recommendation (EtR) frameworks. These tools exist to evaluate benefits and harms systematically, incorporate values and equity and communicate recommendations in a way clinicians can implement and families can trust.

This is especially important for COVID-19 vaccination. The evidence supports the safety and effectiveness of COVID-19 vaccines for all eligible individuals ages 6 months and older, with particular importance for:
• Pregnant people, who benefit from protection against severe disease and whose infants can benefit from passive antibody protection early in life; and
• Children with high-risk or chronic conditions, who face increased risk of serious outcomes from COVID-19 and therefore benefit strongly from vaccination.

To enable the most effective way to proactively protect infants, children, and adolescents from serious or potentially deadly diseases, NAPNAP urges CDC and ACIP to deliver clear, evidence-based guidance on vaccine safety and efficacy, and to communicate factually so the public understands the life-saving benefits of immunization.

Title: Comment from Keller, Andrea
Comment:
My name is Andrea Keller and I have a thirty year old son who was injured by his infant vaccines. At twelve months Sam received his MMR and VARIVAX vaccines at his “well-baby” check up. He had received all his recommended vaccines prior to this appointment. Being a good mother meant taking your baby to the pediatrician and following all their guidance. I was a good mother or so I thought. Actually, I was an uninformed compliant mother and I have lived with regret because after my son's twelve month well visit he began a downward spiral developmentally and health wise. He stopped answering to his name. Sought isolation. Stopped sleeping. Jumped constantly on furniture or in his crib. Covered his ears. Became a picky eater. Started stacking toys instead of playing with them. Stopped imitating. Stopped using the few words he had. In addition, he started getting sick all the time. Chronic ear infections, bowel problems, allergies, fevers, and would vomit for no reason. He would have days of complete lethargy where he would repeatedly throw up. He regressed and changed right before my eyes. It was hard to witness and no one had any explanations. We were told to get him evaluated for autism. He was diagnosed at 2 years and one month of age.
Fast forward to today he can't speak, suffers from full body apraxia (a brain body disconnect). Most concerningly he started having Tonic clonic seizures when he turned twenty-one.I never saw anyone have a seizure until I saw him have his first. Now, I've seen him have over one hundred of them. Sam has had a seizure in Wal-mart, in the car, at restaurants, in hotels, in swimming pools and he's even fallen down a flight of steps having one. Up to forty percent of those with autism develop a seizure disorder. He had a seizure in a friend's pool during covid and had to go to the hospital as he inhaled water. Luckily his covid test came back negative. He could have gone in as a near drowning victim and gotten caught up in the covid hospital situation. Plus, we may not have been able to be with him which is totally imperative because he can't advocate for himself.
Sam has suffered from PANDAS ( Pediatric Autoimmune Neuropsychiatric Disorders Associated with Streptococcal Infection) which was completely debilitating. He's had H pylori that caused him to lose twenty-two pounds in five weeks and be hospitalized for eleven days and sent home on an IV. He was already extremely thin at that time. He's started having some mild heart issues at this time. He's suffered from SIBO that caused much distress with eating. His entire life he has been plagued with slow gut motility. Just last month this created great stress and an increase in aggression and self-injury. To witness your child slam their head over and over on the counter top never gets easier. He has a permanent scar on his forehead for he has been doing this for decades. What lasting harm is he causing?
According to Toby Rogers, Ph.D 115,000 children are slipping into autism every year in the United States. If forty percent of these are regressing into autism and are profoundly affected like my son what is going to happen to them when we parents die? As my son's primary caregiver my life has been devoted to him and his needs. I gave up my career in education to care for him and all his needs. His care between special therapies and biomedical treatments to address his numerous health issues cost a fortune. ABA (Applied behavior analysis) the recommended treatment for autism cost us more the first year we did it then my husband made that year. We ran up credit cards and borrowed money to help pay for it. The outcome was poor. Turns out apraxic children don't do well with treatments that rely on imitation. What causes apraxia? Could it be the neuro-toxins in vaccines like thimerosal and aluminum? If you can't control your body you're not a very good imitator. When my son was twenty-five we stumbled upon a program called Spelling to Communicate (S2C). What a life-changing program. And we just stumbled upon it thanks to a book written by a father and nonspeaking son- JB and Jamison Handley- UNDERESTIMATED AN AUTISM MIRACLE. If only we knew of this program when my son was younger his life, our lives could have been so different. If only something didn't cause him to regress in the first place his life would have been so different. His life stolen from him as an infant. His ability to communicate independently and his health ruined. I am but one mom telling you my son's story but I can assure you there are hundreds of thousands of mothers with very similar stories. We know our children were not born this way. They were injured. THIS NEEDS TO STOP. Please do whatever you can to make that happen. Thank you for your time and attention to my son Sam's story.

Sincerely,
Andrea Keller

Title: Comment from McHenry, Patricia
Comment:
“Hello, my name is Patricia McHenry and I appreciate the opportunity to provide public comments to the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices. My daughter Melissa had just finished her freshman year at college and on May 9, 2016 we had just arrived back home from packing her up when she collapsed on my back deck and passed suddenly. Try as I might I was with her and did my best to revive her. I am devastated to say the least. What happened to this beautiful vibrant girl of 18 years old. She was diagnosed at an early age with classic MVP (mitral valve prolapse). Every year I took her to St. Francis Hospital in Roslyn, NY to have it checked before school would start and it was always fine. She was cleared to swim competitively for 12 years and never had any issues as well as worked as a life guard. Just a few weeks after she received her mandatory MMR booster to attend her college freshman year the heart doctor said her her MVP had gotten worse but they would watch it and follow up in 6 months when she came home for Christmas break which we did and were told to it was the same and follow up when school finished. Needless to say when we returned home in May she suddenly passed. I could not imagine what could have caused this but the autopsy said her MVP was extremely enlarged. When Covid vaccines were introduced I saw that many young children developed heart problems and many suddenly passed. I can’t help that a cookie cutter approach could also have been what caused my daughter’s sudden passing she was forced to receive this MMR vaccine even when she had a heart issue. I miss her so much. It will be 10 years that she suddenly left us and it has been a long rode for me and my family. Also noting we were pressured for her to receive the HPV vaccines a few years before being told without it she could develop Cancer. Really!?! Is that an actual fact? Well my daughter also developed Bells Palsy which at 16 was quite traumatic for her and then to lose this beautiful young girl suddenly has been alot to bear. Were these vaccines so necessary?! The guilt and PTSD has been alot to endure. Never was I told of the side effects when I brought her for follow ups, let alone that they can kill or maim. She’s with me always and I know in my heart she wants me to speak out to prevent any family from going through what we have.

CLOSE:
Thank you for the opportunity to share my experience with immoral and unconstitutional vaccine mandates. Sincerely, Patricia McHenry

Title: Comment from Hudson, Heather
Comment:
A Mother’s Account: Fighting for My Son
On Sunday, March 1, 2026, my 26 year old son suffered his fifth stroke.  I am writing this from his hospital bedside while doctors try to stabilize him. During this hospitalization they discovered a blood clot in his heart along with noninfectious endocarditis inflammatory heart disorder. Their hope is to eventually perform open-heart surgery to remove the clot, but right now he is too sick to survive the operation. His autoimmune blood-clotting disorder continues to form clots even if he is fully anticoagulated.
This most recent stroke has been devastating. It damaged a large portion of the left side of his brain. His left arm is now immobile, and he has been mostly non-verbal since the stroke. My son, who once spoke easily and loved life, now has to relearn how to speak.
Even in the hospital this week, despite being on blood thinners, he has developed more clots: one in each arm and deep vein thromboses in both legs at the same time. Doctors consider his condition terminal.
Watching this happen to your child is something no mother should ever have to experience.
Before 2021, my son was healthy, strong, and full of promise. When he was 21, he was an avid runner. He ran twenty-five miles a week at the gym and loved running along the seashore. He was not under a doctor’s care. He had been tested for autoimmune diseases twice before the pandemic and had none.
He had a bright future ahead of him.
After receiving a COVID-19 vaccine in late July 2021, everything changed.
In the following months, my son developed a severe autoimmune blood-clotting disorder called antiphospholipid syndrome (APS). He also survived catastrophic antiphospholipid syndrome (CAPS), the most severe and life-threatening form of the disease. From that point forward, his body began attacking itself.
He was hospitalized repeatedly with life-threatening complications. He suffered a severe pulmonary embolism, developed multiple blood clots in both lungs, and a deep vein thrombosis in his right leg. Imaging later showed he had also suffered a stroke. His heart became enlarged and his valves began leaking.
After those early strokes, my once-active son was left with profound weakness on his right side and could no longer walk on his own.
His autoimmune problems continued to multiply. He was diagnosed with Raynaud’s syndrome, Sjögren’s syndrome, and a hemorrhagic lung disorder. Neurological conditions appeared as well, including postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome (POTS) and chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP). Most of these conditions developed rapidly within days or months after the vaccine.
In 2022 he returned to college and was a 4.0 student. I watched him push through illness that would stop most people. Sadly, his disease kept progressing.
From October 2023 to March 2024, He was hospitalized again with deep vein thromboses occurring simultaneously in all four limbs, suffered three more strokes, developed a cerebral venous blood clot and severe narrowing of the veins in his brain.
From the beginning of this illness, his mobility was severely limited. On a good day he walked about fifty feet with a cane before needing to stop or use a wheelchair.
For more than four years, I have fought for my son.
We have gone from hospital to hospital searching for help. But again and again we were turned away or dismissed. My son was never placed on immune-modulating therapies or monoclonal antibody treatments that might address the autoimmune nature of his disease.
Because this vaccine injury is not recognized, hospitals often do not follow up properly. The medical system keeps asking for proof. Without recognition there is no pathway to gather it.
For years doctors have known he has antiphospholipid syndrome. Yet life saving treatment protocols never materialized. Insurance barriers blocked care. Disability systems blocked access. In many cases doctors simply did not try to pursue treatment at all.
The long struggle to get someone, anyone, to help has now led to this catastrophic moment.
My son’s fifth stroke has taken a large part of the left side of his brain. His arm is paralyzed. He is fighting to speak again. And the blood-clotting disorder that caused it was never properly addressed.
As a mother, there is nothing more painful than watching your child suffer and feeling powerless to help them. I would do anything to save my son. I have spent years fighting for answers, begging doctors to look deeper, asking systems to recognize what is happening to him.
No mother should have to watch her child’s life slip away like this while the system argues about whether the problem even exists.
That is why I am asking the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) to recognize severe COVID-19 vaccine injuries in young people like my son.
The first step to solving a problem is acknowledging that it exists.
You cannot find what you are not looking for.
My son needs help.
